item.title: Zeteo Film Wins BAFTA! Plus, Watch Our Explosive Investigative Doc, 'Who Killed Shireen?'
item.description: Many of you know by now that Zeteo isnвҖҷt afraid to take risks. Below are two documentaries we supported or produced вҖ“ with your help! вҖ“ that have been critically acclaimed and have made news around the world. Last year, we acquired and released вҖҳ Gaza: Doctors Under AttackвҖҷ вҖ“ the powerful filmвҖҰ
item.author: Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
Many of you know by now that Zeteo isnвҖҷt afraid to take risks. Below are two documentaries we supported or produced вҖ“ with your help! вҖ“ that have been critically acclaimed and have made news around the world.

Last year, we acquired and released вҖҳ Gaza: Doctors Under AttackвҖҷ вҖ“ the powerful film about healthcare workers in Gaza that the BBC shelved and was too scared to air due to вҖңimpartiality concerns.вҖқ

On Sunday night, about a year later, the film took home a prestigious BAFTA TV Award. British journalist Ramita Navai accepted the award alongside Ben De Pear, executive producer at Basement Films вҖ“ the brilliant team who produced the documentary (and also this original Zeteo one on rising extremism within Israel).

Navai delivered a powerful message on stage: вҖңWe refuse to be silenced and censored.вҖқ De Pear also directly addressed the BBC, asking, вҖңGiven you dropped our film, will you drop us from the BAFTAs screening later tonight?вҖқ

Zeteo is proud to have made history by supporting the film and holding power to account when the BBC was too scared to.

Next on our must-watch list is our original 40-minute investigative documentary вҖҳ Who Killed Shireen?вҖҷ

Today sadly marks four years since Palestinian-American journalist Shireen Abu Akleh was killed by Israeli forces in the Occupied West Bank. The US government never fully investigated who was behind her murder, and there was no accountability for the Israeli soldier who shot her. The world didnвҖҷt even know who he was. Until our film.

In our fearless pursuit of justice and the truth, Zeteo did what the US government refused to do and sent a team of journalists to investigate her killing. Along the way, the team uncovered shocking new details from high-ranking officials about the Biden administrationвҖҷs investigation of the shooting and the overall US-Israeli relationship.

From the New York Times and

These films could not have happened without the support of our subscribers.

Please know that your financial contributions, small or large, are going toward accountability journalism that other outlets are still afraid to take on. If youвҖҷre a free subscriber, consider upgrading your subscription today to support more work. And if youвҖҷre interested in supporting us in a big way to help us fund projects like these films, consider becoming a Founding Member. Of course, you can also always donate to us here.

Catch up on some of ZeteoвҖҷs latest stories:

Thank you for being a paid subscriber to Zeteo, your support helps keep this media movement alive. If youвҖҷd like to wear your support, donвҖҷt forget to check out

For any issues with your subscription, please email us at info@zeteonews.com.

item.title: Top Susan Collins Donor Rakes in Millions From Maine Workers
item.description: The largest individual donor to Republican Senator Susan CollinsвҖҷs super PAC leads a firm thatвҖҷs raked in tens of millions of dollars in fees from MaineвҖҷs retirement system. As his firm profits off MaineвҖҷs public workers, private equity billionaire Stephen Schwarzman has, in turn, plowed millionsвҖҰ
item.author: "Zeteo | U.S. Politics"
item.content:encoded:
The largest individual donor to Republican Senator Susan CollinsвҖҷs super PAC leads a firm thatвҖҷs raked in tens of millions of dollars in fees from MaineвҖҷs retirement system.

As his firm profits off MaineвҖҷs public workers, private equity billionaire Stephen Schwarzman has, in turn, plowed millions into efforts to re-elect Collins and keep the Senate under Republican control.

Last year, Schwarzman donated $2 million to Pine Tree Results, the primary super PAC backing CollinsвҖҷs reelection campaign. HeвҖҷs also given $5 million this election cycle to the Senate Leadership Fund, a super PAC affiliated with GOP Senate leaders thatвҖҷs planning to spend $42 million to boost Collins in Maine.

Schwarzman is the CEO of the Blackstone Group, which managed roughly $480 million for MaineвҖҷs retirement system last year, state records show.

Over the past 15 years, Blackstone has scored roughly $49 million in fees and expenses off the state of MaineвҖҷs investments, according to an estimate by Zeteo. This is an exceedingly conservative estimate, as it does not include the fees that alternative-investment firms like Blackstone typically charge on fundsвҖҷ profits.

To estimate this figure, Zeteo reviewed annual reports from Maine detailing the stateвҖҷs investment management expenses, as well as past news stories and records from other states detailing the firmвҖҷs fee schedules.

Starting in 2021, the Maine Public Employees Retirement System stopped disclosing, as part of its annual reports, the investment expenses itвҖҷs paid to specific funds each year. The stateвҖҷs retirement system rejected ZeteoвҖҷs public records request asking it to detail the total annual fees and investment expenses itвҖҷs paid to Blackstone.

The system said this information is вҖңnon-public because it consists of вҖҳtrade secrets or commercial or financial information that relates to actual or potential private market investments of the retirement system.вҖҷвҖқ

Schwarzman, who made $1.2 billion in dividends and total compensation last year, reportedly owns about 20% of Blackstone.

Reached for comment, a Collins campaign spokesperson referred Zeteo to Pine Tree Results PAC, which did not respond to our outreach. Blackstone did not respond to ZeteoвҖҷs request for comment.

Over the past few decades, state and local retirement systems have poured vast sums into high-risk, high-fee alternative investments, such as private equity and real estate funds. These investments are exceptionally lucrative for asset managers, and come at a steep cost for pension funds.

The Maine Public Employees Retirement System disclosed paying $128 million in investment expenses on its alternative asset classes last year, compared to roughly $1.2 million on its traditional public market investments.

As Collins seeks a sixth Senate term in Maine, sheвҖҷs relying on significant support from Pine Tree Results PAC, which recently launched attack ads targeting oysterman and veteran Graham Platner, the presumptive Democratic nominee.

Pine Tree Results has received big checks from some billionaire private equity executives whose firms have gutted MaineвҖҷs industry and workforce.

In SchwarzmanвҖҷs case, his firm, Blackstone, has made a fortune off MaineвҖҷs pension system, which manages the retirement savings of teachers, legislators, judges, and state, county, and municipal employees.

MaineвҖҷs investments with Blackstone have undergirded one of its most controversial areas of business.

Blackstone is widely criticized as helping exacerbate the housing crisis in the US, through its investments in both single-family rental homes and apartment communities around the country.

At one point, MaineвҖҷs retirement system had $76 million invested in Blackstone Real Estate Partners VII. According to corporate records, this fund previously controlled Invitation Homes, the Blackstone subsidiary that bought up foreclosed single-family homes in the wake of the 2008 financial crisis and formed the largest single-family-rental company in the US.

Blackstone exited Invitation Homes in 2019, before diving back into the single-family rental market in earnest by 2021.

Schwarzman previously gave $2 million to a different pro-Collins super PAC during her successful 2020 reelection campaign.

Collins cast a key vote for Donald TrumpвҖҷs 2017 tax law, which slashed taxes for the wealthy and corporations, and offered major benefits for private equity firms like Blackstone.

The Republican senator also provided a key vote to advance TrumpвҖҷs 2025 tax bill, which again slashed taxes for the wealthy and corporations вҖ“ while paying for the tax cuts, in part, by gutting healthcare and food assistance for the poor.

The legislation, known as TrumpвҖҷs вҖңBig Beautiful Bill,вҖқ included a suite of lucrative perks for the ultra-wealthy and private equity firms. Schwarzman donated $2 million to the Collins super PAC a day before she cast an important procedural vote for the 2025 tax bill, though she ultimately voted against the final legislation when it came to the Senate floor.

Both Trump tax laws have preserved the so-called вҖңcarried interestвҖқ loophole, which allows private equity managers to categorize their earnings as capital gains rather than regular income, and thus pay a lower tax rate than most Americans.

Democrats have long pledged to eliminate the loophole, including during Barack ObamaвҖҷs presidency. In 2010, Schwarzman infamously slammed DemocratsвҖҷ proposed tax changes as being вҖңlike when Hitler invaded Poland in 1939.вҖқ

In 2017, Collins put forward an amendment to close the carried interest loophole, but quickly backed away from it.

item.title: рҹӨ® Trump the Golden Calf
item.description: On this day in 1997, chess grandmaster Garry Kasparov conceded defeat by Deep Blue, an IBM supercomputer. Having lost 2-1 with three draws, Kasparov said simply, вҖңI lost my fighting spirit.вҖқ Nowadays, college professors flooded with essays written by ChatGPT might know how he felt. Good morning,вҖҰ
item.author: First Draft | Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
On this day in 1997, chess grandmaster Garry Kasparov conceded defeat by Deep Blue, an IBM supercomputer. Having lost 2-1 with three draws, Kasparov said simply, вҖңI lost my fighting spirit.вҖқ Nowadays, college professors flooded with essays written by ChatGPT might know how he felt.

Good morning, friends. Prem here. Wishing you a wonderful Monday and new week, wherever you are, and a happy belated MotherвҖҷs Day to all you magnificent moms across the world, doing your best to help us be our best. Thank you. рҹ’ң

In todayвҖҷs вҖҳFirst Draft,вҖҷ a grotesque golden statue of Donald Trump is unveiled at one of his lavish resorts; an ex-Biden State Department official collects tens of thousands of dollars from lobbyists and figures including Antony Blinken and Jake Sullivan as he tries to beat a populist for a US House seat in Nebraska; and debate flares regarding Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, MAGA exile Marjorie Taylor Greene, and their views on Israel and Palestine.

Late last week, a giant golden statue of a sadistic, narcissistic, psychopathic antagonist was unveiled вҖ“ blasphemously, some said.

Oh, and we were also introduced to a gold statue of Homelander, a power-hungry, insecure villain from the Amazon superhero show вҖҳThe Boys.вҖҷ

In Florida on Thursday, a group of evangelical and Jewish clergy, led by South Carolina congressional candidate and presidential вҖңspiritual adviserвҖқ Pastor Mark Burns, gathered to bless a 22-foot-tall, gold-colored statue of Donald Trump.

The statue, dubbed вҖңDon ColossusвҖқ and actually bronze covered in gold leaf, stands proudly at the Trump National Doral golf club resort, depicting Trump raising his fist, as he did after surviving an assassination attempt in Butler, Pennsylvania, in 2024.

Trump addressed the launch event, and his cult followers, by phone. Thanking attendees for their support, particularly Pastor Burns, the president inevitably rambled off-topic.

After talking golf, because of the venue, Trump said: вҖңIran is not doing good because of us. They are not doing well at all. And good things are happening.вҖқ

He was speaking right after he was forced to pause his slapdash effort to escort ships through the Strait of Hormuz, the crucial waterway closed because of his war, because key allies had not given permission.

In any case, it didnвҖҷt take much for many people to raise an eyebrow at the obsequious display, at the spectacle of religious leaders gathered beneath a garish statue. Did someone say idolatry? Does that stuff even matter anymore?

Of course, as BetteridgeвҖҷs Law of Headlines instructs us, any such question can be answered with a resounding вҖңNo.вҖқ And yet itвҖҷs still worth considering how far some religious leaders have gone in functionally deferring to Trump as God incarnate.

The story of the golden calf comes from the Book of Exodus. It goes like this: Moses was on Mount Sinai for 40 days, receiving the Ten Commandments. The Israelites became anxious that he wouldnвҖҷt return, so they asked his brother, Aaron, to create a вҖңGodвҖқ to lead them. Aaron fashioned a statue of a golden calf, to which the people offered sacrifices. This made God angry. Moses convinced God not to destroy the people, but returned and destroyed the idol, while thousands were killed. Finally, God sent a plague among the people. The story warns of the perils of idolatry and impatience.

How do TrumpвҖҷs religious acolytes square this particular circle? Look no further than Pastor Burns, who on Sunday answered criticism of his actions in front of the golden statue of Trump by saying: вҖңLet me be clear: this is not a вҖҳgolden calf.вҖҷвҖқ

Arguing that вҖңthereвҖҷs a major difference between worship and honor,вҖқ Burns cited other figures with statues erected to them.

вҖңPresident Trump is not dedicating this statue to himself,вҖқ Burns said. вҖңThis was a moment made possible by more than 6,000 patriots who came together to honor what they believe this movement represents: strength, resilience, courage, and leadership that helped shape the future of the United States of America.

вҖңAs Christians, we worship God alone. But Scripture also teaches us to give honor where honor is due.вҖқ

Of course, one reason the claim that such a display is not blasphemous rings hollow вҖ“ or indeed the claim that itвҖҷs not as if Trump dedicated the statue to himself вҖ“ is that Trump posted a photo depicting himself as Jesus, just weeks ago. And has got into repeated fights with the pope.

ThereвҖҷs also the fact that the Doral statue was paid for by a bunch of cryptocurrency bros who got into a financial dispute with the sculptor, who said he worked to instructions including that the statue be slimmer than Trump actually is, and that it not have what the sculptor called the aging presidentвҖҷs вҖңturkey neck.вҖқ

But then, why bother counting such absurdities? We should remember that the hypocrisy of the religious right has been exposed again and again. The world knows itвҖҷs less concerned with faith in a higher power dedicated to universalist ideals than with faith in whatever enables it to wield the highest power over all. And the unholy essence of religious right support for the Trump administration is the ultimate case in point.

No higher power of mine, or of any normal person, blesses a sexual abuser, a proud racist, a bloodthirsty warmonger, whose foremost accomplishments include bombing schoolchildren and covering up files related to one of the most infamous pedophiles ever to walk the earth.

If a вҖңpastorвҖқ can excuse all that, and more, what difference can a gold-colored statue possibly make?

  • Iran responds:Tehran answered a US proposal to end the war. State mediasaidconditions included an end to the naval blockade and deferred action on its nuclear ambitions. Qatar, UAE, and Kuwait reported Iranian drone attacks.
  • More war:TrumpcalledIranвҖҷs response вҖңtotally unacceptable.вҖқ Speaking toSinclair, he said the US вҖңmay continue military action against Iran for another two weeks,вҖқ adding: вҖңWe have specific objectives we aimed to achieveвҖҰ and itвҖҷs possible we have already accomplished about 70% of them.вҖқ
  • Ground war:Benjamin Netanyahu seemed to tell CBS that Trump wants boots on the ground to tackle issues, including IranвҖҷs nuclear ambitions. вҖңWhat President Trump has said to me, вҖҳI want to go in there,вҖҷвҖқ the Israeli prime ministersaid. вҖңAnd I think it can be done physicallyвҖҰ you go in, and you take it out, why not? ThatвҖҷs the best way.вҖқ
  • Anti-social:Netanyahu also hinted at wider efforts,saying: вҖңI think thereвҖҷs been a concerted effort by several states to basically vilify IsraelвҖҰ primarily in the social media... WeвҖҷve not fought back yet, so weвҖҷll have to do that.вҖқ
  • Lebanon carnage:The Lebanese Health MinistrysaidIsrael killed 51 people in 24 hours, amid a supposed вҖңceasefire.вҖқ Israel вҖңcontinues to violate international laws and humanitarian normsвҖқ by targeting medical assets and workers, the ministry said. Thedeath tollsince the latest escalation began is now at least 2,795 people, including more than 170 children.

On Tuesday, a Democratic primary in Nebraska will pit a populist long-time resident of the state against a two-decade State Department official who moved back just months ago, and who in the Biden administration was part of the diplomatic chain that funneled aid to Israel amid its genocide in Gaza.

In NebraskaвҖҷs first congressional district, career State Department official Chris Backemeyer is taking on Eric Moyer, a renewable energy developer. The seat is held by Republican Mike Flood, who won handily in 2024. But in that yearвҖҷs Senate contest, independent populist Dan Osborn actually won the district against incumbent Republican Deb Fischer.

Backemeyer served in a number of State Department roles over 20 years, as well as briefly in the White House and as an adviser to Vice President Kamala Harris.

Most recently, he was a senior official in the Bureau of Near Eastern Affairs, which is consulted in reviewing weapons transfers to nations in its region. Backemeyer took вҖңresponsibility on behalf of the bureauвҖқ on issues including political-military affairs, foreign assistance, and human rights. Responsibilities also included implementing assistance policy in accordance with US policy, managing foreign assistance funds, and overseeing several divisions, including one focused on Israel and Palestine.

Sources said this purview would have included consulting on matters related to aid to Israel amid its genocide in Gaza.

Several people resigned from diplomatic and military roles under the Biden-Harris administration. Backemeyer left after Donald Trump returned to power, taking a buyout from Elon MuskвҖҷs so-called вҖңDepartment of Government Efficiency,вҖқ saying he felt there was вҖңno mission or purpose leftвҖқ in the department, after being directed to help fire other staffers.

Since then, Backemeyer has maintained a stance virtually indistinguishable from a Biden-Harris State Department spokesperson. Asked in January if he supports continuing to fund Israel, Backemeyer delivered a near-400-word response without answering the question, instead saying the situation was вҖңreally complicatedвҖқ and echoing genocide-justifying claims that Hamas used Palestinians as human shields.

On the same podcast three months earlier, Moyer responded simply, saying he supports conditioning aid to Israel, calling it вҖңunconscionableвҖқ that the matter was even up for debate, given IsraelвҖҷs indiscriminate killing of women and children.

Including a $50,000 loan to himself, Backemeyer has raked in more than $350,000 вҖ“ seven times more than Moyer. A substantial amount has come from outside Nebraska (primarily the DMV), including from ex-Secretary of State Antony Blinken and ex-National Security Adviser Jake Sullivan, corporate lobbyists, and ex-Gulf nation ambassadors.

  • AOC vs. MTG:Passions flared after Alexandria Ocasio-Cortezsaidshe wasnвҖҷt buying far-right Trump apostate Marjorie Taylor GreeneвҖҷs views on IsraelвҖҷs war in Gaza, which MTG hascalleda genocide. AOC said: вҖңI personally do not trustвҖҰ a proven bigot and antisemite onвҖҰ what is good for Gazans and Israelis.вҖқ
  • ADL mourns:Abe Foxman, a Holocaust survivor who led the Anti-Defamation League for 28 years until 2015,diedaged 86. A staunch voice for Israel, he opposed the Islamic community center, the so-called вҖңGround ZeroвҖқ mosque, near the World Trade Center site in Manhattan, and called for the firing of вҖҳBeyond IsraelismвҖҷ host Simone Zimmerman when she worked for Bernie Sanders.
  • DOJ disarray:Fallout from TrumpвҖҷs push to prosecute former FBI Director James Comey has rocked the Department of Justice, damaging vital cases, including one concerning an attack in Afghanistan, theWashington Postreported.
  • Midterms threat:Amid growing fears of GOP election interference, TrumpsaidRepublicans would have an вҖңElection Integrity ArmyвҖқ in each state this year.
  • вҖҳSmaller shotsвҖҷ:Speaking to Sinclair about vaccines, Trumpblurted: вҖңI look at these beautiful little babies, and they get a vat, I mean, like a big glass of stuff pumped into their bodiesвҖҰ I would love to see much smaller shots, like four visits to the doctor. And I think you would have a much better result with the autism.вҖқ
  • Tapes fight:Former President Joe Biden willfighta DOJ attempt to release 70 hours of audio made in 2017 as Biden wrote a memoir. The tapes were provided to the special counsel who investigated BidenвҖҷs retention of classified material.

Commissioned by Congress in 1832 and unveiled in 1840, Horatio GreenoughвҖҷs statue of George Washington, the first such official portrayal of a US president, proved extremely controversial. Why?

Keep your eyes out for the answer below!

  • рҹҮөрҹҮё DonвҖҷt forget Gaza:Israeli strikes killed at least three people, including the head of the criminal police force in Khan Younis, вҖӢWessam Abdel-Hadi, the Gaza Health Ministrysaid. More than 850 Palestinians have been killed since the supposed October ceasefire.
  • рҹҮ®рҹҮ¶ Secret base:Israel built and defended a special forces outpost in Iraq, theWall Street Journalreported. Iraqi troops close to finding it were hit with an airstrike.

  • рҹҮ®рҹҮұ

    Friendly fire:Benjamin Netanyahu emerged unscathed from hisinterviewwith Major Garrett of CBS, the news outlet run by self-confessed вҖңZionist fanaticвҖң Bari Weiss. Barely questioned about Gaza or attacks on civilians in Iran, Netanyahu even claimed, without pushback, that Iranians called him вҖңBeloved Bibi.вҖқ

  • рҹҮөрҹҮұ

    Pole apart:Ex-Justice Minister Zbigniew Ziobro, wanted in his country of Poland for alleged misuse of funds and use of spyware against opponents, and exiled in Hungary until the defeat of Viktor OrbГЎn, isin the US. Trump personally approved his visa.

  • рҹҮӘрҹҮё

    Virus latest:Passengers from the cruise ship stricken by hantavirusbeganto fly home from Tenerife. Three people have died, with five more known infections.


The harrowing documentary film вҖҳGaza: Doctors Under Attack,вҖҷ from Basement Films, won a British Academy Television Award, or BAFTA, in the current affairs category. Referring to IsraelвҖҷs bombing of вҖңevery single one ofвҖқ GazaвҖҷs hospitals, reporter Ramita Navai referred to вҖңthe findings of our investigation that the BBC paid for but refused to show,вҖқ adding: вҖңWe refuse to be silenced and censored.вҖқ Zeteo was proud to acquire the international rights to this award-winning film that the BBC tried to block. You can watch it here.

  • As the average US gas price reached $4.52, Energy Secretary

    Chris Wrightrefused to repeat his previous predictionto NBCвҖҷs вҖҳMeet the PressвҖҷthat it would be under $3 a gallon by summer. вҖңI canвҖҷt predict the price of energy in the short-term or even the medium-term,вҖқ Wright told the same show, despite evidently having done so before.

  • Trump will enter this weekвҖҷs summit with Xi Jinping of China вҖңterribly weakened,вҖқ a top Democrat said.

    Senator Jack ReedtoldFox News Sunday: вҖңWeвҖҷre seeingвҖҰ significant gas prices, significant increases in grocery prices and in all sorts of prices for the American home. And the American people are significantly concerned about this conflict with Iran. So thatвҖҷs not a strong position to be in when youвҖҷre talking to an economic and a geopolitical rival.вҖқ

  • South Carolina congressman and former Democratic House leadership member

    James Clyburnpredicted racist GOP gerrymandering enabled by the Supreme Court might yet backfire. вҖңBe very careful what you pray for,вҖқ the 85-year-oldtoldCNNвҖҷs State of the Union, вҖңbecause what I do believe is that when they finish with the redistricting, there will be the possibilities of at least three Democrats getting elected here in South Carolina to the United States Congress.вҖқ
  • Monday, May 11:The suspect in the White House CorrespondentsвҖҷ Dinner assassination attempt isscheduledto appear in federal court.
  • Tuesday, May 12:Nebraska and West Virginiawillhold US House primaries.
  • Wednesday, May 13:The Sixth Circuit Court of Appeals issetto weigh a Department of Justice bid for Michigan voter roll data.
  • Thursday, May 14:Trump isscheduledto visit China for a two-day summit with Xi Jinping to discuss trade and the US-Iran War.
  • Friday, May 15:Palestinians willmark Nakba Day, to remember the 1948 forced displacement of more than 750,000 people.

ZeteoвҖҷs Martin Pengelly and Alexa Cohen contributed to this newsletter.

Still want to get Zeteo content, but not this newsletter? You can unsubscribe directly from вҖҳFirst DraftвҖҷ by going to вҖҳ

If you enjoy Zeteo, share it with your friends and earn rewards when they subscribe.

Of course, one reason the claim that such a display is not blasphemous rings hollow вҖ“ or indeed the claim that itвҖҷs not as if Trump dedicated the statue to himself вҖ“ is that Trump posted a photo depicting himself as Jesus, just weeks ago. And has got into repeated fights with the pope.

ThereвҖҷs also the fact that the Doral statue was paid for by a bunch of cryptocurrency bros who got into a financial dispute with the sculptor, who said he worked to instructions including that the statue be slimmer than Trump actually is, and that it not have what the sculptor called the aging presidentвҖҷs вҖңturkey neck.вҖқ

But then, why bother counting such absurdities? We should remember that the hypocrisy of the religious right has been exposed again and again. The world knows itвҖҷs less concerned with faith in a higher power dedicated to universalist ideals than with faith in whatever enables it to wield the highest power over all. And the unholy essence of religious right support for the Trump administration is the ultimate case in point.

No higher power of mine, or of any normal person, blesses a sexual abuser, a proud racist, a bloodthirsty warmonger, whose foremost accomplishments include bombing schoolchildren and covering up files related to one of the most infamous pedophiles ever to walk the earth.

If a вҖңpastorвҖқ can excuse all that, and more, what difference can a gold-colored statue possibly make?

  • Iran responds:Tehran answered a US proposal to end the war. State mediasaidconditions included an end to the naval blockade and deferred action on its nuclear ambitions. Qatar, UAE, and Kuwait reported Iranian drone attacks.
  • More war:TrumpcalledIranвҖҷs response вҖңtotally unacceptable.вҖқ Speaking toSinclair, he said the US вҖңmay continue military action against Iran for another two weeks,вҖқ adding: вҖңWe have specific objectives we aimed to achieveвҖҰ and itвҖҷs possible we have already accomplished about 70% of them.вҖқ
  • Ground war:Benjamin Netanyahu seemed to tell CBS that Trump wants boots on the ground to tackle issues, including IranвҖҷs nuclear ambitions. вҖңWhat President Trump has said to me, вҖҳI want to go in there,вҖҷвҖқ the Israeli prime ministersaid. вҖңAnd I think it can be done physicallyвҖҰ you go in, and you take it out, why not? ThatвҖҷs the best way.вҖқ
  • Anti-social:Netanyahu also hinted at wider efforts,saying: вҖңI think thereвҖҷs been a concerted effort by several states to basically vilify IsraelвҖҰ primarily in the social media... WeвҖҷve not fought back yet, so weвҖҷll have to do that.вҖқ
  • Lebanon carnage:The Lebanese Health MinistrysaidIsrael killed 51 people in 24 hours, amid a supposed вҖңceasefire.вҖқ Israel вҖңcontinues to violate international laws and humanitarian normsвҖқ by targeting medical assets and workers, the ministry said. Thedeath tollsince the latest escalation began is now at least 2,795 people, including more than 170 children.

On Tuesday, a Democratic primary in Nebraska will pit a populist long-time resident of the state against a two-decade State Department official who moved back just months ago, and who in the Biden administration was part of the diplomatic chain that funneled aid to Israel amid its genocide in Gaza.

In NebraskaвҖҷs first congressional district, career State Department official Chris Backemeyer is taking on Eric Moyer, a renewable energy developer. The seat is held by Republican Mike Flood, who won handily in 2024. But in that yearвҖҷs Senate contest, independent populist Dan Osborn actually won the district against incumbent Republican Deb Fischer.

Backemeyer served in a number of State Department roles over 20 years, as well as briefly in the White House and as an adviser to Vice President Kamala Harris.

Most recently, he was a senior official in the Bureau of Near Eastern Affairs, which is consulted in reviewing weapons transfers to nations in its region. Backemeyer took вҖңresponsibility on behalf of the bureauвҖқ on issues including political-military affairs, foreign assistance, and human rights. Responsibilities also included implementing assistance policy in accordance with US policy, managing foreign assistance funds, and overseeing several divisions, including one focused on Israel and Palestine.

Sources said this purview would have included consulting on matters related to aid to Israel amid its genocide in Gaza.

Several people resigned from diplomatic and military roles under the Biden-Harris administration. Backemeyer left after Donald Trump returned to power, taking a buyout from Elon MuskвҖҷs so-called вҖңDepartment of Government Efficiency,вҖқ saying he felt there was вҖңno mission or purpose leftвҖқ in the department, after being directed to help fire other staffers.

Since then, Backemeyer has maintained a stance virtually indistinguishable from a Biden-Harris State Department spokesperson. Asked in January if he supports continuing to fund Israel, Backemeyer delivered a near-400-word response without answering the question, instead saying the situation was вҖңreally complicatedвҖқ and echoing genocide-justifying claims that Hamas used Palestinians as human shields.

On the same podcast three months earlier, Moyer responded simply, saying he supports conditioning aid to Israel, calling it вҖңunconscionableвҖқ that the matter was even up for debate, given IsraelвҖҷs indiscriminate killing of women and children.

Including a $50,000 loan to himself, Backemeyer has raked in more than $350,000 вҖ“ seven times more than Moyer. A substantial amount has come from outside Nebraska (primarily the DMV), including from ex-Secretary of State Antony Blinken and ex-National Security Adviser Jake Sullivan, corporate lobbyists, and ex-Gulf nation ambassadors.

  • AOC vs. MTG:Passions flared after Alexandria Ocasio-Cortezsaidshe wasnвҖҷt buying far-right Trump apostate Marjorie Taylor GreeneвҖҷs views on IsraelвҖҷs war in Gaza, which MTG hascalleda genocide. AOC said: вҖңI personally do not trustвҖҰ a proven bigot and antisemite onвҖҰ what is good for Gazans and Israelis.вҖқ
  • ADL mourns:Abe Foxman, a Holocaust survivor who led the Anti-Defamation League for 28 years until 2015,diedaged 86. A staunch voice for Israel, he opposed the Islamic community center, the so-called вҖңGround ZeroвҖқ mosque, near the World Trade Center site in Manhattan, and called for the firing of вҖҳBeyond IsraelismвҖҷ host Simone Zimmerman when she worked for Bernie Sanders.
  • DOJ disarray:Fallout from TrumpвҖҷs push to prosecute former FBI Director James Comey has rocked the Department of Justice, damaging vital cases, including one concerning an attack in Afghanistan, theWashington Postreported.
  • Midterms threat:Amid growing fears of GOP election interference, TrumpsaidRepublicans would have an вҖңElection Integrity ArmyвҖқ in each state this year.
  • вҖҳSmaller shotsвҖҷ:Speaking to Sinclair about vaccines, Trumpblurted: вҖңI look at these beautiful little babies, and they get a vat, I mean, like a big glass of stuff pumped into their bodiesвҖҰ I would love to see much smaller shots, like four visits to the doctor. And I think you would have a much better result with the autism.вҖқ
  • Tapes fight:Former President Joe Biden willfighta DOJ attempt to release 70 hours of audio made in 2017 as Biden wrote a memoir. The tapes were provided to the special counsel who investigated BidenвҖҷs retention of classified material.

Commissioned by Congress in 1832 and unveiled in 1840, Horatio GreenoughвҖҷs statue of George Washington, the first such official portrayal of a US president, proved extremely controversial. Why?

Keep your eyes out for the answer below!

  • рҹҮөрҹҮё DonвҖҷt forget Gaza:Israeli strikes killed at least three people, including the head of the criminal police force in Khan Younis, вҖӢWessam Abdel-Hadi, the Gaza Health Ministrysaid. More than 850 Palestinians have been killed since the supposed October ceasefire.
  • рҹҮ®рҹҮ¶ Secret base:Israel built and defended a special forces outpost in Iraq, theWall Street Journalreported. Iraqi troops close to finding it were hit with an airstrike.

  • рҹҮ®рҹҮұ

    Friendly fire:Benjamin Netanyahu emerged unscathed from hisinterviewwith Major Garrett of CBS, the news outlet run by self-confessed вҖңZionist fanaticвҖң Bari Weiss. Barely questioned about Gaza or attacks on civilians in Iran, Netanyahu even claimed, without pushback, that Iranians called him вҖңBeloved Bibi.вҖқ

  • рҹҮөрҹҮұ

    Pole apart:Ex-Justice Minister Zbigniew Ziobro, wanted in his country of Poland for alleged misuse of funds and use of spyware against opponents, and exiled in Hungary until the defeat of Viktor OrbГЎn, isin the US. Trump personally approved his visa.

  • рҹҮӘрҹҮё

    Virus latest:Passengers from the cruise ship stricken by hantavirusbeganto fly home from Tenerife. Three people have died, with five more known infections.


The harrowing documentary film вҖҳGaza: Doctors Under Attack,вҖҷ from Basement Films, won a British Academy Television Award, or BAFTA, in the current affairs category. Referring to IsraelвҖҷs bombing of вҖңevery single one ofвҖқ GazaвҖҷs hospitals, reporter Ramita Navai referred to вҖңthe findings of our investigation that the BBC paid for but refused to show,вҖқ adding: вҖңWe refuse to be silenced and censored.вҖқ Zeteo was proud to acquire the international rights to this award-winning film that the BBC tried to block. You can watch it here.

  • As the average US gas price reached $4.52, Energy Secretary

    Chris Wrightrefused to repeat his previous predictionto NBCвҖҷs вҖҳMeet the PressвҖҷthat it would be under $3 a gallon by summer. вҖңI canвҖҷt predict the price of energy in the short-term or even the medium-term,вҖқ Wright told the same show, despite evidently having done so before.

  • Trump will enter this weekвҖҷs summit with Xi Jinping of China вҖңterribly weakened,вҖқ a top Democrat said.

    Senator Jack ReedtoldFox News Sunday: вҖңWeвҖҷre seeingвҖҰ significant gas prices, significant increases in grocery prices and in all sorts of prices for the American home. And the American people are significantly concerned about this conflict with Iran. So thatвҖҷs not a strong position to be in when youвҖҷre talking to an economic and a geopolitical rival.вҖқ

  • South Carolina congressman and former Democratic House leadership member

    James Clyburnpredicted racist GOP gerrymandering enabled by the Supreme Court might yet backfire. вҖңBe very careful what you pray for,вҖқ the 85-year-oldtoldCNNвҖҷs State of the Union, вҖңbecause what I do believe is that when they finish with the redistricting, there will be the possibilities of at least three Democrats getting elected here in South Carolina to the United States Congress.вҖқ
  • Monday, May 11:The suspect in the White House CorrespondentsвҖҷ Dinner assassination attempt isscheduledto appear in federal court.
  • Tuesday, May 12:Nebraska and West Virginiawillhold US House primaries.
  • Wednesday, May 13:The Sixth Circuit Court of Appeals issetto weigh a Department of Justice bid for Michigan voter roll data.
  • Thursday, May 14:Trump isscheduledto visit China for a two-day summit with Xi Jinping to discuss trade and the US-Iran War.
  • Friday, May 15:Palestinians willmark Nakba Day, to remember the 1948 forced displacement of more than 750,000 people.

ZeteoвҖҷs Martin Pengelly and Alexa Cohen contributed to this newsletter.

item.title: How to Stop Trump From Interfering in the Midterm Elections
item.description: The further Donald Trump falls, the more ways to tilt the mid-term elections he explores. Top election lawyer Marc Elias вҖҷs mantra: вҖңHeвҖҷll try it, and we can fight it.вҖқ Marc Elias specializes in election law, voting rights and campaign finance and in 2020 founded Democracy Docket . He joinedвҖҰ
item.author: Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
The further Donald Trump falls, the more ways to tilt the mid-term elections he explores. Top election lawyer Marc EliasвҖҷs mantra: вҖңHeвҖҷll try it, and we can fight it.вҖқ

Marc Elias specializes in election law, voting rights and campaign finance and in 2020 founded Democracy Docket. He joined ZeteoвҖҷs John Harwood in a live conversation on Substack where subscribers tuned in and asked questions.

Watch the full video above to hear Elias unpack:

  • Why the US Supreme Court gutting the Voting Rights Act was the

    вҖңworst Supreme Court decisionвҖқof his life

  • How he is actively fighting TrumpвҖҷs election manipulation attempts

  • What the legacy media isnвҖҷt telling you about TrumpвҖҷs attempt to seize ballots

  • вҖҰand much more.

Free subscribers can watch a 4-minute preview. Consider upgrading to a paid subscription and never hit a paywall again!

This was a live conversation on Zeteo.com and the Substack app, where subscribers could ask questions. Be sure to subscribe to catch the next Substack Live.

item.title: Israel Kills Palestinian as His Wife Gives Birth, Tortures Peace Activists, and Orders Thousands of West Bank TreeвҖҰ
item.description: Israel killed a Palestinian man while his wife was giving birth. It reportedly tortured peaceful activists. And its ultranationalist national security minister is dreaming of nooses for Palestinians. Plus, the Trump administration ramped up its efforts to deport pro-Palestine student protestвҖҰ
item.author: This Week in Palestine | Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
Israel killed a Palestinian man while his wife was giving birth. It reportedly tortured peaceful activists. And its ultranationalist national security minister is dreaming of nooses for Palestinians. Plus, the Trump administration ramped up its efforts to deport pro-Palestine student protest leaders.

This is just a glimpse of the reality Palestinians and their supporters lived through this week:

Organizers of the Global Sumud Flotilla that Israeli forces violently and illegally intercepted in international waters last week reported that the two leaders Israel kidnapped and took to Israel suffered torture and other severe mistreatment during their detention.

An Israeli court extended Saif Abukeshek and Thiago ГҒvilaвҖҷs detention. No charges have been filed. Both are on hunger strike, drinking only water. ГҒvilaвҖҷs mother died later this week.

(On May 9, the lawyers representing the two activists announced they were expected to be released.)

Israeli forces raided the occupied West Bank city of Nablus and killed 26-year-old Nayef Samaro, who was heading to the hospital where his wife was giving birth to their first child. Samaro had been shopping for baby clothes, per Haaretz. His body was taken to the same hospital where his wife was in labor. Their son, Yaman, was born less than 24 hours later.

Israeli forces wounded several others, including a 12-year-old child, during the raid.

Ultranationalist Israeli Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir posted to TikTok a video about dreaming of nooses вҖ“ a reference to the recently passed racist death penalty law for Palestinians he championed.

Over the weekend, he celebrated his 50th birthday with a noose cake. (Read Zeteo contributor Diana ButtuвҖҷs essay on how Ben-GvirвҖҷs obsession with killing Palestinians reflects something in Israeli society that is much bigger than just him.)

The UN Palestinian refugee agency (UNRWA) warned that more and more people in Gaza are suffering skin infections вҖңdue to a surge in rats, lice & fleas.вҖқ

Humanitarians in recent weeks have repeatedly sounded the alarm that rodent bites are becoming increasingly common across the Strip.

UNRWA said that because basic medicines are in short supply, вҖңmany children are left without the treatment they need.вҖқ

People in #Gaza are suffering from skin infections due to a surge in rats, lice & fleas. UNRWA health teams continue to treat thousands of cases. In Gaza basic medicines are in short supply, many children are left without the treatment they need. Aid at scale needs to be allowed in.В  #UNRWAworks

Tue, 05 May 2026 09:00:22 GMT

Ultranationalist Israeli Finance Minister Bezalel Smotrich ordered IsraelвҖҷs governing administration in the occupied West Bank to uproot 3,000 trees, Haaretz reported.

This is on top of the thousands of trees Israel has uprooted on Palestinian land as it continues its illegal settlement building and de facto annexation of the West Bank.

IsraelвҖҷs manufactured malnutrition crisis in Gaza is having devastating consequences for pregnant women and their children, Doctors Without Borders (MSF) reported.

Between June 2025 and January 2026, over half of the 201 mothers of newborns MSF tracked were malnourished at some point during their pregnancy. Of the babies born to mothers who had experienced malnutrition, 90% were born prematurely, and 84% had a low birth weight.

The New York Times reported that documents it obtained show the case to deport Columbia University student protest leader Mahmoud Khalil was considered a high priority for the Board of Immigration Appeals before it even received the case. He could be deported before the Supreme Court hears his case, the Times noted.

The board last month denied KhalilвҖҷs appeal to have his case thrown out and issued final removal orders. According to the Times, there have been several unusual aspects to KhalilвҖҷs case, including that the board made its decision within nine days of the final court documents being filed вҖ“ an unusually quick timeline вҖ“ and that at least three judges recused themselves from the case. KhalilвҖҷs lawyer told the Times that the abnormalities showed the case вҖңhas been controlled from Day 1 by higher-ups in the administration.вҖқ

Separately, the board reinstated immigration proceedings against Palestinian Columbia University student Mohsen Mahdawi earlier this week.

item.title: The Democratic Senate Map Doesn't Look So Bad Anymore
item.description: Depressed enough by the Republican election sweep, Democrats in November 2024 sank even deeper when they considered the shape of Donald TrumpвҖҷs victory. In 24 of the 31 states he carried, Trump routed Kamala Harris by double-digit margins. Since polarization increasingly aligns election outcomes upвҖҰ
item.author: "Zeteo | U.S. Politics"
item.content:encoded:
Depressed enough by the Republican election sweep, Democrats in November 2024 sank even deeper when they considered the shape of Donald TrumpвҖҷs victory.

In 24 of the 31 states he carried, Trump routed Kamala Harris by double-digit margins. Since polarization increasingly aligns election outcomes up and down the ballot, that sent an ominous signal about DemocratsвҖҷ future competitiveness for other offices too. Their path toward recapturing the Senate ran most steeply uphill of all.

Six months before Election Day 2026, that path doesnвҖҷt look so steep anymore.

TrumpвҖҷs steadily eroding public standing вҖ“ 35% approval in the latest CNN polling average вҖ“ has Democrats confident of regaining a majority in the House. Each of the last five presidents have suffered that blow in midterm contests.

But regaining the Senate вҖ“ the chokepoint for a presidentвҖҷs Cabinet and judicial appointments вҖ“ matters more. Democrats need to carry at least two states that candidate Trump won by at least 11 percentage points.

And the collision of TrumpвҖҷs second presidency with reality has dramatically boosted the odds that they can. The old 2026 shorthand: вҖҳbad map.вҖҷ The new, hopeful one, from Democratic pollster Geoff Garin: вҖңThe map is really enlarged.вҖқ

Take a stroll across it.

Ohio has grown increasingly red since Barack Obama carried it in his 2012 re-election. But Democrats have a strong shot with former Senator Sherrod Brown, an economic populist who lost by just 3.5 percentage points last election cycle as Trump won Ohio by triple that margin.

Democrats havenвҖҷt won an Alaska Senate seat since 2008. But they recruited an ideal candidate in Mary Peltola, whoвҖҷs already been elected statewide and faces vulnerable incumbent Dan Sullivan.

Iowa no longer figures in Democratic presidential planning. But Trump has fallen underwater there, joining unpopular GOP Governor Kim Reynolds, and incumbent Senator Joni Ernst decided not to run. The betting-market favorite for the Democratic nomination, Josh Turek, boasts a compelling non-political biography as a two-time gold medalist for the US Paralympic basketball team.

In Texas, dreams of a blue revival looked comatose after Trump gained ground with Latinos in 2024. Now polls show those gains evaporating, a bitter Republican primary pits a damaged establishment incumbent against a corrupt MAGA challenger, and Democratic nominee James Talarico displays unusual gifts as a Christian-themed communicator in an unusually religious state.

Democrats nurture long-shot hopes even where they shouldnвҖҷt have a prayer: states like Nebraska and Montana (both with strong Democrat-friendly independent candidates), South Carolina (punch-drunk incumbent Lindsey Graham was recently photographed carrying a bubble wand at Disney World), and Florida (whose incumbent gained her seat by appointment, not election).

At minimum, Garin notes, вҖңRepublicans are going to have to play defense in a lot of places.вҖқ

Yes, in all those red states, Republicans remain favorites. So they remain favorites to hold the Senate. But not by much.

What reordered the landscape is a near-perfect storm of political malpractice.

Elected to reduce consumer prices, Trump pushed them higher with tariffs and higher still with his bungled Iran War. He obsesses on gold-plated baubles (like a new White House ballroom) rather than the problems of ordinary Americans. His pay-for-play corruption underscores the vast inequality of wealth that has embittered his MAGA supporters. Violent excesses on deportations have squandered his advantage on immigration.

Younger voters Trump added in 2024, like his Latino voters, have fled. Remember all the noise about Joe BidenвҖҷs fitness? A Washington Post-ABC News-Ipsos poll last week showed most Americans believe Trump at 79 lacks the physical and mental health to serve.

All that solidifies Democratic prospects for their must-hold battleground seats. In Georgia, Jon Ossoff has performed strongly enough to generate 2028 presidential buzz; in Michigan, the Democratic tailwind reduces the stakes of their tough primary contest.

The two fattest Republican targets have grown ever-more promising. In North Carolina, which has teased Democrats for nearly 20 years, former Governor Roy Cooper holds the early edge.

In Maine, ugly past statements and a Nazi-style tattoo didnвҖҷt stop populist Graham Platner from grabbing the Democratic nomination from a two-term governor. The 41-year-old oysterman reflects the partyвҖҷs turn toward younger candidates. And he may have caught the five-term Republican senator, 73-year-old Susan Collins, just when her thoughtful-moderate persona has passed its political expiration date.

Collins and all Republican incumbents face a common problem. The GOPвҖҷs near-total submission to Trump prevents them from credibly distancing themselves from his unpopularity, the go-to midterm move for members of any presidentвҖҷs party.

Has the ground shifted enough for the four-seat gain Democrats need? The recent special election for TennesseeвҖҷs 7th House district provides a cautionary tale.

In 2024, Trump swamped Harris there by 22 percentage points. By December 2025, the altered political environment fueled hopes of a Democratic upset. But the district simply proved too red, and energetic young Democrat Aftyn Behn ultimately lost by nine points.

BehnвҖҷs impressive improvement on HarrisвҖҷ 2024 showing: 13 points. The DemocratsвҖҷ 2024 deficit in Alaska, Iowa, and Texas: slightly more than 13 points.

Twenty-year-old memories give Democratic strategists hope. In 2006, they needed to gain six seats to recapture the Senate, including a couple in states George W. Bush had won easily two years earlier.

That spring, it remained a long-shot. But in the runup to Election Day, the political climate kept improving вҖ“ just enough for Democrats to pull it off.

John Harwood is the former chief Washington correspondent for CNBC and White House correspondent for CNN. He has interviewed every president from George H.W. Bush to Joe Biden. Sign up for вҖҳThe Stakes with John HarwoodвҖҷ to get all of his columns in your inbox.

item.title: BidenвҖҷs Genocide Squad Must Be Stopped Before They Strike Again
item.description: Avril Haines, the former Director of National Intelligence who ordered me and thousands of other intelligence officials to overlook concerns about war crimes so we would continue feeding intelligence to the Israeli military, has had the grace to largely avoid the public eye since leaving office,вҖҰ
item.author: Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
Avril Haines, the former Director of National Intelligence who ordered me and thousands of other intelligence officials to overlook concerns about war crimes so we would continue feeding intelligence to the Israeli military, has had the grace to largely avoid the public eye since leaving office, which is what you or I might do if we felt responsible for one of the worst crimes in modern history.

Not so for the small inner circle of Biden advisers who gave Haines her orders. The men behind Joe BidenвҖҷs genocidal вҖңbear hugвҖқ policy of unconditional US support for the Israeli government are now popping up everywhere from Stephen Colbert to the op-ed pages of the New York Times to their own podcasts, presenting themselves as responsible experts critiquing TrumpвҖҷs chaotic foreign policy. The Biden guys, far from seeking anonymity or amnesty, expect to be rewarded for their performance in Gaza with continued influence in the Democratic Party and powerful roles in the next administration. ThatвҖҷs why, even knee-deep in multiple Trump crises, we canвҖҷt afford to ignore the ambitions of BidenвҖҷs top advisers. HereвҖҷs how theyвҖҷre continuing to influence DemocratsвҖҷ foreign policy as if Gaza never happened:

  • Former Secretary of State

    Antony Blinken, whoskirtedthe law,lied to Congress, andcooked the bookson human rights reporting to keep arming the military carrying out the Gaza genocide, is now on theboard of directorsof the influential Democratic think tank Center for American Progress.
  • Jake Sullivan, BidenвҖҷs national security adviser and kingpin of his Middle East policy, who alsofoughtto keep arming Israel and lied repeatedly that we were вҖңcloseвҖқ to a ceasefirehis administration hadno intentionof achieving, isback on the boardof National Security Action, a group he cofounded that seeks to guide DemocratsвҖҷ foreign policy positions.

  • SullivanвҖҷs former deputy,

    Jon Finer, who alsoliedto the public that Biden was pressuring Israel for a deal, has alsojoinedthe Center for American Progress as a senior fellow. Along with Sullivan, heвҖҷs also on theadvisory boardof Foreign Policy for America, a Democrat-aligned advocacy group with a previously solid record on Gaza.

  • Middle East lead

    Brett McGurk, whogreenlitIsraelвҖҷs bloody 2024 invasion of Lebanon and appeared tocondonethe Israeli government using hunger as a weapon вҖ“ he and aides later reportedlydismissedreports of starvation in Gaza by quipping вҖңwhere are all the skinny kids?вҖқ вҖ“ is now a ubiquitous talking head for CNN. HeвҖҷs not doing TV for the money вҖ“ he couldcash inon his Gulf diplomatic connections in private if he wanted.

It cannot be overstated that none of the top Biden вҖ“ or Harris вҖ“ advisers have publicly acknowledged, much less apologized for, their individual contributions to the Gaza genocide, leaving us to take it on faith that they would not abet a genocide again if given the chance in 2029 or beyond.

But why would they owe us any answers? Zero accountability in US foreign policy is the norm. Blinken and Joe Biden himself supported the invasion of Iraq and came out ahead; no officials faced real consequences for Iraq, Libya, Yemen, or for any number of other violent misadventures championed by foreign policy elites. US support for the Gaza genocide is only possible because US officials at all levels understand opposing war crimes is far more hazardous to your career than supporting them.

This inverted accountability structure relies on tacit elite acceptance that the DCвҖҷs victims are disposable. Ivy League universities looked past piles of Palestinian bodies to award posts to Sullivan, McGurk, and Finer last year. Harvard, squashing any doubts about the continuity of elite impunity, created a professorship for Jake Sullivan named after Henry Kissinger, which could also be read as a warning about the body count Sullivan might accrue if allowed back in government.

Sullivan canвҖҷt even think of anything he would have done differently in Gaza, nor can Blinken or McGurk. The closest thing to a mea culpa from team Biden came in an op-ed by Finer and top Harris national security adviser Phil Gordon that flatly declared BidenвҖҷs rationale for unconditional support for IsraelвҖҷs government вҖңbecame far less compelling over time.вҖқ They published this in September 2025 вҖ“ months after the horrific Trump-endorsed famine in Gaza made mainstream Democrats comfortable criticizing the Israeli government вҖ“ suggesting Finer and Gordon are in possession of more of a weathervane than a backbone. The same goes double for Sullivan, who, around the same time, declared he supported conditioning future arms sales to Israel, with zero reexamination of his own refusal to do just that while he was in power.

Belated post-mortems from Biden officials may occasionally tepidly pin blame on Biden, now conveniently a political non-entity, but never blame the other advisers who actually ran the sunsetting presidentвҖҷs Mideast policy, who are still very much in play. Once you notice these omissions, itвҖҷs hard to avoid the conclusion that the first priority of even the most remorseful Biden advisers is not repairing US foreign policy, itвҖҷs protecting the other offenders вҖ“ and themselves.

ThatвҖҷs the charitable explanation for why a Biden alum would inject themself back into politics without trying to answer for the tens or hundreds of thousands of lives they destroyed or the millions of Americans they deliberately misled, but it also shows why they canвҖҷt be trusted to make the right calls if given a second chance.

Every member of BidenвҖҷs Gaza team, even those who voiced concerns after his term or вҖ“ allegedly вҖ“ during it, continues to make it abundantly clear their solidarity lies with their accomplices, not their victims.

If thereвҖҷs any hope for justice for those victims, itвҖҷs that the excesses of the Trump administration have pushed a growing number of Democratic lawmakers to start talking about accountability. For now, itвҖҷs accountability for out-of-control ICE and border patrol agents, for generals who carry out TrumpвҖҷs unlawful orders, and for Trump officials themselves. But a campaign for accountability can be neither credible nor effective if it applies only to members of one political party.

What would accountability for Blinken, Sullivan, et al, in addition to their Trump administration counterparts, look like? The Department of Justice could investigate them for violating federal laws governing arms transfers. Congress could ratify the Rome Statute, making them eligible for trials at The Hague, or pass laws making it easier for victimsвҖҷ families to sue US officials. The US could even sign an extradition treaty with a future Palestinian state.

More realistic in the near term is political accountability. Politicians who solicit advice from BidenвҖҷs genocide squad deserve to be treated the same way as those who take money from AIPAC: Unworthy of our trust on issues of morality and national security and dangerously out of touch with Democratic voters.

Finally, itвҖҷs worth acknowledging the argument in favor of keeping the Biden bunch in rotation, which boils down to: lawmakers and donors already know them, and think theyвҖҷre experienced and really smart. ItвҖҷs tough to argue with someone who thinks experience facilitating a genocide is a valuable resume bullet, and the superior intelligence of this cohort is debatable вҖ“ Blinken tried to simultaneously tut-tut and claim credit for TrumpвҖҷs first strikes on Iran, and Harris adviser Ilan Goldenberg argued this January that Trump should threaten Iran with airstrikes. But even if you believe in the powers of renowned superbrain Jake Sullivan, we should challenge the notion that the Biden Gaza team is irreplaceable. DC, in fact, has no shortage of talented people who understand government. Some even have principles. Democrats can craft policy and fully staff the National Security Council without an Operation Paperclip for Biden alums who belong at The Hague.

There is room for debate on the scope of accountability, but not on whether to pursue it. Accountability for DCвҖҷs genocide class is an imperative: To seek justice for their victims, to prove post-Trump that the United States is still a nation of laws, to keep the original architects of the Gaza genocide from worming their way back into positions where they can again inflict unspeakable harm, and to teach the next generation of national security officials that their careers are best served by preventing war crimes, not abetting them.

What does the world look like if we fail to hold the genocide class to account? YouвҖҷre living in it.

Harrison Mann is a former US Army major and executive officer of the Defense Intelligence AgencyвҖҷs Middle East/Africa Regional Center who resigned in protest of his officeвҖҷs support for IsraelвҖҷs war in Gaza under the Biden administration. He is currently with the group Win Without War and recently became a Zeteo contributor.
