For any issues with your subscription, please email us at info@zeteonews.com.

item.title: Zeteo Turns Two! Exclusive Photos From Our Anniversary Party
item.description: Two whole years! Last week, Zeteo took a night off and marked its second birthday with a jam-packed, electric evening in Manhattan. Held at acclaimed Lebanese bistro Au ZaвҖҷatar in Midtown and sponsored by Substack , the celebration brought together entertainers, politicians, and journalists вҖ“ fromвҖҰ
item.author: Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
Two whole years!

Last week, Zeteo took a night off and marked its second birthday with a jam-packed, electric evening in Manhattan.

Held at acclaimed Lebanese bistro Au ZaвҖҷatar in Midtown and sponsored by Substack, the celebration brought together entertainers, politicians, and journalists вҖ“ from Chef JosГ© AndrГ©s to Zohran Mamdani.

The night was a testament to the power of independent media, and a measure of a new age of journalism built on truth-telling, holding power to account, and asking the tough questions that other outlets wonвҖҷt. In this increasingly chaotic media landscape, weвҖҷd like to think Zeteo has become a force to be reckoned with.

WeвҖҷre thrilled to share these photos with you. We couldnвҖҷt have made it two whole years without the generous support of you, the subscribers, and especially our paid subscribers. Thank you for having our back.

Alright, party over. Year three of Zeteo starts now! We have no plans to stop making noise with hard-hitting interviews, sharp analysis, and bombshell documentaries. And we want you along for the ride because this is your platform as much as ours. So tell us in the comments: what do you want from Zeteo this year? WeвҖҷre listening.

Onwards and upwards!

P.S., find more photos from the party here.

Catch up on more from ZeteoвҖҷs anniversary celebration:

Thank you for being a paid subscriber to Zeteo, your support helps keep this media movement alive. If youвҖҷd like to wear your support, donвҖҷt forget to check out

For any issues with your subscription, please email us at info@zeteonews.com.

item.title: Trump Is Only Pretending to вҖҳFightвҖҷ Antisemitism
item.description: On this day in 1970, national guardsmen shot dead four students and wounded nine during an anti-Vietnam War protest at Kent State University in Ohio. No soldier was held accountable, but images of the killings remain famous, most recently being republished as Donald Trump sent the National Guard toвҖҰ
item.author: First Draft | Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
On this day in 1970, national guardsmen shot dead four students and wounded nine during an anti-Vietnam War protest at Kent State University in Ohio. No soldier was held accountable, but images of the killings remain famous, most recently being republished as Donald Trump sent the National Guard to Democratic cities.

Happy Monday; itвҖҷs Swin again. This is the first full week of Jewish American Heritage Month 2026, and Donald Trump and his administration are continuing to push the absurd lie that they are committed to stopping antisemitism. This next statement wonвҖҷt shock many Zeteo readers, but: noвҖҰ the MAGA-era Republican Party вҖ“ which constantly acts like it wants to jail the families who hide the little girl in the attic вҖ“ does not actually care about fighting antisemitism.

In todayвҖҷs вҖҳFirst Draft,вҖҷ we have another scoop for you underscoring just how little they care. Plus, TrumpвҖҷs polling hits another low, the Strait of Hormuz remains closed, oil prices continue to soar, and acting Attorney General Todd Blanche thinks you need to show your ID to eat at a restaurant.

Ever since he returned to power, Donald Trump and his top officials have claimed they are вҖңcombating antisemitismвҖқ вҖ“ and have used this as a pretext for launching crackdown after crackdown on free speech, immigration, students, civil liberties, and TrumpвҖҷs enemies.

It may have been slightly easier for Team Trump to claim that it is fighting antisemitism if the administration werenвҖҷt also simultaneously pulling the plug on programs designed to fight antisemitism. Zeteo spoke at length with the director of one such program, which lost its longtime State Department funding shortly after Trump regained the keys to the Oval Office.

In mid-April 2025, Secretary of State Marco Rubio announced on Elon MuskвҖҷs Twitter that вҖңunder my direction,вҖқ the State Department has вҖңcanceled another 139 grants worth $214 million.вҖқ He added: вҖңAmerican taxpayers should not be funding misguided programs like вҖҳBuilding the Migrant Domestic Worker-Led MovementвҖҷ in Lebanon or вҖҳGet the Trolls Out!вҖҷ in the United Kingdom.вҖқ

According to its website, Get the Trolls Out is вҖңled by the Media Diversity Institute (MDI) with the support of six partners spread throughout Europe.вҖқ The project has operated in multiple countries, and its programs are aimed at fighting antisemitism in media, social media, and politics. The project and MDI have long sought to push back against various forms of bigotry and hate, including anti-Muslim hatred.

MDI was also a signatory to a 2024 letter urging the Israeli government to allow reporters open access to Gaza. And in September 2025, the organization joined a protest in Brussels opposing IsraelвҖҷs slaughter of journalists in Gaza. вҖңWe stand by our colleagues in Gaza who bravely risk their lives to document conflict,вҖқ Luc Steinberg, the head of operations at MDI Global, said in a statement. вҖңWe demand those responsible for their deaths be held accountable.вҖқ

But the groupвҖҷs focus on combating antisemitism was so prominent that вҖ“ according to documents that were read to Zeteo вҖ“ when the State Department inked its contracts with MDI to help fund its вҖңTrollsвҖқ-related work, the titles or lead paragraphs of multiple grant applications were specifically about fighting anti-Jewish hatred.

The organization had partnered with the US State Department over the course of Republican and Democratic presidencies, and the first Trump administration saw the work worthwhile enough to keep funding the project during his initial four years in office. That all changed last year.

The head of MDI says that the Get the Trolls Out initiatives can no longer operate in multiple countries, now that the Rubio-era State Department pulled the funding. Efforts to appeal to the US government and restore the partnership have, apparently, gone nowhere in recent months.

вҖңWe still believe there has been some misunderstanding of the work we have been doing under the State DepartmentвҖҷs funding; combating antisemitism is the objective we have in common,вҖқ Milica Pesic, executive director at the London-based MDI, tells Zeteo. вҖңItвҖҷs the people, the communities we care for, who suffer the most because of this misunderstanding.вҖқ

For more than a year, this is the game that the Trump-Vance administration has played, and youвҖҷd have to be a stooge or a chump to fall for it. Just last week, Trump uber-loyalist Harmeet Dhillon, one of the Justice DepartmentвҖҷs most resplendently MAGAfied figures, delivered a speech comparing the alleged antisemitism among the вҖңeducated elitesвҖқ in the United States today to the rise of Nazi Germany.

Meanwhile, an array of Jewish groups and activists have spent the last year warning that the Trump administrationвҖҷs actions carried out under the guise of countering antisemitism are, in fact, making American Jews less safe. A study last year found nearly three-quarters of American Jews can easily see through President TrumpвҖҷs bullshit, and believe he вҖңis using antisemitism as an excuse to penalize and tax college campuses.вҖқ

Indeed, the Trump administration is now in court trying to force the University of Pennsylvania to hand over a list of Jews on its campus. (The easiest possible IQ test on the planet is: If the Trump administration asks you to turn over your list of Jews, is it a good idea to do it?)

None of this should be surprising, given that Trump is a demagogue and demagogues are always looking for minorities to scapegoat. In the past decade, he has repeatedly stereotyped and lashed out at the clear majority of American Jews, sneering at them that they are bad, disloyal Jews because they donвҖҷt vote for Trump вҖ“ and donвҖҷt appreciate everything heвҖҷs done for Israel.

This president and his goon squad running the federal bureaucracy only care about вҖңantisemitismвҖқ to the extent that they can use it to try to crush their enemies, or to cynically play minority groups off of one another.

The GOP and this administration, by their actions and their words, very clearly do not give a damn about eliminating the scourge of antisemitism вҖ“ they care about protecting the feelings and interests of their fellow far-right Trump fans in Israel.

Death tolls:

  • Iran:Iranian state mediareported, citing officials, that US-Israeli attacks have killed 3,468 people.
  • Lebanon:Israel has killed more than 2,679 people, including at least 177 children, in Lebanon, according to thecountryвҖҷs Health Ministry.
  • US troops:At least 13 US troops have died in incidents related to the war.
  • Israel:At least35people, including 12 Israeli soldiers, have been killed, according to officials.

And in more war newsвҖҰ

  • вҖҳOperation FreedomвҖҷ:Donald Trumpsaidthe US would start вҖңguidingвҖқ ships from neutral countries through the Strait of Hormuz вҖ“apparentlywithout a naval escort. He also claimed вҖңvery positiveвҖқ talks with Iran, but promised вҖңforcefulвҖқ action if the movement of ships was вҖңinterfered with.вҖқ
  • Iran balks:IranвҖҷs state mediacalledTrumpвҖҷs plan part of his вҖңdelirium,вҖқ and the countryвҖҷs officialswarnedthat вҖңforeign armed forces, especially the aggressive вҖӢU.S. Army, will be attacked.вҖқ
  • Strait strikes:The UKsaida bulk carrier wasattackedby a small craft in the waterway. The US said its blockade of Iranian ports extended to a 49th ship.
  • OPEC acts:Seven countries in the oil countriesвҖҷ alliancesaidthey would increase production in June, in pursuit of вҖңmarket stability.вҖқ
  • Dire diesel:The closure of the Strait continued to affect US fuel prices. In Michigan, a swing state, diesel pricesreachedan all-time average high of $5.99.
  • Press plea:Pope LeomarkedWorld Press Freedom Day by paying tribute to вҖңthe many journalists and reporters who have been victims of war and violence.вҖқ Israel haskilledat least 260 journalists since 2023.

New poll lows are common for Donald Trump these days вҖ“ however often he retreats to his own personal safe space, the Villages, to rave about his supposed success, as he did on Friday. But even by the presidentвҖҷs standards, the news from the Washington Post, ABC News, and Ipsos on Sunday was very far from good.

In the survey, TrumpвҖҷs disapproval rating soared to 62%. Given that such numbers take work вҖ“ not just adverse political or economic headwinds, but real effort to sink so low вҖ“ itвҖҷs worth taking a look at just how Trump is screwing up. On the cost of living, a whopping 76% of Americans disapprove of his performance. (He says cost-of-living concerns are Democratic lies, which is, of course, a lie.) On inflation, 72% disapprove. (He says inflation is вҖңsolvedвҖқ and вҖңdone.вҖқ) On the war with Iran, which is exacerbating such problems, 66% donвҖҷt like what Trump is doing. On the economy as a whole, disapproval stands at 65%.

TrumpвҖҷs standing with Republicans remains good, at 85%, but only 25% of independents approve of the job heвҖҷs doing. Predictably, the WaPo/ABC/Ipsos poll made good reading for Democrats hoping to take the US House.

And then thereвҖҷs the mental health question, which Trump himself keeps harping on about, as in his rambling speech at the Villages when he went back to the well about a вҖңcognitive testвҖқ he supposedly aced. You know, the one about вҖңa lion, a giraffe, a bear, and a shark [and] they say, which one is the bear?вҖқ

Oddly enough, given such utterances and according to the Post, вҖңNearly 6 in 10 (59%)вҖқ say Trump вҖңdoes not have the mental sharpnessвҖқ to serve effectively, while вҖңa majority (55%) say he is not in good enough physical health to serve effectively. Both are slightly more negative than they were in the February survey and are significantly more negative than last September.вҖқ

  • Odd Todd:Acting AG Todd BlanchetoldNBC the indictment of former FBI Director James Comey for threatening Donald Trump with an вҖң86 47вҖқ message made of seashells was вҖңnot just about a single Instagram post. This is about a body of evidence the grand jury collected over вҖҰ 11 months.вҖқ Funnily enough, Blanche didnвҖҷt say what that evidence was вҖ“ and also said thousands of other people who have broadcast such messages are not being investigated.
  • Threat spree:Trump carried on issuing actual threats,sayingformer AG Eric Holder, who resigned in 2015, was guilty of вҖңtreasonous conductвҖқ for supposedly rigging the 2020 election вҖ“ which Trump lost fair and square.
  • Italian job:Secretary of State Marco Rubio is off to Italy,TheGuardianreported, with a mission to thaw relations with PM Giorgia Meloni and Pope Leo, subjects of TrumpвҖҷs abuse and bizarre AI posts.
  • Speaking of whichвҖҰTrumpsharedan image of himself, Rubio, JD Vance, and Doug Burgum, all shirtless, with a bikini-clad woman in the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool. In the real world, the construction site for TrumpвҖҷs renovation of the pool was sprayed with graffiti referencing ComeyвҖҷs вҖң86 47вҖқ message.
  • True colors:Trump alsoposteda real picture of House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries, accompanied by racist abuse. Jeffriesfiredback.
  • Giuliani hospitalized:Former New York Mayor turned Trump henchman Rudy Giuliani, 81, was on Sunday in critical condition in a Florida hospital, his spokespersonsaid.

According to a debunked if persistent urban myth, the profile of which prominent historical figure is carved into the back of the head of the Abraham Lincoln statue at the Lincoln Memorial in Washington?

Keep your eyes out for the answer below!

  • рҹҮөрҹҮё DonвҖҷt forget Gaza:Israelextendedits detention of two members of an aid flotilla, as their lawyer said theyвҖҷve suffered beatings by Israeli authorities. Meanwhile, Israeli National Security Minister (and sociopath?) Itamar Ben-Gvir celebrated his birthday withcakesdecorated with nooses and guns: a reference to the recent law imposing the death penalty for Palestinians only.
  • рҹҮәрҹҮҰрҹҮ·рҹҮә Drone wave:Ukrainelaunchedattacks against Russia, hitting Baltic naval and oil facilities. A Kremlin spokesperson said such strikes could cause global oil prices affected by the US-Israel-Iran war to rise yet further.
  • рҹҮҝрҹҮҰ Virus alert:Three are dead and at least three are in intensive care in asuspected outbreakof the rodent-spread hantavirus on a cruise ship in the Atlantic. Victims were taken to South Africa, where authorities were checking for further cases.
  • рҹҮІрҹҮұ Critic taken:A former minister turned critic of the Mali junta was abducted by armed and hooded men, APreported. Mountaga Tall, a former education minister, now leads a party opposed to the military government, which has been attacked by militant groups.


Not sure what kind of restaurants Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche eats at, where he says you have to show ID to enter. Maybe theyвҖҷre next door to wherever Donald Trump buys groceries.

  • On

    CNNвҖҷs вҖҳState of the Union,вҖҷformer Massachusetts congressmanBarney Frank, who has entered hospice care, continued publicity for a book he may not see published. A leading campaigner for LGBTQ+ rights, Frank, 86, now thinks Democrats have shifted too far left too quickly on the вҖңTвҖқ part of that acronym. Nor is he much of a fan of Graham Platner, the insurgent progressive who beat Governor Janet Mills in the Maine Senate primary. According to Frank, Platner вҖңactually shares with Trump this capacity toward making the most out of the anger that people feel.вҖқ

  • Former Fox host

    Jeannine Pirrois both the US attorney for the District of Columbia and the owner of one of the best rude nicknames in politics. On Sunday, вҖңJudge Box of WineвҖқspoke to Jake Tapperof CNN about the shooting at the White House CorrespondentsвҖҷ Dinner in DC last weekend. Confirming it was the suspect who shot a Secret Service agent saved by his bulletproof vest, Pirro also seemed inadvertently to confirm the shooterвҖҷs manifesto use of the phrase вҖңa pedophile rapist and traitorвҖқ was a reference to Donald Trump.

  • On

    CBSвҖҷs вҖҳFace the Nation,вҖҷSenator Raphael Warnockof Georgia lamented the effective death of the Voting Rights Act of 1965, as confirmed this week by the right-wing Supreme Court in a ruling that triggered action in Louisiana to wipe out Black congressional seats. вҖңLetвҖҷs be clear,вҖқ said the senator, also a pastor at thechurchwhere Martin Luther King, Jr. preached. вҖңWhat happened this week is nothing less than a massive and devastating blow, not only to our democracy, but particularly to people of color in the South.вҖқ
  • Tuesday, May 5:Ohio and Indiana arescheduledto hold primary elections. Plus, EU finance ministers are scheduled to meet to discuss the Iran War. PanamaвҖҷs President JosГ© RaГәl Mulino issetto host Israeli President Isaac Herzog.
  • Wednesday, May 6:Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick issetfor a voluntary interview by the House Oversight Committee in its Jeffrey Epstein investigation.
  • Thursday, May 7:The US Court of Appeals for DCwillconsider the PentagonвҖҷs bid to punish Senator Mark Kelly for telling troops to resist illegal orders.
  • Friday, May 8:Right-wing populist Laura Fernandez issetto take office as Costa RicaвҖҷs president.


ZeteoвҖҷs Martin Pengelly contributed to this newsletter.

Still want to get Zeteo content, but not this newsletter? You can unsubscribe directly from вҖҳFirst DraftвҖҷ by going to вҖҳ

If you enjoy Zeteo, share it with your friends and earn rewards when they subscribe.

  • Dire diesel:The closure of the Strait continued to affect US fuel prices. In Michigan, a swing state, diesel pricesreachedan all-time average high of $5.99.
  • Press plea:Pope LeomarkedWorld Press Freedom Day by paying tribute to вҖңthe many journalists and reporters who have been victims of war and violence.вҖқ Israel haskilledat least 260 journalists since 2023.

New poll lows are common for Donald Trump these days вҖ“ however often he retreats to his own personal safe space, the Villages, to rave about his supposed success, as he did on Friday. But even by the presidentвҖҷs standards, the news from the Washington Post, ABC News, and Ipsos on Sunday was very far from good.

In the survey, TrumpвҖҷs disapproval rating soared to 62%. Given that such numbers take work вҖ“ not just adverse political or economic headwinds, but real effort to sink so low вҖ“ itвҖҷs worth taking a look at just how Trump is screwing up. On the cost of living, a whopping 76% of Americans disapprove of his performance. (He says cost-of-living concerns are Democratic lies, which is, of course, a lie.) On inflation, 72% disapprove. (He says inflation is вҖңsolvedвҖқ and вҖңdone.вҖқ) On the war with Iran, which is exacerbating such problems, 66% donвҖҷt like what Trump is doing. On the economy as a whole, disapproval stands at 65%.

TrumpвҖҷs standing with Republicans remains good, at 85%, but only 25% of independents approve of the job heвҖҷs doing. Predictably, the WaPo/ABC/Ipsos poll made good reading for Democrats hoping to take the US House.

And then thereвҖҷs the mental health question, which Trump himself keeps harping on about, as in his rambling speech at the Villages when he went back to the well about a вҖңcognitive testвҖқ he supposedly aced. You know, the one about вҖңa lion, a giraffe, a bear, and a shark [and] they say, which one is the bear?вҖқ

Oddly enough, given such utterances and according to the Post, вҖңNearly 6 in 10 (59%)вҖқ say Trump вҖңdoes not have the mental sharpnessвҖқ to serve effectively, while вҖңa majority (55%) say he is not in good enough physical health to serve effectively. Both are slightly more negative than they were in the February survey and are significantly more negative than last September.вҖқ

  • Odd Todd:Acting AG Todd BlanchetoldNBC the indictment of former FBI Director James Comey for threatening Donald Trump with an вҖң86 47вҖқ message made of seashells was вҖңnot just about a single Instagram post. This is about a body of evidence the grand jury collected over вҖҰ 11 months.вҖқ Funnily enough, Blanche didnвҖҷt say what that evidence was вҖ“ and also said thousands of other people who have broadcast such messages are not being investigated.
  • Threat spree:Trump carried on issuing actual threats,sayingformer AG Eric Holder, who resigned in 2015, was guilty of вҖңtreasonous conductвҖқ for supposedly rigging the 2020 election вҖ“ which Trump lost fair and square.
  • Italian job:Secretary of State Marco Rubio is off to Italy,TheGuardianreported, with a mission to thaw relations with PM Giorgia Meloni and Pope Leo, subjects of TrumpвҖҷs abuse and bizarre AI posts.
  • Speaking of whichвҖҰTrumpsharedan image of himself, Rubio, JD Vance, and Doug Burgum, all shirtless, with a bikini-clad woman in the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool. In the real world, the construction site for TrumpвҖҷs renovation of the pool was sprayed with graffiti referencing ComeyвҖҷs вҖң86 47вҖқ message.
  • True colors:Trump alsoposteda real picture of House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries, accompanied by racist abuse. Jeffriesfiredback.
  • Giuliani hospitalized:Former New York Mayor turned Trump henchman Rudy Giuliani, 81, was on Sunday in critical condition in a Florida hospital, his spokespersonsaid.

According to a debunked if persistent urban myth, the profile of which prominent historical figure is carved into the back of the head of the Abraham Lincoln statue at the Lincoln Memorial in Washington?

Keep your eyes out for the answer below!

  • рҹҮөрҹҮё DonвҖҷt forget Gaza:Israelextendedits detention of two members of an aid flotilla, as their lawyer said theyвҖҷve suffered beatings by Israeli authorities. Meanwhile, Israeli National Security Minister (and sociopath?) Itamar Ben-Gvir celebrated his birthday withcakesdecorated with nooses and guns: a reference to the recent law imposing the death penalty for Palestinians only.
  • рҹҮәрҹҮҰрҹҮ·рҹҮә Drone wave:Ukrainelaunchedattacks against Russia, hitting Baltic naval and oil facilities. A Kremlin spokesperson said such strikes could cause global oil prices affected by the US-Israel-Iran war to rise yet further.
  • рҹҮҝрҹҮҰ Virus alert:Three are dead and at least three are in intensive care in asuspected outbreakof the rodent-spread hantavirus on a cruise ship in the Atlantic. Victims were taken to South Africa, where authorities were checking for further cases.
  • рҹҮІрҹҮұ Critic taken:A former minister turned critic of the Mali junta was abducted by armed and hooded men, APreported. Mountaga Tall, a former education minister, now leads a party opposed to the military government, which has been attacked by militant groups.


Not sure what kind of restaurants Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche eats at, where he says you have to show ID to enter. Maybe theyвҖҷre next door to wherever Donald Trump buys groceries.

  • On

    CNNвҖҷs вҖҳState of the Union,вҖҷformer Massachusetts congressmanBarney Frank, who has entered hospice care, continued publicity for a book he may not see published. A leading campaigner for LGBTQ+ rights, Frank, 86, now thinks Democrats have shifted too far left too quickly on the вҖңTвҖқ part of that acronym. Nor is he much of a fan of Graham Platner, the insurgent progressive who beat Governor Janet Mills in the Maine Senate primary. According to Frank, Platner вҖңactually shares with Trump this capacity toward making the most out of the anger that people feel.вҖқ

  • Former Fox host

    Jeannine Pirrois both the US attorney for the District of Columbia and the owner of one of the best rude nicknames in politics. On Sunday, вҖңJudge Box of WineвҖқspoke to Jake Tapperof CNN about the shooting at the White House CorrespondentsвҖҷ Dinner in DC last weekend. Confirming it was the suspect who shot a Secret Service agent saved by his bulletproof vest, Pirro also seemed inadvertently to confirm the shooterвҖҷs manifesto use of the phrase вҖңa pedophile rapist and traitorвҖқ was a reference to Donald Trump.

  • On

    CBSвҖҷs вҖҳFace the Nation,вҖҷSenator Raphael Warnockof Georgia lamented the effective death of the Voting Rights Act of 1965, as confirmed this week by the right-wing Supreme Court in a ruling that triggered action in Louisiana to wipe out Black congressional seats. вҖңLetвҖҷs be clear,вҖқ said the senator, also a pastor at thechurchwhere Martin Luther King, Jr. preached. вҖңWhat happened this week is nothing less than a massive and devastating blow, not only to our democracy, but particularly to people of color in the South.вҖқ
  • Tuesday, May 5:Ohio and Indiana arescheduledto hold primary elections. Plus, EU finance ministers are scheduled to meet to discuss the Iran War. PanamaвҖҷs President JosГ© RaГәl Mulino issetto host Israeli President Isaac Herzog.
  • Wednesday, May 6:Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick issetfor a voluntary interview by the House Oversight Committee in its Jeffrey Epstein investigation.
  • Thursday, May 7:The US Court of Appeals for DCwillconsider the PentagonвҖҷs bid to punish Senator Mark Kelly for telling troops to resist illegal orders.
  • Friday, May 8:Right-wing populist Laura Fernandez issetto take office as Costa RicaвҖҷs president.


ZeteoвҖҷs Martin Pengelly contributed to this newsletter.

item.title: Debunking 5 Myths A Pro-Israel Lobby Group Is Spreading About the US-Israel Relationship
item.description: Almost everyone, at this point, has heard of AIPAC, it seems. The Israel-first lobby has made itself notorious through its hardline stance вҖ“ and has become an increasingly toxic name in American politics, particularly on the Democratic side of the aisle. But thatвҖҷs where AIPACвҖҷs lesser-knownвҖҰ
item.author: Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
Almost everyone, at this point, has heard of AIPAC, it seems. The Israel-first lobby has made itself notorious through its hardline stance вҖ“ and has become an increasingly toxic name in American politics, particularly on the Democratic side of the aisle. But thatвҖҷs where AIPACвҖҷs lesser-known counterpart, Democratic Majority for Israel (DMFI), hopes to fill the gap being created as Democrats forswear AIPAC funding.

Founded in 2019 in the wake of Donald TrumpвҖҷs election and dismantling of the US-Iran deal negotiated by the Obama administration вҖ“ both of which many Democrats saw as having been supported by AIPAC вҖ“ DMFI sends a softer message: It supports a two-state solution for Israel-Palestine. And it is sometimes critical of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu. But make no mistake вҖ“ it is here to shore up the flagging (one might say, evaporating) support for Israel among Democrats, through lobbying and, via its sister organization DMFI PAC, with political spending that totals over $25 million since its founding. As DMFI PACвҖҷs webpage explains, the organization вҖңworks to elect pro-Israel Democrats up and down the ballot and defeat those few anti-Israel Democratic candidatesвҖқ вҖ“ a description it rounds off by explaining that вҖңbeing pro-Israel isnвҖҷt only good policy, itвҖҷs also good politics.вҖқ ThatвҖҷs a catchphrase that will surely be familiar to long-time AIPAC watchers. And if there was any remaining doubt, itвҖҷs worth noting that not only are some of DMFI PACвҖҷs biggest contributors are also top contributors to AIPAC, but also that DMFI and AIPAC often back the same candidates.

AIPAC lists on its website a series of supposed benefits the US receives as a result of its relationship with Israel вҖ“ a series of myths that we responded to with the actual facts here.

So, last month, when DMFI posted its arguments for вҖңWhy the US-Israel Alliance Matters for Democrats,вҖқ we felt it was only fair to give its list the same treatment. Here are its assertions вҖ“ and why they are so, so wrong:

вҖңDemocratic voters support maintaining the US-Israel alliance. Polling commissioned byвҖҰ DMFI PAC, late last year shows that a majority of Democrats favor maintaining a strong US-Israel relationship.вҖқ

The Facts

Whatever the results of a poll by an organization explicitly dedicated to maintaining Democratic Party support for Israel may claim (and DMFI has not released the full data, or even the question it asked to obtain this result), multiple independent polls in recent years have shown cratering US public support for Israel, particularly among Democrats. For instance, a March 2026 NBC News Poll found that just 13% of Democrats hold a positive view of Israel, with 57% seeing it in a negative light. An October 2025 Pew Poll found that 77% of Democrats have an unfavorable view of the government of Israel. A February 2026 Gallup Poll found that a supermajority of Democrats (65%) have greater sympathies for Palestinians compared to those who favor Israel (17%). And, most recently, an April 7, 2026, Pew Poll shows that 80% of Democrats and Democratic-leaning independents view Israel negatively.

DMFI should release the full poll it conducted, including the precise phrasing of its question. Without that, weвҖҷre marking this one a complete myth.

вҖңThe US-Israel alliance is independent of any single administration or conflict. Israel maintains the ability to defend itself by itself and strengthens regional deterrence without requiring permanent US troop deployments.вҖқ

The Facts

The first part of this statement isnвҖҷt actually a myth; itвҖҷs a problem. No US relationship with any country should exist in a vacuum, with no regard to US politics or world events. As Thomas Jefferson said, the US should not enter вҖңentangling alliancesвҖқ вҖ“ and what could be more entangling of an alliance than one that is entirely independent of reality? As we have seen with back-to-back administrations that have pursued an approach of unconditional support for Israel, the absence of any willingness to question the relationship is a harm to the US, not a reason for it to matter to Democrats.

When it comes to IsraelвҖҷs ability to defend itself вҖ“ and to do so вҖңwithout requiring permanent US troop deploymentsвҖқ вҖ“ would DMFI like to put this to the test? The US provides Israel with billions of dollars every year in taxpayer funding to support its military, and is also almost the sole source of IsraelвҖҷs fighter jet fleet and most of its munitions. In recent months, the US has deployed multiple naval vessels equipped with missile defense systems to protect Israel from incoming Iranian ballistic missiles вҖ“ firing several yearsвҖҷ worth of US interceptor production to defend Israel. Meanwhile, US troop deployments around the Middle East вҖ“ many of which seem greatly intended to respond to conflicts generated relative to Israel вҖ“ certainly seem permanent.

вҖңUS security assistance to Israel is worth the investment, comparable to what the US invests in other critical partners such as South Korea, Japan, and Ukraine. Unlike those allies, however, Israel does not require a permanent US troop presence.вҖқ

The Facts

On this one, DMFI is simply вҖ“ and profoundly вҖ“ factually incorrect. The US provides Israel with $3.8 billion a year in security assistance вҖ“ but Korea and Japan pay the US for its role in their defense. In fact, South Korea pays the United States $1.14 billion annually in host nation cost-sharing under the 2024 Special Measures Agreement, while under the US-Japan Host Nation Agreement, Japan pays $1.3 billion a year. Korea and Japan also spend their own money when buying US weapons вҖ“ rather than relying, as Israel does, on handouts from the US taxpayer. And while it is true that Korea and Japan host extensive US bases (which, again, they pay for), DMFI is also wrong that Israel does not require a permanent US presence: a recent presentation by the US Defense Department confirmed the presence of a long-rumored US base in Israel at Site 512, and since DMFI made their post, Israel has explicitly invited the US to relocate much of their regional presence to bases in the country.

вҖңPreserving the alliance advances long-term US interests. Israel provides unique capabilities, including missile defense systems, cyber expertise, regional intelligence, and operational experience, that strengthen American security and reduce the likelihood that the US would face future crises without capable partners.вҖқ

The Facts:

Are those вҖңunique capabilitiesвҖқ Israel provides, such as missile defense systems, the same Iron Dome, Arrow, and DavidвҖҷs Sling systems that the US itself subsidizes to the tune of billions of dollars? And have any of those systems ever been used to help defend the US (aside from our own forces who are based in Israel)? Moreover, given the conflict with Iran, how can anyone argue that the partnership with Israel вҖңreduces the likelihood that the US would face future crisesвҖқ вҖ“ with or without capable partners? The US-Israel relationship is a direct cause of the crises we currently face, and Americans вҖ“ certainly including an overwhelming majority of Democrats вҖ“ would rather we not face them at all, rather than enter them at the behest of Israel.

HereвҖҷs a fact: DMFI faces an unenviable challenge. Its mission these days is to defend the indefensible to a swiftly thinning audience. Its greatest tool in this effort remains the war-chest of DMFI PAC and the constellation of dark money organizations that align with it. But when it comes to rational persuasion, all the group can offer are unfounded assertions, faulty logic, and, in at least one instance above, outright falsehoods.

If you visit the DMFI PAC webpage, you will find, prominently displayed, a quote from Democratic House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries: вҖңI am grateful for the support of DMFI PAC and look forward to continuing to advance the unbreakable US-Israel bond for the good of the Middle East and the world.вҖқ For as long as Democratic leaders associate themselves with AIPAC, as well as an organization that, for all intents and purposes, functions as an AIPAC cut-out, the partyвҖҷs credibility will be the greatest myth of all.

Josh Paul is the co-founder of A New Policy, which was founded in October 2024 with the goal of reshaping US policies toward Israel and Palestine and bringing them in line with AmericaвҖҷs interests. Paul resigned from the Biden administration over its policy of unconditional support for Israel's assault on Gaza.

Robert McDonald is the senior legislative analyst at A New Policy.

Check out more from Zeteo:

Thank you for being a paid subscriber to Zeteo, your support helps keep this media movement alive. If youвҖҷd like to wear your support, donвҖҷt forget to check out

For any issues with your subscription, please email us at info@zeteonews.com.

item.title: Memo to Dems: Fight Fire With Fire!
item.description: The ultra-reactionary majority on the Supreme Court has finished gutting the Voting Rights Act. In Louisiana v. Callais , Justice Samuel Alito struck down the remainder of Section 2 of that law, reversing a lower-court decision that required Louisiana to draw two majority-minority districts, toвҖҰ
item.author: Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
The ultra-reactionary majority on the Supreme Court has finished gutting the Voting Rights Act. In Louisiana v. Callais, Justice Samuel Alito struck down the remainder of Section 2 of that law, reversing a lower-court decision that required Louisiana to draw two majority-minority districts, to ensure its African American population had representation. As Adam Serwer explains in the Atlantic, AlitoвҖҷs argument rests on constitutional prohibitions against racism in the 14th and 15th amendments. A requirement to ensure Black representation is actually an вҖңunconstitutional racial gerrymander,вҖқ Alito wrote.

The Republican governor of Louisiana, Jeff Landry, immediately halted primary elections already in progress so he could draw new maps disenfranchising as many Black residents as possible. The rest of the Republican Party is following suit. They might easily net 13 additional seats just in the South, eliminating a huge chunk of the Congressional Black Caucus. At the state level, it will be a bloodbath for Black representation, just as it was in the 1890s when RepublicansвҖҷ political ancestors in the Ku Klux Klan finished consolidating Jim Crow tyranny.

Callais is so tendentious, itвҖҷs kind of morbidly fascinating.

In a sense, Alito has come up with an entirely new theory of constitutional interpretation that we might call вҖңanti-originalism,вҖқ in which constitutional provisions are held to mean the opposite of what the people who wrote them said they meant. In terms of following the obvious and well-documented intentions of constitutional fathers like John Bingham and William Stewart, it is exactly like arguing that the preamble of the Constitution intends to create the worst possible union, prevent all justice, leave the community open to attack, and place вҖңwe the peopleвҖқ under the permanent subjugation of the British crown.

ItвҖҷs one more ruling to add to the pile of Chief Justice John RobertsвҖҷs crimes against the Constitution, law, and plain common decency вҖ“ much worse than the oppressions that kicked off the American Revolution in the first place, incidentally вҖ“ a record that justifies a wholesale restructuring of the Supreme Court to restore freedom and democracy to the United States.

But in the meantime, Democrats should fight fire with fire. ItвҖҷs time to redistrict every last possible Republican out of office. ItвҖҷs time to unleash the baconmanders.

For years, election nerds have been drawing up the most outrageous possible gerrymanders to favor Democrats. Because liberals tend to be concentrated in cities, such maps typically involve cutting big metro areas like Chicago or New York City into narrow slices that then stretch all the way across the state, like rashers of bacon. This Illinois map, for instance, would eliminate three Republican seats in part by creating a district that stretches all the way from Chicago to Cairo, in the very southern tip of the state.

The argument for doing this is game theory 101. Since 2010, Republicans have conducted an utterly ruthless campaign of gerrymandering, which has succeeded wildly. In the 2012 election, for instance, Democrats got more votes for the House of Representatives, but Republicans kept a 33-seat majority because of their prior cheating. (Imagine what President Barack Obama might have accomplished with another trifecta from 2013-14.) In the 2018 election in Wisconsin, Democrats won the popular vote for the state assembly by more than eight points, but Republicans won almost two-thirds of the seats. That is arguably more aggressive cheating than anything Victor OrbГЎn ever did in Hungary.

This happened in part because Democrats did not immediately fight back in states they controlled. In the prisonerвҖҷs dilemma, if you defect and the other side does not, then defecting is the smart strategy. More generally, in any competition, if you can win by cheating, people will tend to do it. Just look at professional sports.

If cheating cannot be prohibited, then the rational response is retaliation. If Republicans gerrymander, Democrats should counter-gerrymander, thus not only restoring a semblance of fairness to national elections but also proving that cheating is not a guaranteed way to win. As Greg Sargent writes at The New Republic, analysis from Fair Fight Action shows that if Democrats gerrymander their remaining blue states, they might net 10 seats, and if they win a big victory in the upcoming midterms (which seems likely) and then gerrymander swing states as Republicans did in 2011, they might net 22 seats. That would more or less counteract RepublicansвҖҷ plans for Jim Crow II.

To be fair, Democrats have been fighting back. California and Virginia passed gerrymanders in response to Donald Trump demanding that Texas do a highly unusual mid-decade gerrymander. But the California map was not nearly as aggressive as it might have been, and the first round of retaliation did not take. Republicans responded to a taste of their own medicine by spitefully doubling down. Clearly, theyвҖҷll need another, larger dose.

And that illustrates a critical point: the Republican Party is not some coolly rational participant in a social science experiment. We are dealing with a pack of depraved monsters who love lying, cheating, and stealing for their own sakes. None but a party of utter moral degenerates could possibly nominate someone like Trump for president. They will keep cheating until it costs them many elections in a row, or they are forced to stop.

Make no mistake, a nationwide gerrymander-off is far from ideal. An election in which most states send single-party delegations that might as well have been elected by a statewide vote makes a hash of the whole idea of the House of Representatives, which is supposed to provide some semblance of local control. Ideally, such a goofy situation would open the door to some kind of dГ©tente in which fair maps could be mandated by federal law. Or better yet, representatives could be elected in multi-member districts with ranked choice voting (as advocated by Fair Vote), which would not only make gerrymandering basically impossible, but provide representation to minority party voters in red and blue states.

But the only way that is going to happen is if Democrats win national power, break the illegitimate, traitorous, racist majority on the Supreme Court, and restore fair elections by force. Republicans will not see reason unless they are strapped into the Ludovico Device and made to see.

Ryan Cooper is a senior editor at the American Prospect, and the author of вҖҳHow Are You Going to Pay for That?: Smart Answers to the Dumbest Question in Politics.вҖҷ

item.title: Israel Starves a Prominent Journalist in Prison, Commits 'Piracy' at Sea, and Kills More Palestinians in Gaza
item.description: A quick note: You were auto-subscribed to receive вҖҳThis Week in Palestine.вҖҷ We hope you find this project worth reading every week. But if youвҖҷd like to unsubscribe from this newsletter but still receive other Zeteo content, go to вҖҳ Its war in Lebanon, and its continued bombing in Gaza вҖ“ despiteвҖҰ
item.author: This Week in Palestine | Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
A quick note:

You were auto-subscribed to receive вҖҳThis Week in Palestine.вҖҷ We hope you find this project worth reading every week. But if youвҖҷd like to unsubscribe from this newsletter but still receive other Zeteo content, go to вҖҳ

Its war in Lebanon, and its continued bombing in Gaza вҖ“ despite so-called вҖңceasefiresвҖқ in place вҖ“ did not stop Israel from conducting violent raids in the occupied West Bank and conducting вҖңpiracyвҖқ at sea.

Settler violence continued unabated, and the condition of a prominent journalist who had been held by Israel without trial or charge for a year was more proof of the torture and abuse Palestinians go through in Israeli prisons.

It was yet another troubling week in Palestine:

Palestinians in central GazaвҖҷs Deir Al-Balah voted in the first elections in two decades. It was mostly a symbolic vote meant to show that Gaza and the West Bank, where elections were also being held, cannot be separated politically, the Palestinian Authority said. Hamas did not formally field candidates, and turnout was low in Deir Al-Balah because, as Al JazeeraвҖҷs Hind Khoudary noted, people were вҖңbusy with surviving.вҖқ

Israeli forces killed at least four Palestinians across Gaza, including a 40-year-old woman who was shot and killed by Israeli troops, according to medical officials who spoke with Reuters.

The death toll in Gaza since the so-called ceasefire began more than six months ago has topped 820. Israel has killed more than 72,600 Palestinians since the genocide began.

Israeli forces carried out large-scale raids across the West Bank. In Qalandia refugee camp, troops searched, evicted, and occupied at least a dozen homes. Israeli forces arrested dozens of Palestinians living in the camp.
Israeli troops similarly raided Kafr Aqab and al-Ram, towns near Jerusalem, where they arrested two recently released prisoners, Majd and Ahmed Abu Gharbiya. At the same time, Israeli authorities issued demolition notices for dozens of homes, shops, and businesses in those towns.

IsraelвҖҷs genocide has left at least 96% of GazaвҖҷs farmland and critical agricultural infrastructure destroyed or inaccessible, the aid group Mercy Corps found. The finding is especially concerning, considering Israel continues to severely restrict aid entering the Strip.

Even if IsraelвҖҷs strikes were to end and Palestinians were allowed to access the land, Mercy Corps noted that вҖңrecovery will be severely constrained by contamination, unexploded ordnance, and ongoing restrictions on essential inputs and materials.вҖқ

Israel intercepted more than 20 boats part of the Global Sumud Flotilla and abducted nearly 200 activists, humanitarians, and journalists on board, including Zeteo contributor Alex Colston, in what organizers described as вҖңpiracy.вҖқ

All but two of the activists were eventually released in Greece. They reported being beaten and denied adequate food and water while held by Israel. Israel took two activists вҖ“ Saif Abukeshek, a Spanish and Swedish national of Palestinian origin, and Thiago ГҒvila, a Brazilian national вҖ“ hostage and said it was transporting them to Israel for questioning.

Unrecognizable, veteran Palestinian journalist Ali Samoudi was released from Israeli prison after being held in so-called вҖңadministrative detentionвҖқ for a year.

Samoudi said that when he was detained, he weighed about 264 pounds and now weighs around 132 pounds. вҖңThe food is very bad. Even a cat would not eat what they eat,вҖқ said Samoudi, who was also shot when Israeli forces killed Al JazeeraвҖҷs Shireen Abu Akleh in 2022. Samoudi was featured in ZeteoвҖҷs documentary about the slain journalist.

Israeli prisons are a вҖңgraveyard for the living,вҖқ Samoudi said.

Armed Israeli settlers launched a wave of attacks on Palestinians across the occupied West Bank, storming and opening fire on homes near Hebron, assaulting a Palestinian man in Deir Dibwan and stealing his tractor, blocking farmers from their land and attacking a man in Masafer Yatta, and severely beating two young men near Tubas.

According to Haaretz, the Israeli militaryвҖҷs head of Central Command recently warned that settler violence вҖ“ which he described as вҖңJewish terrorismвҖқ вҖ“ risks triggering a Palestinian uprising: вҖңAt some point this could spill over, and when it does, itвҖҷs a major thing.вҖқ

ZeteoвҖҷs Layla Nayfeh contributed to this article.

Miss last weekвҖҷs edition? Catch up here. Plus, check out more recent essays and interviews from Zeteo:

If you enjoy Zeteo, share it with your friends and earn rewards when they subscribe.

item.title: DonвҖҷt Listen to Trump: His Iran War Still IsnвҖҷt Over and Still IsnвҖҷt Legal
item.description: The Iran war is over! At least, it is according to President Donald Trump, who just declared Iran вҖңhostilitiesвҖқ have been вҖңterminated.вҖқ Before you run to Times Square to grab the nearest sailor or nurse , IвҖҷve got bad news вҖ“ Trump is lying. The Iran war is not over by any legal or physicalвҖҰ
item.author: Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
The Iran war is over! At least, it is according to President Donald Trump, who just declared Iran вҖңhostilitiesвҖқ have been вҖңterminated.вҖқ Before you run to Times Square to grab the nearest sailor or nurse, IвҖҷve got bad news вҖ“ Trump is lying. The Iran war is not over by any legal or physical standard. With the Strait of Hormuz still shut, the US Navy still trying to enforce a blockade, thousands of extra troops still deployed to the region, and Trump still threatening to bomb Iran again, why is he suddenly trying to declare вҖңmission accomplished?вҖқ

The answer is that Friday marked a special anniversary for congressional Republicans hoping to disassociate themselves from a disastrous war they have so far largely endorsed: 60 days from March 2, when Trump formally notified Congress of his war on Iran.

The so-called вҖң60-day clockвҖқ comes from the 1973 War Powers Resolution (WPR), a bill passed to prevent a repeat of Vietnam, which saw successive US presidents drag the country into a foreign quagmire without seeking approval from Congress, the only branch of government with the constitutional authority to declare war. The WPR stipulates that if the president enters вҖңhostilitiesвҖқ following an attack on the United States, he can continue operations for up to 60 days before securing congressional authorization required under the Constitution. The term вҖңhostilitiesвҖқ used in the law refers both to active combat and situations where troops are at risk of coming under fire.

The WPR has been used and abused by both parties, with Republicans now treating it like a 60-day hall pass for Trump. On the contrary, says Eric Eikenberry, government relations director at Win Without War: вҖңThe вҖҳ60-day clockвҖҷ in the WPR is not congressional permission for the president to wage any war they like for two months.вҖқ

Frustrated Republicans like Senators Susan Collins and Thom Tillis have seized on the WPRвҖҷs 60-day deadline in the hope of prodding the administration to wrap up a war that Trump increasingly appears emotionally and intellectually incapable of ending. Other Republicans have also pointed to a provision of the WPR that allows the president to extend a war for 30 days, to claim that Trump still has a month of slack, while Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth suggested on Thursday that the current ceasefire means a pause in the 60-day countdown.

Whatever math you use, it doesnвҖҷt really matter: TrumpвҖҷs Iran war has been illegal since day one.

Per the WPR, the president can only go to war without congressional authorization in the event of an attack вҖңupon the United States, its territories or possessions, or its armed forces.вҖқ In this case, Trump clearly shot first. And, as TrumpвҖҷs former National Counterterrorism Center Director Joe Kent attested when he resigned in protest over this war, Iran posed no imminent threat to US forces. The strongest evidence to the contrary, and perhaps clearest window into the decision-making behind this war, comes from White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt: вҖңThis was a feeling the president had based on facts.вҖқ

TrumpвҖҷs faux-peace announcement came via a letter to the Senate claiming hostilities were вҖңterminatedвҖқ because вҖңthere has been no exchange of fire between the United States and Iran since April 7, 2026,вҖқ when the temporary ceasefire went into effect. There are a few problems here: First, a US Navy destroyer did fire on a blockade-running Iranian cargo ship just two weeks ago. Even if there truly had been no вҖңexchange of fire,вҖқ TrumpвҖҷs attempted blockade of Iran is very much an act of war under US and international law. On top of that, US troops are absolutely still engaged in вҖңhostilitiesвҖқ as covered by the WPR, given TrumpвҖҷs threats to resume bombing any day.

Senator Collins, a proponent of the legally redundant 60-day deadline, fired a warning shot on Thursday when she joined Democrats in a vote attempting to end the war, the first Republican to do so other than Senator Rand Paul.

Eikenberry calls TrumpвҖҷs termination of hostilities letter вҖңthe thinnest of gruelвҖқ but notes вҖңthat heвҖҷs offering it at allвҖҰ is a sign of his growing recognition that a handful of Senate Republicans have increasingly little appetite for this war. They can go a long way toward deciding whether this catastrophe continues, or whether Trump is cornered into truly ending it soon.вҖқ

Congressional Republicans and the White House know the war is unpopular and that theyвҖҷre going to take the blame for cost-of-living-exploding Iranflation if they donвҖҷt end the war ASAP вҖ“ though itвҖҷs possible ASAP may not be fast enough to repair the US economy by the time of the midterms.

Asked on Friday why he isnвҖҷt seeking congressional approval for his Iran war, Trump called it вҖңunconstitutional,вҖқ arguing, вҖңNobodyвҖҷs ever asked for it before, nobodyвҖҷs ever used it before вҖ“ why should we be different?вҖқ Though TrumpвҖҷs gripe isnвҖҷt factually correct, he sounds a lot like Barack Obama, whose lawyers argued the WPRвҖҷs 60-day countdown was вҖңunconstitutionalвҖқ during his ill-conceived Libya bombing campaign in 2011. And unfortunately, Trump does have a point about presidents frequently bypassing congressional authorization.

Yes, Trump has launched unauthorized wars or strikes against Iran, Ecuador, Venezuela, Nigeria, and hapless fishermen in the Caribbean and Eastern Pacific, but the only precedents heвҖҷs really breaking are geographical, not legal. Joe Biden maintained a months-long war against YemenвҖҷs Houthis without congressional authorization, under increasingly flimsy justifications of self-defense. Obama also decided to interpret the 2001 Authorization for the Use of Military Force вҖ“ intended to go after entities responsible for 9/11 вҖ“ so broadly as to make it nearly meaningless, attacking groups like Al-Shabab in Somalia or ISIS in Syria, which simply didnвҖҷt exist in 2001.

Since the 1973 War Powers Resolution, presidents have launched dozens of military operations without congressional approval or a plausible self-defense justification. How do they get away with it? Congress is happy to let them. Our representatives willingly abdicating their most sacred power вҖ“ voting on matters of war and peace вҖ“ might make the framers roll over in their grave, but itвҖҷs a logical choice. The lesson many drew from the 2003 invasion of Iraq, which was authorized by Congress, was not to avoid pointless wars but just to avoid voting on them, so as not to take the blame if things went south.

Ending AmericaвҖҷs multiplying wars and reigning in the imperial presidency will require pressuring our representatives вҖ“ or electing new ones вҖ“ until we have a Congress willing to do its job.

Harrison Mann is a former US Army major and executive officer of the Defense Intelligence AgencyвҖҷs Middle East/Africa Regional Center who resigned in protest of his officeвҖҷs support for IsraelвҖҷs war in Gaza under the Biden administration. He is currently with the group Win Without War and recently became a Zeteo contributor.

Check out more from Zeteo:

If you enjoy Zeteo, share it with your friends and earn rewards when they subscribe.

item.title: рҹҮ®рҹҮ·рҹҮәрҹҮё Is Iran Trying to 'Jimmy Carter' Donald Trump?
item.description: On this day in 2003, George W. Bush gave his now-notorious вҖңMission AccomplishedвҖқ speech, declaring вҖңmajor combat operations in Iraq have endedвҖқ on board the USS Abraham Lincoln. Spoiler alert: they hadnвҖҷt ended! Still, wouldnвҖҷt it be great if Donald Trump did his own вҖңMission AccomplishedвҖқ speechвҖҰ
item.author: First Draft | Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
On this day in 2003, George W. Bush gave his now-notorious вҖңMission AccomplishedвҖқ speech, declaring вҖңmajor combat operations in Iraq have endedвҖқ on board the USS Abraham Lincoln. Spoiler alert: they hadnвҖҷt ended! Still, wouldnвҖҷt it be great if Donald Trump did his own вҖңMission AccomplishedвҖқ speech now as a way of ending his endless war in Iran?

Happy Friday, all. ItвҖҷs Swin and Andrew again. If youвҖҷre living in America right now and you still doubt youвҖҷre living in an empire in decline, remind yourself that our disastrously unpopular, mentally decayed leader is still very badly losing a horrifying foreign war that he started, and yet all he seems to want to talk about is his вҖңballroomвҖқ and punishing a former host of вҖҳThe Man Show.вҖҷ Now, letвҖҷs talk more about that war, and the lies that President Donald Trump continues to peddle to the American people about his negotiating position, even as his own intelligence officials quietly warn of a much grimmer truth.

In todayвҖҷs вҖҳFirst Draft,вҖҷ Iran prepares a familiar fate for Trump, Louisiana leads the way for the GOP after the death of the Voting Rights Act, and the public doesnвҖҷt believe Donald should get his White House ballroom in order to feel safe.

Donald Trump likes to claim he holds all the вҖңcardsвҖқ and is in вҖңno rushвҖқ to make a deal to end the illegal war in Iran he started earlier this year. He claims the Iranian government is вҖңbeggingвҖқ him to end the war and that the US has вҖңallвҖқ the leverage in negotiations.

That would be news to various US officials, some of whom have told the president and other senior administration personnel that the Iranians, in fact, have a ton of leverage вҖ“ to a degree that could be disastrous for Trump and Republicans in the looming midterm elections.

In recent weeks, US officials have privately discussed classified intelligence assessments suggesting that the government in Tehran isnвҖҷt close to breaking, even after two months of war and a sustained economic assault, two administration officials and two other sources briefed on the matter tell us. Administration officials, citing US intel, have also warned that senior Iranian officials are keenly aware that if the war drags on much longer, it could further damage Trump and the Republican PartyвҖҷs chances at the polls in November.

вҖңThey know they can Carter him,вҖқ a senior administration official familiar with the intelligence and internal deliberations says, referring to how the Iranian hostage crisis helped tank Jimmy CarterвҖҷs presidency by the time of the 1980 US election.

And if Tehran doesnвҖҷt get a deal to end the conflict that it sees as sufficiently favorable вҖ“ a scenario that would invariably include concessions that the White House has said cross TrumpвҖҷs вҖңred linesвҖқ вҖ“ US intelligence officials believe the Iranians would likely be able to keep fighting into the end of the year at least, the sources add.

Much of this has been directly briefed to Trump, who vacillates wildly between scrounging for face-saving off-ramps and stressing his desire to sharply escalate his Middle East war, and who continues to spin to the American public that he has all the leverage and that he is the one with time on his side during these supposed peace talks.

If you believe that, we have a monorail to sell you.

Two of our sources say several senior administration officials have talked to Trump about this intel specifically because they are trying to nudge him in the direction of вҖңdeclaring victoryвҖқ and pulling the plug on the war.

вҖңThis thing needs to end,вҖқ one White House official says, adding that the president вҖңneeds to understandвҖқ that the Iranian regime вҖңhas its own cardsвҖқ вҖ“ and one is the US midterm elections.

Indeed, many Republican strategists and politicians believe TrumpвҖҷs Iran war has already helped doom their party come NovemberвҖҷs midterms вҖ“ which Trump himself has said will determine the fate of the rest of his domestic and international agenda. Since the start of the war, Iran has effectively closed the Strait of Hormuz, cutting off the flow of oil, spiking energy prices, and throttling the global economy. Trump has responded by imposing a blockade against Iran. Following news that Trump is planning for a prolonged naval chokehold вҖ“ and is considering a new wave of military strikes вҖ“ oil prices have surged even higher.

If $4 gas was already a political liability for the GOP, parts of the country are now paying around $5 a gallon. American and international economic pain (not to mention body counts) will only spike higher if Trump once again chooses to escalate. For its part, the Iranian government is promising вҖң long and painfulвҖқ retaliation, should the US launch new attacks.

In response to a detailed comment request about this reporting, White House spokeswoman Olivia Wales instead sent us a boilerplate statement claiming that вҖңthe United States met or surpassed all of our clearly stated military objectives in Operation Epic Fury,вҖқ that the American blockade вҖңis working perfectly,вҖқ and that due to TrumpвҖҷs warmongering, вҖңAmericans are already safer for it.вҖқ

Americans, of course, do not think this. And the Republican elite, who have become so subsumed into TrumpвҖҷs violent cult of personality that they canвҖҷt meaningfully break from him, know their fortunes in this and other election cycles are inextricably tied to his approval rating. Several notable pollsters have clocked their worst numbers yet for The Donald вҖ“ and two Trump advisers tell us that some senior White House staffers privately predict the numbers will sink even lower soon.

It appears there are perhaps a few self-aware souls in the White House who understand that being a fussy wannabe god-emperor вҖ” who wonвҖҷt shut up about his decadent вҖңballroom,вҖқ who keeps getting his ass handed to him by the Iranians, who isnвҖҷt making any AmericanвҖҷs life better or cheaper, and who keeps reveling in the pornography of mass violence вҖ” is not necessarily a recipe for a political partyвҖҷs sustained electoral success.

Shame.

Death tolls:

Iran: Iranian state media on Sunday reported, citing officials, that US-Israeli attacks have killed 3,468 people.

Lebanon: Israel has killed more than 2,586 people, including at least 177 children, in Lebanon, according to the countryвҖҷs Health Ministry.

US troops: At least 13 US troops have died in incidents related to the war.

Israel: At least 35 people, including 12 Israeli soldiers, have been killed, according to officials.

And in more war newsвҖҰ

вҖҳLong and painfulвҖҷ: Mojtaba Khamenei, the new Supreme Leader, said the US had no role in the Persian Gulf, vowing Iran would control the Strait of Hormuz and protect nuclear assets. A senior Revolutionary Guards leader promised вҖңlong and painful strikesвҖқ if the US attacks again.

Pete pounded: Pete Hegseth testified in the Senate. Democrat Jack Reed attacked, slamming the secretary of defense for вҖңdangerous statementsвҖқ and bad advice to Trump. Hegseth blamed critics like Reed for polls that show the public does not support the war.

Soccer diplomacy: FIFA chief Gianni Infantino said Iran would play World Cup matches on US soil this summer. Trump said, вҖңIf Gianni said it, IвҖҷm okayвҖқ with it too.

What вҖҳceasefireвҖҷ?: Israel killed more than two dozen people in Lebanon yesterday in repeated strikes, despite the so-called вҖңceasefire.вҖқ

The likely effects of the Trump Supreme CourtвҖҷs gutting of Voting Rights Act protections for racial minorities were made plain on Thursday, when LouisianaвҖҷs Republican governor, Jeff Landry, suspended US House primaries so new districts could be drawn вҖ“ a move meant to ensure a glut of GOP seats and set to be followed by Republicans across southern states.

вҖңThe best way to end race-based discrimination is to stop making decisions based on race,вҖқ Landry said, in a predictably disingenuous statement. вҖңHere in Louisiana, weвҖҷre proud to lead the nation on this charge. Allowing elections to proceed under an unconstitutional map would undermine the integrity of our system and violate the rights of our voters.вҖқ

House primaries had been scheduled for May 16. By executive order, Landry delayed them вҖңuntil July 15вҖҰ or until such time as determined by the legislature,вҖқ so the work of eliminating Black majority districts could begin. Primaries for other offices will go ahead as planned.

Though Justice Samuel AlitoвҖҷs majority opinion in Louisiana v. Callais says it strikes down unconstitutional racial gerrymanders, it in fact destroys vital protections enshrined under Lyndon B. Johnson in 1965, and has since been used to ensure Black and brown representation in Congress.

The NAACP called the Wednesday ruling вҖңa license to silence Black voters and rig the voting system,вҖқ while the Georgia voting rights activist and former state representative Stacey Abrams wrote that the Court вҖңall but killed the law that helped kill Jim Crow.вҖқ

On Thursday, Landry started work to revive the corpse.

Ballroom blitzed: The Washington Post, ABC News, and Ipsos found that 56% of Americans oppose Donald TrumpвҖҷs attempt to build a White House ballroom, which the president claims to need for security reasons.

New charges: TrumpвҖҷs DOJ wants to indict James Comey for allegedly leaking classified information, Bloomberg reported. The former FBI director was indicted this week for threatening the president with a message made from seashells, a case MS NOW said accelerated after AG Pam Bondi was fired last month.

Shutdown over: The Department of Homeland Security is mostly funded after 76 days. The House passed a Senate deal leaving out ICE and Border Patrol, whose berserk violence Democrats oppose, and the GOP will now fund by party line vote.

Maine chance: Governor Janet Mills dropped out of the Democratic Senate primary without endorsing progressive Graham Platner, a month from election day. Establishment figures who backed Mills started playing a blame game.

Bets off: The Senate unanimously passed a resolution to ban staff from betting on prediction markets, after a series of scandals about wagers on political events.

CBS BS: вҖҳ60 MinutesвҖҷ correspondent Sharyn Alfonsi told a DC awards ceremony she rebuffed CBS bosses who asked her to change her famously pulled report about CECOT prison in El Salvador and Trump administration immigration abuses.

What, in 1993, did law enforcement find more than 100 grams of underneath a house shared by Jeff Landry, then a sheriffвҖҷs deputy in St. Martin Parish, Louisiana, now governor of that state?

Keep your eyes out for the answer below!

рҹҮөрҹҮё DonвҖҷt forget Gaza: Israeli forces carried out drone strikes in Gaza City, killing at least three yesterday. Meanwhile, the Israeli government has taken two of the Global Sumud FlotillaвҖҷs leaders hostage and says it is taking them to Israel. The other activists, humanitarians, and journalists Israel abducted overnight Wednesday, including Zeteo contributor Alex Colston, have been taken to Crete.

рҹҮәрҹҮҰрҹҮ·рҹҮә Potential ceasefire?: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky

рҹҮ¬рҹҮ§ Man charged: British authorities have charged a 45-year-old man with attempted murder in this weekвҖҷs stabbings of two Jewish men in London.

рҹҮ§рҹҮ· Reduced time: BrazilвҖҷs Congress approved a measure to reduce the prison sentence of former far-right president and coup plotter Jair Bolsonaro by 20 years, overriding a veto signed by President Luiz InГЎcio Lula da Silva in December.

рҹҮёрҹҮ©рҹҮұрҹҮҫ Migrant wreck: 17 people were found dead and nine others were missing after a boat carrying 33 Sudanese migrants capsized near Tobruk, in Libya.

рҹҮІрҹҮІ House arrest: Former Myanmar leader Aung San Suu Kyi received a commutation, bringing her prison sentence for ordering a coup in 2021 down to house arrest, among pardons given in relation to a Buddhist religious holiday.

New York Times and CNN coverage of the Iran war is вҖңactually seditious,вҖқ says a man who insists other peopleвҖҷs invective is responsible for inciting hatred and encouraging political violence.

The Trump Administration Aims to Penalize Disabled Adults Who Live With Their Families

The White House is quietly looking to drastically reduce disability benefits for some of the poorest Americans, with a proposed rule change that would affect around 400,000 families dependent on critical social programs. In this ProPublica investigation, Eli Hager delves into how the Trump administration is plotting its attack on social program benefits, and how success could affect disabled adults and their family members.

How Tony Dokoupil Became the Face of вҖҳZionist FanaticвҖҷ Bari WeissвҖҷs CBS

In September 2024, Tony Dokoupil of CBS drew attention over a tense interview with author Ta-Nehisi Coates, whom he implied was promoting вҖңextremistвҖқ views by being critical of Israel. Somehow, the interview seemed to lay the groundwork for DokoupilвҖҷs rapid rise within CBS News. ZeteoвҖҷs Justin Baragona conducts a deep dive into DokoupilвҖҷs career in TV and how he became the face of CBS Evening News, even with a controversial record.

Mills Exit Is a Blow to Schumer as Democrats Question His Strategy

Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer was banking on Maine Governor Janet Mills becoming the Democratic nominee to take on GOP Senator Susan Collins in November вҖ“ only for her to drop out of the race on Thursday, just a few months in. New York Times reporter Annie Karni breaks down how SchumerвҖҷs out-of-touch strategies are causing him to lose his influence over the Democratic Party.

ZeteoвҖҷs Martin Pengelly and Akshay Gokul contributed to this newsletter.

Still want to get Zeteo content, but not this newsletter? You can unsubscribe directly from вҖҳFirst DraftвҖҷ by going to вҖҳ

CBS BS: вҖҳ60 MinutesвҖҷ correspondent Sharyn Alfonsi told a DC awards ceremony she rebuffed CBS bosses who asked her to change her famously pulled report about CECOT prison in El Salvador and Trump administration immigration abuses.

What, in 1993, did law enforcement find more than 100 grams of underneath a house shared by Jeff Landry, then a sheriffвҖҷs deputy in St. Martin Parish, Louisiana, now governor of that state?

Keep your eyes out for the answer below!

рҹҮөрҹҮё DonвҖҷt forget Gaza: Israeli forces carried out drone strikes in Gaza City, killing at least three yesterday. Meanwhile, the Israeli government has taken two of the Global Sumud FlotillaвҖҷs leaders hostage and says it is taking them to Israel. The other activists, humanitarians, and journalists Israel abducted overnight Wednesday, including Zeteo contributor Alex Colston, have been taken to Crete.

рҹҮәрҹҮҰрҹҮ·рҹҮә Potential ceasefire?: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky

рҹҮ¬рҹҮ§ Man charged: British authorities have charged a 45-year-old man with attempted murder in this weekвҖҷs stabbings of two Jewish men in London.

рҹҮ§рҹҮ· Reduced time: BrazilвҖҷs Congress approved a measure to reduce the prison sentence of former far-right president and coup plotter Jair Bolsonaro by 20 years, overriding a veto signed by President Luiz InГЎcio Lula da Silva in December.

рҹҮёрҹҮ©рҹҮұрҹҮҫ Migrant wreck: 17 people were found dead and nine others were missing after a boat carrying 33 Sudanese migrants capsized near Tobruk, in Libya.

рҹҮІрҹҮІ House arrest: Former Myanmar leader Aung San Suu Kyi received a commutation, bringing her prison sentence for ordering a coup in 2021 down to house arrest, among pardons given in relation to a Buddhist religious holiday.

New York Times and CNN coverage of the Iran war is вҖңactually seditious,вҖқ says a man who insists other peopleвҖҷs invective is responsible for inciting hatred and encouraging political violence.

The Trump Administration Aims to Penalize Disabled Adults Who Live With Their Families

The White House is quietly looking to drastically reduce disability benefits for some of the poorest Americans, with a proposed rule change that would affect around 400,000 families dependent on critical social programs. In this ProPublica investigation, Eli Hager delves into how the Trump administration is plotting its attack on social program benefits, and how success could affect disabled adults and their family members.

How Tony Dokoupil Became the Face of вҖҳZionist FanaticвҖҷ Bari WeissвҖҷs CBS

In September 2024, Tony Dokoupil of CBS drew attention over a tense interview with author Ta-Nehisi Coates, whom he implied was promoting вҖңextremistвҖқ views by being critical of Israel. Somehow, the interview seemed to lay the groundwork for DokoupilвҖҷs rapid rise within CBS News. ZeteoвҖҷs Justin Baragona conducts a deep dive into DokoupilвҖҷs career in TV and how he became the face of CBS Evening News, even with a controversial record.

Mills Exit Is a Blow to Schumer as Democrats Question His Strategy

Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer was banking on Maine Governor Janet Mills becoming the Democratic nominee to take on GOP Senator Susan Collins in November вҖ“ only for her to drop out of the race on Thursday, just a few months in. New York Times reporter Annie Karni breaks down how SchumerвҖҷs out-of-touch strategies are causing him to lose his influence over the Democratic Party.

ZeteoвҖҷs Martin Pengelly and Akshay Gokul contributed to this newsletter.
