item.title: In the Shadow of Iran Diplomacy, Pakistan Bulldozes Hundreds of Homes of the Poor
item.description: On the morning of April 14, Sudhir Kiyani was at home in IslamabadвҖҷs Noori Bagh neighborhood when officials from the Capital Development Authority (CDA), accompanied by police, knocked on his door. вҖңCDA officials and police officers asked us to move out within the next hour, or they would knockвҖҰ
item.author: Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
On the morning of April 14, Sudhir Kiyani was at home in IslamabadвҖҷs Noori Bagh neighborhood when officials from the Capital Development Authority (CDA), accompanied by police, knocked on his door. вҖңCDA officials and police officers asked us to move out within the next hour, or they would knock down our house with everything in it,вҖқ Kiyani told Zeteo. His family had lived in that house for seven generations.

His wife and 10-year-old son left that morning with nowhere to go. The boy had attended the first day of sixth grade the day before вҖ“ and has not returned to school since.

Just days earlier, Pakistan had hosted high-stakes talks between the United States and Iran. Delegations arrived alongside international media, many of whom couldnвҖҷt resist praising IslamabadвҖҷs calm, manicured beauty. Landmarks like the Islamabad Serena Hotel and the Jinnah Convention Center briefly became symbols of the cityвҖҷs global moment. Since then, Islamabad has remained in the headlines, with expectations of a second round of talks.

But even as the Pakistani capital basked in the diplomatic spotlight, another story was quietly unfolding.

Soon after the delegations departed, authorities moved into IslamabadвҖҷs Noori Bagh neighborhood and demolished more than 200 homes, displacing more than 500 people, within just 12 hours, according to residents. In the protests and clashes that followed, more than 350 people were reportedly arrested, many charged under an anti-terrorism law.

Noori Bagh is a 300-year-old village in the Noorpur Shahan area of the cityвҖҷs Bari Imam neighborhood, one of 29 вҖҳunrecognizedвҖҷ settlements.

This monthвҖҷs demolitions are part of the CDAвҖҷs ambitious plans to redesign the city, which, according to the Alliance for Urban Rights, have exposed all 29 settlements to imminent demolition and could displace more than 400,000 people in Islamabad.

Similar demolitions have been ongoing for more than a decade, but residents and activists have alleged that the government used the вҖңIslamabad TalksвҖқ as a pretext to accelerate the demolitions and crackdown on protests. вҖңWhile Pakistan attempts to babysit global peace, the state is waging a war against its poor at home,вҖқ said Alia Amirali, a professor at Quaid-e-Azam University and a housing rights organizer, at a community meeting in Islamabad this week.

Speaking to Zeteo, Amirali emphasized how hosting the peace negotiations affected the pace of city-wide demolitions. вҖңThe timing of this operation indicates, now that Pakistan has positioned itself as a global peacemaker, it wants to refashion Islamabad into a global city,вҖқ she said. вҖңAnd thatвҖҷs where the vision of Islamabad as an eclectic mix of Shanghai and Manhattan is coming from.вҖқ

The CDA reports to Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi, who was seen alongside Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif in official photos from the talks. In press conferences earlier this year, Naqvi said he intended to transform Islamabad, envisioning a skyline inspired by вҖңManhattan-styleвҖқ high-rises or ShanghaiвҖҷs rapid urban expansion.

Tanvir Abbasi, a resident, told Zeteo that he believes the government used the вҖңIslamabad TalksвҖқ to draw attention away from residentsвҖҷ demands for housing rights. In January, during a similar demolition campaign, Abbasi was hit by pellets in his face and almost lost an eye.

The CDA has claimed it acquired the land using legal procedures and called protesters вҖңmiscreants.вҖқ But residents and land rights advocates alleged the operation targeted a neighborhood where families hold documented claims going back generations, and that it bypassed a court order intended to protect their homes. The CDA did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

In 1992, the Islamabad Capital Territories Zoning Regulations divided the city into 5 zones, where zones 2 and 5 were opened for private housing schemes, and zone 4 was limited to sports, recreation, institutions, farming, and model villages. Court documents show Noorpur Shahan entirely falls under zone 3, where the Margall National Park is and where further construction is prohibited.

Amirali explained that Noorpur Shahan was one of many declared a model village and afforded protection under the wildlife department. вҖңThese residents were registered voters, were given access to the national grid, and have now been made homeless overnight,вҖқ she said.

In January, Member of the National Assembly Tallal Chaudhry confirmed more than 29,115 trees had been uprooted last year in the area, to make way for infrastructural development in the area, at the cost of the lives and livelihoods of many.

While Chaudhry and Climate Minister Mussadiq Malik both claimed the move only affected paper mulberry trees, an invasive species causing pollen allergy, forest experts and climate activists say authorities are uprooting large swaths of native greenery.

Islamabad is PakistanвҖҷs only planned city, designed in 1960 when the capital was moved from Karachi. Most of its residents migrated from other parts of the country, but the homes being demolished are in some of its oldest neighborhoods, where families have lived for generations. That history is etched into places in Noori Bagh, home to the Bari Imam shrine, the mausoleum of a 17th-century Punjabi Sufi ascetic.

Abbasi was headed to the Supreme Court of Pakistan on April 14 when his phone rang. вҖңThey are demolishing everyoneвҖҷs homes,вҖқ a friend told him. Abbasi was on his way to challenge the lifting of a stay order that barred demolitions in the neighborhood. Under Pakistani law, he said, a stay order becomes permanent six months after it is issued and cannot be reversed. It was the only legal barrier between the bulldozers and his home, where his family had lived for more than 200 years.

вҖңWhen I returned, I saw my neighbors desperately searching through the rubble for parts of their homes they could reuse, fishing for window panes or bricks they could use to rebuild their homes,вҖқ Abbasi said. вҖңI have never seen something like this in my life, where a government is evicting its own poor, who had been living on a piece of land for hundreds of years.вҖқ

As residents protested the demolitions вҖ“ some hurling stones at police вҖ“ security forces responded with baton charges, tear gas, and pellet fire, according to multiple residents. Several people were injured and rushed to hospitals, residents said. Families of the detained claimed that many of those who were arrested were charged under the Anti-Terrorism Act, a non-bailable law typically reserved for serious acts of violence. Residents also claimed that police complaints were filed against some individuals who were not even present during the demolitions.

Local media have faced severe censorship, hampering coverage of the demolitions.

Amirali said members of the press have been unable to report freely. вҖңThe level of surveillance of the media on this issue is an indicator of how keen they are to ensure their international image is not tarnished by the massive atrocities they are currently committing against the most vulnerable,вҖқ she said.

Meanwhile, the demolitions are having far-reaching impacts. Under severe mental stress from being driven to homelessness, a 45-year-old man committed suicide on Tuesday, according to Abassi. He was the breadwinner for his mother and sister.

вҖңI would say there was more law and order in the country during Zia-ul-HaqвҖҷs martial law,вҖқ said Abbasi, referring to the military dictator who ruled Pakistan from 1977 to 1988 with an iron fist. вҖңWe have no hope from the authorities left anymore.вҖқ

Kiyani, the father whose home was demolished, said his sonвҖҷs school is now too far for the family to reach. For now, the boy is not enrolled anywhere. вҖңWith one swift stroke,вҖқ Kiyani said, speaking from a relativeвҖҷs home. вҖңWe have lost everything.вҖқ

Areeba Fatima is an investigative data journalist based in New York City. She has worked as a video producer, investigative reporter, and broadcast journalist in Pakistan for five years, and is now pursuing a masterвҖҷs in data journalism at Columbia University.

Check out more from Zeteo:

item.title: 'Piracy': Israel Kidnaps Nearly 200 Peace Activists Aboard Flotilla 600 Miles Away From Gaza
item.description: Israeli forces intercepted a Gaza aid flotilla over 600 miles from the coast of Gaza on Wednesday night, abducting the crews of nearly two dozen boats in international waters near Greece. According to the flotillaвҖҷs live tracker , 22 boats carrying 179 crew members were intercepted, and over 40вҖҰ
item.author: Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
Israeli forces intercepted a Gaza aid flotilla over 600 miles from the coast of Gaza on Wednesday night, abducting the crews of nearly two dozen boats in international waters near Greece.

According to the flotillaвҖҷs live tracker, 22 boats carrying 179 crew members were intercepted, and over 40 others are still sailing.

The latest flotilla effort, the Global Sumud Flotilla, includes more than 400 people from over 70 nations. It is now the fifth humanitarian flotilla that Israel has intercepted in the past two years. People from around the world have joined the efforts as an attempt to breach IsraelвҖҷs illegal blockade of Gaza and deliver humanitarian aid to millions of Palestinians suffering from IsraelвҖҷs occupation and genocide.

Among those kidnapped are 15 people from the US, including Zeteo contributor Alex Colston.

This is the furthest away from Gaza that Israel has seized an aid flotilla. There is no legal authority that enables the Israeli government to commit what some are calling вҖңpiracyвҖқ in international waters.

вҖңHow on earth is [it] possible that Israel is allowed to assault and seize vessels in international waters just off Greece/Europe?вҖқ UN special rapporteur Francesca Albanese said in a social media post. вҖңBesides what you can think of Apartheid Israel and its genocidal leaders, this should send shock waves across Europe. Apartheid without borders.вҖқ

On Wednesday night, the Israeli navy contacted the flotilla by radio through the Greenpeace Arctic Sunrise vessel.

вҖңThis is the Israeli Navy. Attempts to break the lawful maritime security blockade on the Gaza Strip constitutes a violation of international law,вҖқ a voice says. вҖң...Any further attempts to sail towards Gaza places your safety at risk and leaves the IDF no choice but to take necessary measures at its disposal to enforce the lawful maritime security blockade.вҖқ


One passenger aboard a flotilla vessel that wasnвҖҷt intercepted told Zeteo that a drone flashed lights at the vessel last night and military vessels were visible in the distance, but Israeli forces have otherwise not made contact. The boat is planning to anchor in Crete to shelter from a storm.

The crew member, who asked to remain anonymous out of safety concerns, said Israeli forces sabotaged the engine of at least one flotilla vessel, stranding people aboard ahead of the expected storm, but other vessels in the fleet were able to pick them up.

On Wednesday, hours before Israeli forces illegally intercepted the ships, Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz said he вҖңimposed sanctionsвҖқ on the flotillaвҖҷs crowdfunding campaign, ridiculously claiming it is вҖңorganized by the Hamas terror organization, in cooperation with additional international organizations and under the guise of a humanitarian aid flotilla.вҖқ

On Thursday, Donald TrumpвҖҷs so-called вҖңBoard of Peace,вҖқ which is supposedly overseeing the reconstruction of Gaza, smeared the flotilla as вҖңperformative love-boat activism of people who know nothing of and care even less for the condition of Gazans.вҖқ

After the Israeli Foreign Ministry said it would take the kidnapped crew members to Israel, Foreign Affairs Minister Gideon SaвҖҷar then claimed on Thursday that the abducted passengers will be handed over to Greece, and thanked the Greek government for its вҖңwillingness to receive the flotilla participants.вҖқ

SaвҖҷar falsely claimed that people who want to deliver aid to Gaza can do so through the so-called Board of Peace, and called IsraelвҖҷs naval blockade вҖңlawful.вҖқ

Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu praised the Israeli Navy on social media.

IsraelвҖҷs attack has prompted worldwide condemnation. In a statement, the Italian government said it вҖңcondemns the seizure of the Global Sumud Flotilla vessels,вҖқ and called on Israel to вҖңimmediately release all the unlawfully detained ItaliansвҖқ and for the вҖңfull respect of international law and guarantees on the physical safety of the people on board.вҖқ

Italy added that it is вҖңcommitted to continue supplying humanitarian aid to Gaza in the framework of our cooperation and in respect of international law.вҖқ

Spanish Prime Minister Pedro SГЎnchez slammed Israel, saying it вҖңis once again violating international law by assaulting a civilian flotilla in waters that do not belong to it.вҖқ

вҖңThe EU must suspend the association agreement NOW and demand that Netanyahu comply with the law of our seas,вҖқ he added, referring to the 2000 trade and relations agreement between the European Union and Israel.

The Turkish Ministry of Foreign Affairs slammed IsraelвҖҷs attack as вҖңan act of piracy.вҖқ

вҖңBy targeting the Global Sumud Flotilla, whose mission is to draw attention to the humanitarian catastrophe faced by the innocent people of Gaza, Israel has also violated humanitarian principles and international law,вҖқ the Turkish government said, calling on the international community to take a unified stance against IsraelвҖҷs вҖңunlawfulвҖқ action.

вҖңThis act of aggression further represents a breach of the principle of freedom of navigation on the high seas.вҖқ

Opposition parties and pro-Palestine advocates in Greece accused the conservative government of complicity in IsraelвҖҷs attack in international waters near Crete.

вҖңThe Greek government is either complicit or utterly incapable of defending our waters and the Law of the Sea against Israeli incursions,вҖқ Yanis Varoufakis, secretary of left-wing party MeRA25, said on Wednesday as Israel was targeting the fleet.

The March to Gaza Greece initiative accused the government of cooperating with Israel.

вҖңIt is obvious they are lying,вҖқ the group said in a statement. вҖңThe ships are within the Greek SAR zone, the same area where they were targeted by drones last summer. We demand the Greek government, the Coast Guard, and the Ministry of Maritime Affairs ensure the safe passage of our fleet and prevent further aggression.вҖқ

If Greece did cooperate with the Israeli government in any way, the implications would be far-reaching. It would pin one NATO member state, Greece, against several others in the region вҖ“ including Italy, Spain, and Turkey вҖ“ who have slammed the attack.

A NATO official referred Zeteo to Greek authorities, which did not respond. The White House referred to the US State Department, which did not respond.

Last October, Israel intercepted a 44-vessel flotilla, which was crewed by individuals including European Union Members of Parliament Rima Hassan and Emma Forreau, Nelson MandelaвҖҷs grandson Mandla Mandela, вҖҳPortrait of a Lady on FireвҖҷ star AdГЁle Haenel, and Zeteo contributor Greta Thunberg.

Israeli soldiers allegedly strip-searched, zip-tied, and blindfolded members, even forcing some, including Thunberg, to take photo-ops with the Israeli flag. In Israeli detention, crew members were allegedly subjected to abusive treatment, including sleep and medical deprivation, beatings, and having guns pointed at them.

Even still, months later, hundreds of people from around the world set off on the latest flotilla mission.

Flotilla organizers told Alex Colston at the onset of this yearвҖҷs mission that ongoing genocide, coupled with the Iran war, made the risks worth it this time around.

вҖңThis is not a risk-free mission,вҖқ Brazilian activist Thiago ГҒvila, one of the GSFвҖҷs lead organizers, told Alex at the time. вҖңNonetheless, a nonviolent solidarity mission is a fraction of the risk that Palestinians have gone through every single day for eight decades.вҖқ

The passenger Zeteo spoke to on Thursday added that the reason for the flotilla mission is вҖңbecause our leaders have failed us.вҖқ

вҖңIf we allow Israel to continue kidnapping civilians illegally when theyвҖҷre not even near Gaza, it shows a reckless and careless attitude towards civilian life by all world leaders,вҖқ the passenger said. вҖңIf leaders wanted to show us they cared about humanity and actually wanted peace, they would sanction Israel and its allies.вҖқ

Read ZeteoвҖҷs statement on IsraelвҖҷs illegal actions:

Have any tips or information for us? You can text Prem on Signal at premthakker.35 and Minnah at minnahfarshad.94

item.title: Graham Platner Will Be MaineвҖҷs Democratic Senate Nominee
item.description: With over a month left in the Democratic primary, Graham Platner is now the partyвҖҷs presumptive nominee for US Senate in Maine, in the pivotal fight against fake moderate Susan Collins, the five-term Republican incumbent. Janet Mills suspended her campaign on Thursday, reluctantly ceding theвҖҰ
item.author: "Zeteo | U.S. Politics"
item.content:encoded:
With over a month left in the Democratic primary, Graham Platner is now the partyвҖҷs presumptive nominee for US Senate in Maine, in the pivotal fight against fake moderate Susan Collins, the five-term Republican incumbent.

Janet Mills suspended her campaign on Thursday, reluctantly ceding the primary to Platner without endorsing him. An anti-war, anti-Israel oysterman and Marine combat veteran, Platner was a complete unknown when he launched his bid. He faced his share of scandals and negative news coverage while building a massive lead in the polls вҖ“ and ultimately cruising to the nomination.

That Platner came out of nowhere to take control of the race is an embarrassment for Senate Democratic leader Chuck Schumer, who recruited Mills, the two-term incumbent governor, into the race.

Schumer and Senator Kirsten Gillibrand, who chairs the Democratic Senatorial Campaign Committee, put out a humbling statement on Thursday indicating they will support Platner moving forward. вҖңAfter years of allowing TrumpвҖҷs abuses of power, Senator Collins has never been more vulnerable and we will work with the presumptive democratic nominee Graham Platner to defeat her,вҖқ they wrote.

Zeteo broke the news of PlatnerвҖҷs launch in August, and weвҖҷve followed his campaign on the ground in Maine. Platner, who was backed early on by Bernie Sanders, appears to be building a grassroots machine, with the candidate declaring in recent weeks that his campaign has 15,000 volunteers. He has packed town hall after town hall around the state, sharing his views with voters and his anger at the political system, presenting a left-wing message focused on building worker power and beating back the forces robbing Americans of their worth, freedom, and time.

Much of the mainstream media and many liberals on social media wrote Platner off after it was revealed that he for many years had a skull-and-crossbones tattoo he got as a Marine that resembled a Nazi symbol. News outlets also dug up PlatnerвҖҷs old Reddit posts, which included misogynistic and bigoted comments, among other things.

The scandals did surprisingly little to affect his primary campaign. He covered his tattoo up, denied he knew its meaning, and apologized. He also apologized for many of his Reddit posts. (It should be noted that throughout his campaign Platner has consistently spoken out against xenophobia, fascism, and right-wing culture-war campaigns against LGBTQ+ communities.)

In Maine, it has been clear that voters were not scared off by the news coverage вҖ“ and polls supported this. Recent surveys found him with a 30-point lead over Mills, and that heвҖҷs leading Collins, too.

MillsвҖҷs ads about PlatnerвҖҷs internet posts, featuring video of him shirtless with the tattoo, didnвҖҷt move the needle for her вҖ“ and may have even helped seal the race for Platner. Mills stopped running the ads earlier this month, as her campaign apparently began running low on cash. (A pro-Collins super PAC began running ads targeting Platner on both topics this week.)

While Mills didnвҖҷt campaign that hard, Platner has been hustling, and picking up speed. HeвҖҷs held over 60 town halls, and recently rallied in Portland with progressive Senator Elizabeth Warren, before a crowd of 1,000 people.

вҖңWeвҖҷve got a political system that, in many ways, is entirely inaccessible to the average American,вҖқ Platner said at a town hall earlier this month in Windham. вҖң[Politics] tends to be conducted by people who we donвҖҷt recognize, who donвҖҷt often come from our communities and are espousing policies and ideas that do not in any way, shape, or form reflect the things that we think or that we often talk to our neighbors about. I think that you couldnвҖҷt come up with a better example of this right now than the war in Iran.вҖқ

He continued: вҖңPeople see it for what it is. They see it as a war thatвҖҷs been started by Donald Trump, so he can take attention away from the fact that heвҖҷs clearly in the Epstein files. ItвҖҷs a war that everybody can see has been started because [Israeli Prime Minister] Benjamin Netanyahu has been trying to get this war to happen for 30 years, and he finally found a president who was sucker enough to go for it.вҖқ

Platner spent the evening listing off dark truths and red meat for an increasingly pissed-off Democratic voter base. The crowd, in a small meetinghouse filled to the brim, almost certainly past capacity, gave him a standing ovation.

HereвҖҷs what one attendee, Jennifer McGowan, had to say about Platner after the event, speaking with Zeteo: вҖңHe seems to have a really firm grasp on the history of this country, and I like thatвҖҰ But also IвҖҷm really tired of the Democratic Party being a bunch of pussies, to be honest with you. I think that weвҖҷre all too nice, weвҖҷre all too worried about how itвҖҷs going to appear, how itвҖҷs going to look. And I think that thereвҖҷs a way to be tough and assertive without being immoral. I wish we had more people that would do that. I feel like heвҖҷs that kind of guy.вҖқ

At one point during the Windham town hall, Platner noted his campaign couldnвҖҷt have gotten going without an early endorsement from Bernie Sanders. вҖңIвҖҷll tell you as someone that had no money doing this, I got really, really lucky,вҖқ Platner said. вҖңIf it wasnвҖҷt for the fact Bernie Sanders endorsed me in the first couple of weeks. That allowed us to do small-dollar fundraising on a mass scale. [If] we hadnвҖҷt been able to do that, this doesnвҖҷt happen.вҖқ

I spoke with Sanders in September, when he and Platner rallied together at an arena in Portland.

вҖңI think the Democratic leadership is way out of touch. TheyвҖҷre out of touch on whatвҖҷs happening in Gaza. TheyвҖҷre out of touch economically,вҖқ Sanders told me. вҖңI think we need a grassroots movement in this country which transforms the Democratic Party into a party of the working class that is prepared to fight for the issues relevant to the working class. ThatвҖҷs what Graham Platner is about.вҖқ

He said he was backing Platner to send a message to the political establishment: вҖңThe idea of having a working class guy, who has never run for office before, take on Senator Collins вҖ“ and I think thereвҖҷs a good chance he will beat her вҖ“ that sends a message.вҖқ

вҖңGrahamвҖҷs background is very, very different,вҖқ Sanders said. вҖңHeвҖҷs not a conventional politician.вҖқ

вҖңThat sends a message,вҖқ he explained, вҖңthat ordinary people can step up. You can take on the powers that be, the political establishment. You can win.вҖқ

The Democratic political establishment might not appreciate that message вҖ“ but itвҖҷs hearing it loud and clear.

item.title: рҹ•өпёҸ Trump Wants to Spy More... On Our Allies
item.description: рҹҡЁ Breaking: Israel reportedly intercepted nearly a dozen boats part of the latest Gaza aid flotilla and abducted participants in international waters off the coast of Crete вҖ“ more than 600 miles from Gaza. According to the Global Sumud Flotilla tracker, at least 22 of the more than 50 boats in theвҖҰ
item.author: First Draft | Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
рҹҡЁ Breaking: Israel reportedly intercepted nearly a dozen boats part of the latest Gaza aid flotilla and abducted participants in international waters off the coast of Crete вҖ“ more than 600 miles from Gaza. According to the Global Sumud Flotilla tracker, at least 22 of the more than 50 boats in the flotilla had been intercepted by early Thursday. Francesca Albanese called IsraelвҖҷs illegal actions вҖңapartheid without borders.вҖқ

Good morning, all; itвҖҷs Swin and Prem again. This week, President Donald Trump, an elderly wannabe king who serves as the head of state of a shockingly nativist Western government, and whose administration has been rocked by the Jeffrey Epstein scandal, met at the White House with King Charles III, an elderly king who serves as the head of state of a shockingly nativist Western government, and whose family has been rocked by the Epstein scandal.

The two were chummy, smirking, and poking fun. But gurgling just beneath the surface of his attempts to charm the monarch of a close ally, Trump was hiding one of the worst-kept secrets in all geopolitics: That he thinks a lot of these transatlantic alliances are a whole bunch of feckless bullshit and he wants US intelligence to spy on allies like theyвҖҷre China or North Korea.

More on that in a momentвҖҰ But hereвҖҷs some good news! TodayвҖҷs edition of вҖҳFirst DraftвҖҷ has NO paywall, thanks to our sponsor, Ground News. рҹ—һпёҸ рҹҳҺ рҹҷҸ

Now letвҖҷs get to itвҖҰ

In late January, the so-called вҖңDepartment of WarвҖқ publicly released its new National Defense Strategy (NDS), the PentagonвҖҷs companion document to Donald TrumpвҖҷs latest National Security Strategy, which outlines a breathlessly far-right vision for the world and AmericaвҖҷs place in it.

There is, however, a classified version of the Trump-Vance administrationвҖҷs NDS, one that was not released online. Two sources familiar with the matter tell us the classified NDS includes a section left out of the public document вҖ“ paragraphs that call for an aggressive reprioritization of US вҖңintelligence assets,вҖқ in the context of international burden-sharing, and having our allies do what the Americans tell them to do.

In recent months, including during the drafting process for the NDS, some US officials were alarmed by some of the more hyper-pugnacious lines in this section, interpreting them as Team TrumpвҖҷs call for the US to ramp up spying operations on longtime allies.

You mightвҖҷve been able to argue that these internal fears were overblown, if they were happening in a vacuum. But they arenвҖҷt.

For weeks, weвҖҷve been following up on leads on this very topic, and hereвҖҷs one thing we found: Since last year, according to two US officials and another source close to the White House, President Trump has repeatedly told multiple senior administration officials that the American intelligence community should spy more on our allies, including on governments that are part of NATO and Western Europe.

The US has, of course, spied and gathered top-secret intel on allies in the past, in one form or another. But Trump, the sources add, has privately insisted that supposed guardrails the American government does have вҖ“ that limit the extent of our spying on allied nations вҖ“ are, in the presidentвҖҷs succinct phrasing, вҖңstupid.вҖқ

Over the past year-plus, Trump has blurted out that those restrictions and norms make no sense when it comes to how US intelligence operations abroad should work, particularly towards allies and international partners that Trump often feels act too much like вҖңenemiesвҖқ instead of pliant friends. As is the case with his approach to virtually all domestic and foreign affairs, he wants any semblance of guardrails obliterated.

It is unclear how much of TrumpвҖҷs rantings on this matter have been put into actual, covert practice by the US intelligence community. However, as TrumpвҖҷs war on Iran threatens to tear down his administration and the GOP with it, he has routinely insulted or tried to bully or punish allies he deems insufficiently enthused about his disastrous and illegal bloodbath in the Middle East. Over the years, heвҖҷs again and again accused US allies of being вҖң worseвҖқ than foreign adversaries. And last year, according to the

Reached for comment on this story, the press shop for the Pete Hegseth-era Pentagon told Prem: вҖңWe have nothing to offer on this,вҖқ adding that they were saying this, вҖңVery Respectfully.вҖқ Hours later, Trump White House spokeswoman Anna Kelly told us: вҖңThis is totally false вҖ“ but that is typical for Zeteo, whose nutjob founder is such a terrible вҖҳjournalistвҖҷ that he was fired by MSNBC.вҖқ

That is a less respectful but honestly much, much more entertaining statement than what the вҖңWar DepartmentвҖқ was willing to cough up. Team Trump can respond however they wish. We stand by our reporting. Cheers.

We used Ground News to analyze the big story this week on TrumpвҖҷs fresh calls for ABC to fire Jimmy Kimmel.

Ground News is a news app and website that scans 50,000+ articles while offering built-in bias comparisons, fact checks, and funding transparency for every source.

For this story, we see that there is a split in the coverage with 27% left, 22% right, and 51% center . The headlines are the same, but the coverage couldnвҖҷt be more different. Ground News makes it easy to see the difference in how both sides are covering the same story.

The built-in factuality and ownership features also highlight the hidden angles behind every article. With Ground NewsвҖҷ Vantage Plan, information becomes your superpower. Learn more at ground.news/zeteox and get 40% off the unlimited Vantage Plan.

(This portion of the post was sponsored content)

Death tolls:

Iran: Iranian state media on Sunday reported, citing officials, that US-Israeli attacks have killed 3,468 people.

Lebanon: Israel has killed more than 2,576 people, including at least 177 children, in Lebanon, according to the countryвҖҷs Health Ministry.

US troops: At least 13 US troops have died in incidents related to the war.

Israel: At least 35 people, including 12 Israeli soldiers, have been killed, according to officials.

And in more war newsвҖҰ

IranвҖҷs vow: Iranian Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei said in a statement carried by state media today that Tehran will protect its вҖңnuclear and missile capabilitiesвҖқ as a вҖңnational asset.вҖқ

Strait talk: At the White House, Donald Trump talked to US oil companies about how to mitigate the effects of a lengthy blockade of Iranian shipping.

Price jump: Climate Power said US oil companies were raking in $30 million an hour while ordinary Americans pay over $4 a gallon at the pump. Oil prices spiked to wartime highs, as investors worried about the absence of meaningful talks.

Actual diplomacy: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi, the Associated Press said, conducted вҖңcalls with the foreign ministers of India, Kenya and PolandвҖҰ the president of the International Committee of the Red CrossвҖқ and the Lebanese speaker.

Panel beating: In nearly five hours of questioning by the House Armed Services Committee, Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth clashed angrily with Democrats, criticized some Republicans, and revealed the war has cost $25 billion.

Hunger crisis: An analysis by a global poverty monitor estimates that around 1.2 million people in Lebanon will soon face food insecurity arising from Israeli strikes.

According to CNN, Michigan Democratic Senate candidate Mallory McMorrow deleted thousands of old tweets, including claims she still voted in California while living in Michigan, disparaging remarks about Michigan, and messages comparing Donald Trump and his supporters to Nazis.

McMorrow also tweeted about a dream of the US вҖңamicablyвҖқ breaking up, Middle America going one way and the coasts, Canada, Mexico, and parts of Michigan and Texas another, the latter entity nominating Barack Obama вҖңas Prime MinisterвҖқ while вҖңeveryone was given $1,000 and six months to pick a side.вҖқ After initially refusing to say if McMorrow had been expressing вҖңa literal dream or a hypothetical situation she hoped for,вҖқ CNN said, her campaign said it was literally a dream.

McMorrowвҖҷs communications director said the deleted tweets were вҖңnormal tweets by a normal person.вҖқ But lest McMorrowвҖҷs rivals in the tight Democratic Senate primary take too much pleasure in her embarrassment, CNN pointed out that last year one of them, progressive Abdul El-Sayed, also scrubbed lots of potentially damaging tweets.

Court blow: In a 6-3 decision, Supreme Court conservatives gutted the Voting Rights Act provision guarding against racial discrimination. Dissenting, the liberal justices warned of вҖңfar-reaching and graveвҖқ consequences for US democracy.

Florida follows: In Tallahassee, the Republican-controlled legislature passed a new map that could net four more US House seats for the GOP.

Tin ear: In the Oval Office, Donald Trump вҖңhumorouslyвҖқ told NASA administrator Jared Isaacman: вҖңYou heard that question with those beautiful ears of yours ... HeвҖҷs got super hearing.вҖқ Isaacman managed to laugh it off.

Dystopia watch: Amazon is reportedly considering bringing back вҖҳThe Apprentice,вҖҷ the 2000s reality show that rescued Donald Trump from business oblivion before his move into politicsвҖҰ but with Donald Trump Jr. now as the host. Amazon previously spent $75 million on Melania TrumpвҖҷs propaganda film.

Epstein time: Former Attorney General Pam Bondi will give an interview to the House Oversight Committee about the Jeffrey Epstein affair on May 29. Democrats said their threat to hold Bondi in contempt prompted the GOP move.

Centrist fads: Democrat Josh Gottheimer and Republican Mike Lawler introduced a resolution condemning вҖңantisemitic, hate-filled rhetoric,вҖқ naming progressive streamer Hasan Piker and far-right conspiracist Candace Owens and suggesting they are somehow comparable.

Pure cruelty: A Colombian woman who fled torture was deported by the Trump administration to the Democratic Republic of Congo, the Bulwark reported. Adriana Quiroz ZapataвҖҷs lawyer lamented a вҖңcomplete lack of due process of law.вҖқ

As evidenced by TrumpвҖҷs Oval Office encounter with Jared Isaacman of NASA, the president seems to have a thing about big ears. In 2016, a rival now turned close aide was on the receiving end of such mockery. Who?

Keep your eyes out for the answer below!

рҹҮөрҹҮё DonвҖҷt forget Gaza: Major media companies from around the world are demanding Israel allow foreign journalists to enter Gaza. вҖңBeing on the ground is essential. It allows journalists to question official accounts on all sides, to speak directly with civilians and report back what they witness firsthand,вҖқ the executives of several news orgs, including AP, Reuters, and CNN, said in a statement.

рҹҢҺ Press freedom plummets: The freedom of the press is at a 25-year low, Reporters Without Borders said in its annual press freedom index. The US, considered вҖңproblematic,вҖқ is now ranked 64th, falling seven places since Trump retook the White House. Over half of the worldвҖҷs countries fall into the вҖңdifficultвҖқ or вҖңvery seriousвҖқ categories.

рҹҮІрҹҮ¬ Destabilization investigation: Madagascar Deputy Prosecutor Nomenarinera Mihamintsoa Ramanantsoa announced the detention of former French serviceman Guy Baretl, on charges alluding to a plot to destabilize the country.

рҹҮ®рҹҮ© Acid charges: Four Indonesian soldiers went to trial for allegedly carrying out an acid attack on a protester opposing the militarization of the government.

рҹҮІрҹҮҪ Drug charges: Several Mexican officials were charged with drug trafficking and weapons offenses by US officials. Mexico did not immediately respond.


At his congressional hearing, Pete Hegseth basically admitted that TrumpвҖҷs illegal war on Iran has achieved nothing. Time and time again, this administration reveals it has no clue what itвҖҷs doing in Iran, as they burn through billions of taxpayer dollars.

ZeteoвҖҷs Martin Pengelly and Akshay Gokul contributed to this newsletter.

For any issues with your subscription, please email us at info@zeteonews.com.

рҹҡЁ Breaking: Israel reportedly intercepted nearly a dozen boats part of the latest Gaza aid flotilla and abducted participants in international waters off the coast of Crete вҖ“ more than 600 miles from Gaza. According to the Global Sumud Flotilla tracker, at least 22 of the more than 50 boats in the flotilla had been intercepted by early Thursday. Francesca Albanese called IsraelвҖҷs illegal actions вҖңapartheid without borders.вҖқ

Good morning, all; itвҖҷs Swin and Prem again. This week, President Donald Trump, an elderly wannabe king who serves as the head of state of a shockingly nativist Western government, and whose administration has been rocked by the Jeffrey Epstein scandal, met at the White House with King Charles III, an elderly king who serves as the head of state of a shockingly nativist Western government, and whose family has been rocked by the Epstein scandal.

The two were chummy, smirking, and poking fun. But gurgling just beneath the surface of his attempts to charm the monarch of a close ally, Trump was hiding one of the worst-kept secrets in all geopolitics: That he thinks a lot of these transatlantic alliances are a whole bunch of feckless bullshit and he wants US intelligence to spy on allies like theyвҖҷre China or North Korea.

item.title: Behind the Scenes of Bari WeissвҖҷs Latest Pro-Israel Purge of CBS News
item.description: Good evening, and welcome to this weekвҖҷs вҖҳRagebait.вҖҷ Justin Baragona here, bringing you the latest media dispatches from the right-wing industrial outrage complex and Bari World. In tonightвҖҷs edition, we take a behind-the-scenes look at how Bari Weiss blew up CBS NewsвҖҷ international news coverage.вҖҰ
item.author: Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
Good evening, and welcome to this weekвҖҷs вҖҳRagebait.вҖҷ

Justin Baragona here, bringing you the latest media dispatches from the right-wing industrial outrage complex and Bari World. In tonightвҖҷs edition, we take a behind-the-scenes look at how Bari Weiss blew up CBS NewsвҖҷ international news coverage. Plus, another prominent news outlet is telling its staffers to love Israel or leave. Also, is Disney showing some backbone during Kimmelgate 2.0, and where does TrumpвҖҷs America rank in terms of press freedom?

On top of that, if you havenвҖҷt already, please check out my deep-dive profile on the face of BariвҖҷs CBS вҖ“ Tony Dokoupil. I spoke to a lot of people for it!

Earlier this week, CBS News staffers were shocked to learn that well-respected London bureau chief Claire Day вҖ“ who has spent nearly 25 years with the network вҖ“ was leaving the company at the end of the month amid a broad editorial restructuring of CBSвҖҷs international coverage.

While network president Tom Cibrowski claimed in a memo to staff that DayвҖҷs departure had been a mutual decision, both the New York Post and Status quickly reported that she had effectively been вҖңoustedвҖқ from her role following simmering tensions with editor-in-chief Bari Weiss over the bureauвҖҷs Middle East coverage.

The Post, specifically, noted that an unnamed freelance cameraman and editor had accused Day of running the bureau like a вҖңHamas cell,вҖқ while Status reported that Weiss вҖ“ a self-avowed вҖңZionist fanaticвҖқ вҖ“ at one point griped that London was so biased against Israel that вҖңthey might as well be Hamas.вҖқ

Speaking to several CBS sources, IвҖҷve since learned that Weiss may have gained some of her perception about DayвҖҷs supposed anti-Israel bias from her conversations with Cameron Stewart, a freelance cameraman who has been working out of CBS NewsвҖҷ London bureau since late 2019.

According to three sources, since Stewart first drew WeissвҖҷs attention last fall, he has repeatedly boasted that he has a вҖңdirect lineвҖқ to the CBS News chief and has claimed to speak to her regularly.

In the end, before emotionally addressing her pending departure with the bureauвҖҷs staff on Monday, Day asked Stewart to leave the room. вҖңIt was very awkward,вҖқ one staffer who was in attendance told Zeteo. Additionally, some staffers believe that Stewart may have hoped to score a full-time staff position in the United States by ingratiating himself with Weiss.

Stewart has not responded to requests for comment.

Multiple CBS News staffers noted that before her departure, Day regularly pushed back against certain narratives and claims made in the daily editorial calls. These sources added that it was generally in a respectful manner, but could have also led to increased friction with Weiss and her deputies.

Another source of tension in recent weeks was the London bureauвҖҷs plan to send a foreign correspondent to report from within Iran at the start of the war after obtaining a visa to travel inside the Islamic Republic. As Status first reported, Weiss ultimately nixed the plan, citing security and safety concerns.

Weiss, who has openly cheered Donald TrumpвҖҷs illegal war, was also concerned about any backlash CBS would receive for a trip that required Iranian government permission, especially since the White House had blasted CNN for doing the same thing and accused the cable network of peddling pro-Iran propaganda.

Though Weiss had likely formulated her opinion on Day and the London bureau as being overly sympathetic to Palestine and biased against Israel early on, she also never reached out directly to Day to discuss any of this, two sources told me.

While Weiss may have been privately planning to replace Day for months, two sources said that Day was still left вҖңblindsidedвҖқ when she was informed two weeks ago that she was being fired and replaced by Shayndi Raice, a deputy Middle East bureau chief from the Wall Street Journal.

The problem for CBS, however, was that Raice was brought on for a new role of foreign editor вҖ“ a position that had not been publicly posted. Weiss, using this new position as the basis to remove Day as bureau chief, apparently violated UK employment law for wrongful dismissal, particularly since Day wasnвҖҷt being fired for cause.

Ultimately, two sources said, Day quickly reached a severance agreement with the networkвҖҷs lawyers before MondayвҖҷs memo from Cibrowski stating that Day and CBS had вҖңmutually agreedвҖқ that the London bureau chief вҖңwill leaveвҖқ the company on May 1.

Day declined to comment.

Raice, who previously worked with WeissвҖҷs handpicked managing editor Charles Forelle, was officially announced by Weiss as the networkвҖҷs new foreign editor on Wednesday. вҖңAll our international coverage will be led by the foreign editor,вҖқ Weiss wrote in a memo to staff. вҖңSheвҖҷll set priorities, deploy producers and correspondents, and bring together all the exceptional work our overseas teams do.вҖқ

Much like many of the folks Weiss has recently hired or promoted, RaiceвҖҷs stance on Israel and Gaza largely aligns with that of the CBS News chief. A graduate of Yeshiva University who is married to a rabbi who is an associate professor of Jewish studies, Raice has spent the past few years in Israel for the Journal вҖ“ and has taken heat for the paperвҖҷs reporting on the war between Israel and Hamas, particularly a disputed report about UNRWA workersвҖҷ alleged ties to Hamas.

вҖңAt various points since Oct. 7, there have been internal tensions and friction within the Journal over the paperвҖҷs coverage of the conflict in Gaza,вҖқ Semafor reported in 2024, adding: вҖңReporters covering Gaza in the Middle East bureau were concerned about deputy Middle East bureau chief Shayndi RaiceвҖҷs leadership and privately discussed asking editors to remove her from leading coverage.вҖқ

CBS staffers I spoke to pointed out that, much like Weiss before she was hired by David Ellison to run the Tiffany Network, Raice has no experience in broadcast television news. вҖңWe all know why Shayndi Raice has that job,вҖқ one senior staffer told me. вҖңItвҖҷs because sheвҖҷs a Zionist.вҖқ

A representative for CBS News did not respond to a request for comment.

Speaking of media bosses pushing their overt pro-Israel stance onto their employees, Mathias DГ¶pfner вҖ“ CEO of PoliticoвҖҷs parent company Axel Springer вҖ“ rejected the publicationвҖҷs staffersвҖҷ warning that his вҖңrepeated use of POLITICO to promote his political agendaвҖқ risked вҖңtaking on the appearance of editorial slant.вҖқ

During a Monday meeting with staff in response to their letter expressing concern that he was вҖңunderminingвҖқ their reputation with his opinion pieces for the outlet, DГ¶pfner doubled down that the companyвҖҷs core values include вҖңsupport for IsraelвҖҷs right to exist.вҖқ And if staffers disagreed with these вҖңessentials,вҖқ according to Jewish InsiderвҖҷs recording of the call, they should quit.

вҖңNobody should work for Axel Springer despite the essentials or in disagreement with one of the essentials,вҖқ DГ¶pfner declared, with Politico executives expressing вҖңalignmentвҖқ with him. вҖңIf the essentials are not attractive, if the essentials are not a magnet, if the essentials are not a reason why to work for this company, I can only recommend to work for other companies.вҖқ

Is Disney, which kowtowed to Trump as he returned to the White House and further capitulated to him in the ensuing months, finally taking a stand against the presidentвҖҷs authoritarian bullying of the media?

Amid Team TrumpвҖҷs bad-faith campaign to once again cancel Jimmy Kimmel, this time ludicrously claiming the anti-MAGA comic called for the presidentвҖҷs assassination with an age-related joke two days prior to the White House CorrespondentsвҖҷ Dinner attack, the Mouse House decided to do something it hadnвҖҷt done before: fight back.

With the presidentвҖҷs handpicked FCC chairman (and media attack dog) Brendan Carr suddenly challenging ABCвҖҷs broadcast licenses as an obvious retaliatory action to the latest Kimmelgate ginned up by MAGA, Disney вҖ“ the parent company of ABC вҖ“ told the FCC theyвҖҷd see them in court. (The FCCвҖҷs lone Democratic commissioner, meanwhile, said the commission was вҖңclearly not behaving independentlyвҖқ and вҖңdoing the bidding of the White House.вҖқ)

вҖңWe have received the Federal Communications CommissionвҖҷs order initiating an accelerated review of the licenses held by ABCвҖҷs owned television stations,вҖқ a company spokesperson said in a statement.

вҖңABC and its stations have a long record of operating in full compliance with FCC rules and serving their local communities with trusted news, emergency information, and publicвҖ‘interest programming,вҖқ the spokesperson added. вҖңWe are confident that the record demonstrates our continued qualifications as licensees under the Communications Act and the First Amendment, and are prepared to show that through the appropriate legal channels. Our focus remains, as always, on serving viewers in the local communities where our stations operate.вҖқ

It should be noted that Disney named Josh DвҖҷAmaro its new CEO just a few weeks ago. With this being his first Trump test, DвҖҷAmaro is likely looking back at the damage caused to the companyвҖҷs brand when it suspended Kimmel in September during the MAGA ragefest over comments the late-night comic made about Charlie KirkвҖҷs assassination.

The backlash over Kimmel being pulled from the air amid government pressure вҖ“ Carr had threatened to take action against the network prior to the вҖңindefiniteвҖқ suspension вҖ“ made the comedian a free speech hero, leading to a ratings surge when ABC reversed course and brought him back on the air.

Of course, what makes DвҖҷAmaroвҖҷs decision-making easier this time around is that not only have other institutions and media companies prevailed in court against the administrationвҖҷs authoritarian push against the press, but Trump is also historically unpopular right now.

And though much of MAGA media and Trump loyalists are parroting the presidentвҖҷs call for ABC to fire Kimmel while cheering on the FCCвҖҷs action, some conservatives have criticized the administrationвҖҷs heavy-handed approach. Even the right-wing provocateurs who are trying to stoke outrage over KimmelвҖҷs joke are finding that some of their viewers arenвҖҷt buying what theyвҖҷre selling.

вҖңIt is not governmentвҖҷs job to censor speech, and I do not believe the FCC should operate as the speech police,вҖқ Senator Ted Cruz (R-TX) fumed, while far-right pundit Glenn Beck tweeted that вҖңthe government should stay completely out of this controversy.вҖқ

Ahead of Reporters Without BordersвҖҷ release of its 2026 World Press Freedom Index tomorrow, the organization unveiled a billboard on the highway between Mar-a-Lago and the West Palm Beach airport, hinting at its conclusions.

вҖңLand of the not-so-free press anymore,вҖқ the sign reads, showing journalists covering a protest getting bombarded with tear gas.

In last yearвҖҷs index, which covered the calendar year 2024, the United States was ranked 57th in press freedom, and its score вҖ“ which takes into account abuses against journalists and the responses of press freedom specialists вҖ“ found the US was in a вҖңproblematicвҖқ state.

With this yearвҖҷs report being the first to account for TrumpвҖҷs war against the press, which has seen him sue multiple news outlets, defund PBS and NPR, limit the Associated PressвҖҷs access to the White House, sideline the Pentagon press corps, and try to dismantle Voice of America, I asked Clayton Weimers вҖ“ Reporters Without Borders North American director вҖ“ what we should expect in tomorrowвҖҷs release.

вҖңI think anyone whoвҖҷs been paying attention to the press for freedom space for the past year is not going to be surprised by the results of the index when it comes to the United States, given Donald TrumpвҖҷs continued war on press freedom,вҖқ he flatly declared.

Watch out for my newsletter, вҖҳRagebait,вҖҷ every week. I focus on the right-wing outrage-industrial complex and the intersection of our political and media landscapes. Send any tips on Signal (justinbaragona.85).

Check out more from Zeteo:

item.title: First Draft: In the Court of Kim Jong Trump
item.description: On this day in 1974, Republican President Richard Nixon gave in to pressure over the Watergate scandal and released 46 transcripts of taped White House conversations. It failed to placate investigators of the 1972 break-in at the offices of the Democratic National Committee, and on Aug. 8, 1974,вҖҰ
item.author: First Draft | Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
On this day in 1974, Republican President Richard Nixon gave in to pressure over the Watergate scandal and released 46 transcripts of taped White House conversations. It failed to placate investigators of the 1972 break-in at the offices of the Democratic National Committee, and on Aug. 8, 1974, Nixon resigned his office. The good olвҖҷ days when presidential crimes had consequencesвҖҰ

Good morning! Andrew here, covering another typical day in Donald TrumpвҖҷs America, one in which we learned the presidentвҖҷs face will be printed on US passports, and the Justice Department disclosed itвҖҷs prosecuting the former FBI director over a trivial tweet, and the Federal Communications Commission is threatening to revoke a major corporate networkвҖҷs broadcast licenses over a comedianвҖҷs joke. What will today have in store?

In todayвҖҷs вҖҳFirst Draft,вҖҷ Trump continues his drive to turn the US into a tinpot dictatorship, Democrats still need to calibrate their message on corruption, Kash Patel beclowns himself yet again, and a killer ICE agent is back at work.

Late last year, our team at Zeteo began discussing in editorial meetings the many ways in which President Donald Trump has been turning the United States into an Americanized North Korea.

It took a few months to find the right moment to send my colleague Prem around Washington, DC, to produce this stellar video on how Trump was turning the US capital into cultish Pyongyang, given the time we needed to devote to the presidentвҖҷs wars, both foreign and domestic. That turned out to be a good thing for our reporting, because Trump kept generating relevant material.

And wow, does he continue to do so.

According to a report Tuesday from the Bulwark, which the administration tried to devalue by giving it to Fox as an вҖңexclusive,вҖқ TrumpвҖҷs face will soon appear on some US passports вҖ“ supposedly to вҖңcommemorateвҖқ AmericaвҖҷs 250th anniversary, and not just our presidentвҖҷs immense love for himself.

ItвҖҷs an absurd development, but somehow not even a surprise: Trump cultists are slapping his name and likeness on everything they can.

ThereвҖҷs the вҖңDonald J. Trump Institute of PeaceвҖқ in Washington, as well as the вҖңDonald J. Trump and John F. Kennedy Memorial Center for the Performing Arts.вҖқ Republicans in Florida passed a bill to rebrand the airport in Palm Beach, home to TrumpвҖҷs Mar-a-Lago club, the вҖңPresident Donald J. Trump International Airport.вҖқ They made a вҖңPresident Donald J. Trump Blvd.вҖқ in Palm Beach, too.

The National Park Service is putting TrumpвҖҷs face on park passes, alongside George Washington. Please be aware: your pass will be voided if you cover TrumpвҖҷs mug with a sticker.

The administration is planning to print TrumpвҖҷs signature on paper currency. The treasury helpfully noted that this would be вҖңthe first time in history вҖҰ a sitting presidentвҖқ had been so featured, while claiming it was in вҖңhonor of the 250th anniversary of the United States of America.вҖқ

Trump also wants to put his face on $1 coins. That would make him the first living president to have his likeness on US currency, which makes sense, given it has been against the law since 1866. The administration is also prepared to put TrumpвҖҷs face on extra-large commemorative gold coins вҖ“ again, supposedly to celebrate AmericaвҖҷs 250th birthday.

Republicans in Congress have bills to rename the DC Metro the вҖңTrump Train,вҖқ to put his face on the

GOP lawmakers have a bill to carve TrumpвҖҷs face onto Mount Rushmore, and another to rename Dulles airport in Virginia. Trump has reportedly requested that Penn Station in New York be named for him, too.

TrumpвҖҷs cult of personality isnвҖҷt only focused on putting his name and image everywhere. It is deeply invested in making him feel good about himself, with no expense spared.

The administration put on a military parade for TrumpвҖҷs birthday last year. Team Trump claimed вҖ“ of course вҖ“ the embarrassing spectacle was meant to honor the US ArmyвҖҷs 250th birthday, but as you would expect, behind closed doors, officials called it вҖңDonald TrumpвҖҷs birthday parade.вҖқ It cost $30 million.

The Department of Homeland Security spent $220 million on ads ostensibly designed to encourage immigrants to flee the US, in which then-Secretary Kristi Noem effusively thanked the president for his mass-deportation policies.

Noem said from the get-go these ads were TrumpвҖҷs idea, relaying that the president told her: вҖңI want you to thank me. I want you to thank me for closing the border.вҖқ Somehow, a year later, she ended up fired after daring to tell Congress that Trump approved the ads. Whoops!

You canвҖҷt put a price on such displays of loyalty: Trump frequently holds televised meetings in which Cabinet officials and other senior staffers take turns offering gratuitous, competitive praise.

TrumpвҖҷs cult is also carrying out a vast effort to prosecute and punish his enemies on the flimsiest of bases вҖ“ and itвҖҷs only ramping up after the president fired Attorney General Pam Bondi for failing to put his enemies in jail.

On Tuesday, his Department of Justice unveiled its second ridiculous effort to prosecute former FBI Director Jim Comey, this time for posting an image of seashells arranged to say вҖң86 47.вҖқ

In restaurant parlance, вҖң86вҖқ means an item is no longer available. Trump is the 47th president. The DOJ claimed the message was вҖңa threat to kill the president,вҖқ even after Comey deleted it, apologized, and said, вҖңI didnвҖҷt realize some folks associate those numbers with violence. It never occurred to me but I oppose violence of any kind so I took the post down.вҖқ

It remains to be seen whether TrumpвҖҷs DOJ will prosecute far-right influencer Jack Posobiec, whoвҖҷs been part of the Trump PentagonвҖҷs press corps, for posting вҖң86 46вҖқ during Joe BidenвҖҷs presidency.

TrumpвҖҷs Federal Communications Commission, meanwhile, is attempting to punish ABC for refusing to accede to the presidentвҖҷs demand that it fire Jimmy Kimmel, based on a willful misreading of a joke last week about TrumpвҖҷs age and health.

On Tuesday, the FCC ordered ABCвҖҷs network stations to file for broadcast license renewals within 30 days.

Because our president hopes to rule like Kim Jong Un, it makes sense that he targets comedians. We arenвҖҷt supposed to laugh at Donald Trump. We are meant to laugh with him and вҖ“ more importantly вҖ“ love him, as he loves himself.

Yeah, good luck with thatвҖҰ

Death tolls:

Iran: Iranian state media on Sunday reported, citing officials, that US-Israeli attacks have killed 3,468 people.

Lebanon: Israel has killed more than 2,534 people, including at least 177 children, in Lebanon, according to the countryвҖҷs Health Ministry.

US troops: At least 13 US troops have died in incidents related to the war.

Israel: At least 35 people, including 12 Israeli soldiers, have been killed, according to officials.

And in more war newsвҖҰ

What next?: US intelligence agencies are studying how Iran would respond if Trump unilaterally declares victory in the war, Reuters reported. Meanwhile, the Wall Street Journal reported that Trump has told his aides to prepare for an extended blockade of Iran.

TrumpвҖҷs latest warning: The US president early this morning warned: вҖңIran вҖҰ better get smart soon!вҖқ His post was accompanied by an apparently AI-generated image of Trump holding a rifle with the text, вҖңNO MORE MR. NICE GUY,вҖқ and explosions in the background.

Power play: The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps now dominates in Tehran, Reuters said, saying this could harden opposition to a negotiated peace.

Executions ramp up: UN human rights chief Volker TГјrk said he was вҖңappalledвҖқ by at least 21 executions Iran has carried out since the war began. According to the UN, nine were related to JanuaryвҖҷs antigovernment protests, 10 were for an alleged membership in opposition groups, and two were on spying charges.

Oil ploy: The United Arab Emirates will leave the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries (OPEC) in May, saying it would introduce more oil to the global market. It will likely not have much effect if the Strait remains closed.

Double tap: Two Israeli strikes on a building in the Lebanese town of Majdal Zoun killed five, including three rescuers responding to the first attack, authorities said. Prime Minister Nawaf Salam condemned вҖңa new and вҖӢblatant war crime.вҖқ

What to watch: Pentagon chief Pete Hegseth is set to testify before a congressional committee later today for the first time since the US and Israel launched their illegal war on Iran.

The 2026 midterms should be good for Democrats, based on Donald TrumpвҖҷs deep and growing unpopularity, thanks to his mishandling of the economy, disastrous war with Iran, violent immigration policies, and in-your-face corruption.

But new research indicates that as Democrats seek to expand the map and compete in tougher territory, they still need to fine-tune their messaging, particularly as it relates to corruption вҖ“ and how to combat RepublicansвҖҷ messaging about fraud.

The progressive donor network Way to Win polled likely voters in Arizona, Georgia, Mississippi, Nevada, North Carolina, and Texas, and found that вҖңTrumpвҖҷs weakness on the economy and votersвҖҷ blaming of Republicans and big corporations for rising costs are a real opportunity.вҖқ But voters donвҖҷt trust Democrats either.

The research suggests that more populist Democratic messaging on the economy and housing would perform better in those states than conventional Dem messaging. Yet, when it comes to corruption вҖ“ one of votersвҖҷ top concerns вҖ“ Democratic messaging underperformed GOP fearmongering about fraud.

вҖңWe have more work to do to make sure we defend against that Republican narrative, especially because government corruption ranked as a top-two issue among these voters,вҖқ Way to Win co-founder Jenifer Fernandez Ancona tells Zeteo.

Keystone Kash: In discussing the case against James Comey, Kash Patel noted that Comey deleted the post and apologized, information that was not in the indictment but which the current FBI director said was вҖңpresented to the grand jury.вҖқ If so, Patel may have made a legal misstep.

License to chill: Trump FCC thug Brendan Carr is reviewing ABCвҖҷs broadcast licenses, Semafor scooped. The move cites diversity policies but comes as Trump calls for late-night host Jimmy Kimmel to be fired for joking about TrumpвҖҷs death.

Killer switch: The ICE agent who killed Renee Nicole Good in Minneapolis in January has moved states and resumed work, PunchUp reported.

On ICE: A federal court rejected ICEвҖҷs mandatory detention policy, deploring вҖңdetention without bond for millions.вҖқ It looks set for the Supreme Court. Meanwhile, the Trump administration is reportedly hoping to speed up deportations of minors.

Voter veto: A federal judge dismissed the administrationвҖҷs effort to seize electoral rolls from Arizona, just the latest defeat for TrumpвҖҷs attempts to control US elections.

New low: The Trump administration is working to slash benefits for adults with disabilities who live with families who receive food stamps, ProPublica reported.

In Virginia high school soccer in the 1990s, which future Trump attack dog earned the nickname вҖҳRed CardвҖҷ for his repeated ejections for вҖҳunrulyвҖҷ play?

Keep your eyes out for the answer below!

рҹҮөрҹҮё DonвҖҷt forget Gaza: Israeli strikes killed at least four and injured others near Gaza City. The attacks bring the total number of Palestinians killed since the so-called вҖңceasefireвҖқ began to at least 823.

рҹҮёрҹҮ© вҖҳGeneration at stakeвҖҷ: UNICEF documented a rise in violence against children in Darfur, where more than 5 million face вҖңextreme deprivationвҖқ amid genocide in Sudan. The UN agency wants global action, noting that outrage has dimmed.

рҹҮірҹҮ¬ Village attack: Around 29 people were killed in an attack on Guyaku village in Nigeria that was reportedly carried out by a regional affiliate of ISIS.

рҹҮІрҹҮ» Newsroom raid: Maldives police raided Adhadhu Online, seizing laptops and storage devices and imposing a travel ban on journalists after it published a documentary saying President Mohamed Muizzu had an affair with a former aide.

рҹҮ©рҹҮӘ Unsuspected suspects: German police identified suspects in an arson attack on the home of BrandenburgвҖҷs antisemitism commissioner, Andreas BГјttner: two people he knew. Many have claimed that pro-Palestine activists were responsible.


Funny how an actual king - the one from the country we revolted and became independent from! - cares more about checks and balances than our own president.

ZeteoвҖҷs Martin Pengelly and Akshay Gokul contributed to this newsletter.

Still want to get Zeteo content, but not this newsletter? You can unsubscribe directly from вҖҳFirst DraftвҖҷ by going to вҖҳ
