вҖңWe have more work to do to make sure we defend against that Republican narrative, especially because government corruption ranked as a top-two issue among these voters,вҖқ Way to Win co-founder Jenifer Fernandez Ancona tells Zeteo.

Keystone Kash: In discussing the case against James Comey, Kash Patel noted that Comey deleted the post and apologized, information that was not in the indictment but which the current FBI director said was вҖңpresented to the grand jury.вҖқ If so, Patel may have made a legal misstep.

License to chill: Trump FCC thug Brendan Carr is reviewing ABCвҖҷs broadcast licenses, Semafor scooped. The move cites diversity policies but comes as Trump calls for late-night host Jimmy Kimmel to be fired for joking about TrumpвҖҷs death.

Killer switch: The ICE agent who killed Renee Nicole Good in Minneapolis in January has moved states and resumed work, PunchUp reported.

On ICE: A federal court rejected ICEвҖҷs mandatory detention policy, deploring вҖңdetention without bond for millions.вҖқ It looks set for the Supreme Court. Meanwhile, the Trump administration is reportedly hoping to speed up deportations of minors.

Voter veto: A federal judge dismissed the administrationвҖҷs effort to seize electoral rolls from Arizona, just the latest defeat for TrumpвҖҷs attempts to control US elections.

New low: The Trump administration is working to slash benefits for adults with disabilities who live with families who receive food stamps, ProPublica reported.

In Virginia high school soccer in the 1990s, which future Trump attack dog earned the nickname вҖҳRed CardвҖҷ for his repeated ejections for вҖҳunrulyвҖҷ play?

Keep your eyes out for the answer below!

рҹҮөрҹҮё DonвҖҷt forget Gaza: Israeli strikes killed at least four and injured others near Gaza City. The attacks bring the total number of Palestinians killed since the so-called вҖңceasefireвҖқ began to at least 823.

рҹҮёрҹҮ© вҖҳGeneration at stakeвҖҷ: UNICEF documented a rise in violence against children in Darfur, where more than 5 million face вҖңextreme deprivationвҖқ amid genocide in Sudan. The UN agency wants global action, noting that outrage has dimmed.

рҹҮірҹҮ¬ Village attack: Around 29 people were killed in an attack on Guyaku village in Nigeria that was reportedly carried out by a regional affiliate of ISIS.

рҹҮІрҹҮ» Newsroom raid: Maldives police raided Adhadhu Online, seizing laptops and storage devices and imposing a travel ban on journalists after it published a documentary saying President Mohamed Muizzu had an affair with a former aide.

рҹҮ©рҹҮӘ Unsuspected suspects: German police identified suspects in an arson attack on the home of BrandenburgвҖҷs antisemitism commissioner, Andreas BГјttner: two people he knew. Many have claimed that pro-Palestine activists were responsible.


Funny how an actual king - the one from the country we revolted and became independent from! - cares more about checks and balances than our own president.

ZeteoвҖҷs Martin Pengelly and Akshay Gokul contributed to this newsletter.

Still want to get Zeteo content, but not this newsletter? You can unsubscribe directly from вҖҳFirst DraftвҖҷ by going to вҖҳ

item.title: How Tony Dokoupil Became the Face of вҖҳZionist FanaticвҖҷ Bari WeissвҖҷs CBS
item.description: For years, Tony Dokoupil was a run-of-the-mill morning talk show host who had yet to make an indelible impression on television audiences. And then came his explosive and combative September 2024 interview with celebrated author Ta-Nehisi Coates , which paved the way for Dokoupil to become the faceвҖҰ
item.author: Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
For years, Tony Dokoupil was a run-of-the-mill morning talk show host who had yet to make an indelible impression on television audiences.

And then came his explosive and combative September 2024 interview with celebrated author Ta-Nehisi Coates, which paved the way for Dokoupil to become the face of CBS NewsвҖҷs flagship nightly news program and the spiritual successor to Walter Cronkite just a year later.

вҖңI have to say, when I read the book, I imagine if I took your name out of it, took away the awards and the acclaim, took the cover off, the publishing house goes away, the content of that section would not be out of place in the backpack of an extremist,вҖқ Dokoupil accusingly declared during the вҖҳCBS MorningsвҖҷ conversation.

It was that moment that everything appeared to change for Dokoupil.

Prior to that seven-minute segment in the fall of 2024, Dokoupil had been almost a non-entity, despite having spent five years as a co-anchor of a broadcast morning talk show. Suggesting that one of AmericaвҖҷs preeminent thought leaders is an antisemitic terrorist, however, seemed to set off a series of events that possibly led to self-avowed вҖңZionist fanaticвҖқ Bari Weiss running the network and Dokoupil leading вҖҳCBS Evening News.вҖҷ

Still, while the Coates interview proved to be a seismic shift, DokoupilвҖҷs current and former colleagues tell me they were caught off guard at the time by the level of vitriol he showed in that moment.

вҖңI was absolutely gobsmacked, and it did not comport with anything I recall about Tony being ideological. I never saw him being ideological in any way. That interview came straight out of the blue,вҖқ Joy Reid, who worked with Dokoupil at MSNBC, told me.

The intense and pointed nature of the questions to Coates, which saw Dokoupil ask the author what вҖңso particularly offends you about the existence of a Jewish stateвҖқ and if he just didnвҖҷt believe Israel вҖңhas a right to exist,вҖқ apparently sparked murmurs within CBS News about DokoupilвҖҷs possible connections to conservative pro-Israel groups and activists.

Three well-placed CBS News sources said there was internal speculation in the newsroom about whether pro-Israel advocacy groups such as the ADL had begun communicating with Dokoupil to build a relationship with the anchor following the October 7 attacks, though no evidence of editorial coordination has been established.

Two sources familiar with the matter told me that Noa Tishby, an Israeli actress and outspoken activist who once served as the Israeli governmentвҖҷs special envoy for combating antisemitism, met with Dokoupil.

The ADL denied that the organization had privately met with Dokoupil in recent years. Representatives for Tishby did not respond to repeated requests for comment.

CBS News did not respond to repeated requests for comment about whether Dokoupil met with Tishby. A CBS News spokesperson had previously told Zeteo when reached for comment on this story that, вҖңThis whole screed reads like it was written in crayon on the back of a paper placemat. ItвҖҷs entirely fabricated lies. Just like all of ZeteoвҖҷs вҖҳcoverageвҖҷ of CBS News.вҖқ

That Dokoupil went from the controversy-sparking Coates interview to CBS NewsвҖҷs top anchor chair in little more than a year is not so much about the standard machinations of network newsrooms. Instead, this is a story, based on conversations with 11 people who have known him through the years, about what they say is a вҖңmediocreвҖқ but вҖңgood-looking white guyвҖқ who has continually вҖңfailed upwards,вҖқ only to eventually hit pay dirt by defending Israel, saluting Donald TrumpвҖҷs Cabinet members, and finding the one benefactor who would take him to the top вҖ“ Bari Weiss. ItвҖҷs also a behind-the-scenes account, based on multiple well-placed sources, of a newsroom divided over DokoupilвҖҷs new status and journalistic approach.

Growing up in Miami, Dokoupil lived a life of luxury вҖ“ including a nice house, private school, yacht, and expensive vacations in the Caribbean. That was because his father вҖ“ known as вҖңBig TonyвҖқ вҖ“ was a drug smuggler, something Dokoupil wrote about at length in his 2014 autobiographical tale The Last Pirate and recounted during a вҖҳCBS Evening NewsвҖҷ telecast from Miami this year.

According to Dokoupil, the lavish lifestyle dried up before he was 10 years old as his fatherвҖҷs drug addiction spiraled out of control and legal troubles loomed, which eventually saw вҖңBig TonyвҖқ skip town. Dokoupil and his mother, a retired schoolteacher, then relocated to Maryland.

вҖңI felt like my world was cracked in two... There was a before, when anything was possible, all doors were open. And there was an after, where you had to scrimp,вҖқ Dokoupil noted in a 2009 feature about reconnecting with his father.

After graduating from high school in Severna Park, Maryland, Dokoupil earned a scholarship to play baseball at George Washington University (GWU), where he would go on to graduate first in his class from GWUвҖҷs business school. Journalism, at that point, was nowhere on his radar.

вҖңAt the time, I didnвҖҷt know what journalism was,вҖқ Dokoupil told the Severna Park Voice newspaper in 2019. вҖңI thought I was going to do something in athletics.вҖқ

In fact, as recently noted by the Columbia Journalism Review (

It was while he was in a PhD program at Columbia University that he decided to take an unpaid internship at Newsweek, which led to his first job as a journalist.

вҖңI stayed through the great collapse of print вҖ“ all the layoffs, the bails, the hand wringing, all the convulsion,вҖқ Dokoupil recalled. вҖңI ended up in TV because I had done a number of stories for Newsweek that became broadcast in one form or another.вҖқ

After spending six years with Newsweek (which briefly merged with The Daily Beast during his tenure), Dokoupil joined NBC News as a senior writer for its digital division in 2013 before the enterprise unit he worked for shut down. He later shifted to reporting for NBCвҖҷs off-camera investigative unit before becoming an MSNBC correspondent, which would eventually lead to some on-air appearances.

He converted to Judaism in 2008 while engaged to his first wife, Danielle Haas. The couple would have two children, and Dokoupil would later write about his experience getting an adult circumcision in order to complete his conversion.

вҖңMedically speaking, I was already circumcised, along with most of the other babies born in America in the Eighties. But thatвҖҷs no good for God. I needed a hatafat dam brit: a drawing of blood,вҖқ he detailed in a New Republic piece in 2014.

Haas eventually filed for divorce in 2015 and moved the coupleвҖҷs children to Israel, which Dokoupil revealed on air shortly after the October 7 attacks in Israel. The same year he split from Haas, Dokoupil began dating MSNBC correspondent Katy Tur. Two years later, the couple would elope, and they now have two children of their own.

During his time at NBC News and MSNBC, Dokoupil was always compared to fellow correspondent Jacob Soboroff, according to three former MSNBC staffers I spoke with.

вҖңHe didnвҖҷt make much of an impression during his time at MSNBC. Was often compared to Jacob Soboroff, who came up around the same time, so he stopped wearing glasses to help differentiate himself,вҖқ an ex-NBC News executive told me. вҖңBarely appeared on air, unlike Soboroff. I always thought he was sort of like a chameleon.вҖқ

Another former MSNBC journalist quipped that network employees used to joke that Dokoupil was the вҖңhimboвҖқ version of Soboroff.

As others pointed out, Dokoupil didnвҖҷt get much airtime throughout his tenure at MSNBC and NBC News, and he struggled to stand out during his years at the network. вҖңI literally remember nothing about his journalism because it was so unremarkable that it really wasnвҖҷt memorable,вҖқ the ex-MSNBC journalist noted.

Still, the ex-MSNBC journalist described Dokoupil as вҖңquite a nice guyвҖқ and a вҖңperfectly lovely personвҖқ during their shared tenure at the network, adding that they even had вҖңa couple of conversations here and there about his very interesting familyвҖқ history.

Dokoupil did make an impression on Tur, who told Esquire that she met her future husband in the makeup room in 2015 prior to an appearance on Al SharptonвҖҷs show.

вҖңIвҖҷm not very polite, so I said, вҖҳWho the fuck is that? Does he work here? How come I donвҖҷt know who he is?вҖҷвҖқ Tur recounted to Esquire in 2019. вҖңThe makeup artists all chimed in and said, вҖҳThatвҖҷs Tony, weвҖҷve all got a big crush on him.вҖҷвҖқ

While TurвҖҷs career exploded during the 2016 presidential campaign, leading to her becoming an on-air anchor following her work covering Donald Trump on the trail, Dokoupil still found himself stuck in neutral at MSNBC.

Then CBS News came calling, and he moved to the Tiffany Network as a correspondent during the heat of the 2016 political season.

Much like at MSNBC, though, Dokoupil found himself struggling to make a name for himself in his first couple of years at CBS News. As several former and current CBS News staffers told me, Dokoupil was largely relegated to weekend segments and вҖңbottom of the barrelвҖқ stories.

вҖңHe didnвҖҷt know how to write for TV. He didnвҖҷt know how to do any of that kind of stuff. He was just, you know, a good-looking white guy,вҖқ a former CBS News producer said.

Multiple sources noted that вҖҳCBS Sunday MorningвҖҷ showrunner Rand Morrison вҖңkind of put Tony on the mapвҖқ by utilizing him for several segments on the long-running weekend staple.

Still, DokoupilвҖҷs on-air hits were few and far between, so it was unexpected to nearly everyone вҖ“ including Dokoupil himself вҖ“ when then-CBS News president Susan Zirinsky tapped him to be the third anchor on вҖҳCBS MorningsвҖҷ in 2019. Particularly since Dokoupil had never even guest-anchored or hosted a news show up until that point.

вҖңHe had almost zero experience, like he started in the big leagues,вҖқ a network staffer stated. вҖңYeah, thatвҖҷs the thing that you have to know about Tony. He did not pay his dues, as we call it in our television news business; youвҖҷre supposed to climb your way through the ranks.вҖқ

The same staffer recounted a conversation they had with Zirinsky around the time she decided to elevate Dokoupil from relative obscurity to morning show host.

вҖңThey plucked him out and gave him an opportunity on a silver platter, and he said, вҖҳI donвҖҷt know what IвҖҷm doing.вҖҷ And she said, вҖҳWe will carry you.вҖҷ Can you believe that privilege? Yeah, it was stunning, and the fact that she shared that story with me was stunning,вҖқ the staffer declared.

вҖңHeвҖҷs literally the William Hurt character from the movie вҖҳBroadcast News.вҖҷ Because you know that the character that Holly Hunter plays is based on Susan Zirinsky,вҖқ the staffer added.

Outside of CBS NewsвҖҷs blanket denial of ZeteoвҖҷs reporting, Zirinsky herself did not respond to a request for comment.

DokoupilвҖҷs sudden promotion to morning anchor prompted other former and current CBS News staffers to grouse that he didnвҖҷt вҖңearn his stripesвҖқ and he had вҖңnever anchored beforeвҖқ as heвҖҷd вҖңbarely done a live shotвҖқ prior to his ascendance.

вҖңBut thatвҖҷs the open secret from people who are not sure how he got here in the first place,вҖқ a former CBS News correspondent said.

With several CBS News sources describing him as вҖңjust another mediocre white guy who failed upwards,вҖқ the former producer noted that Dokoupil seemed to develop something of an вҖңimposter syndromeвҖқ when he arrived at вҖҳCBS MorningsвҖҷ вҖ“ which led to him appropriating an arrogant, aloof demeanor as the years went on.

While DokoupilвҖҷs morning cohorts, Nate Burleson and Gayle King, were always warm and personable to their guests and fellow CBS colleagues, according to several CBS sources, Dokoupil largely came off as вҖңcoldвҖқ on set.

вҖңTo me, TonyвҖҷs problem is that he just kind of thinks heвҖҷs the smartest guy in the room, and thatвҖҷs like the nexus for everything with him,вҖқ one ex-CBS senior staffer pointedly stated. вҖңHeвҖҷs very much like, вҖҳI know because I know. This is what I believe, so it must be correct.вҖҷ There was a lot of that. And heвҖҷs, you know, a bit of a poser, but he wouldnвҖҷt be the first one.вҖқ

Still, other former CBS colleagues noted that Dokoupil had grown into his role on вҖҳCBS MorningsвҖҷ over time, applauding him for his deft interviewing of tough subjects, such as Marjorie Taylor Greene.

вҖңHe did a good job pressing her and not giving her an open platform to say what she wanted to say,вҖқ the ex-correspondent said. вҖңHe did that well. I thought that actually raised his standing a little bit internally.вҖқ

While Dokoupil had largely steered clear of displaying any ideological stances throughout his news career, that all changed after the October 7 attacks in Israel and the start of the genocide in Gaza.

The Monday after the attacks by Hamas, Dokoupil told вҖҳCBS MorningsвҖҷ viewers that his children and ex-wife were in Israel. вҖңI come to this fairly as a journalist, but IвҖҷm also a father,вҖқ Dokoupil said, before adding: вҖңYou canвҖҷt separate those two at a certain point.вҖқ

вҖңWeвҖҷre talking about the direct, close-range murder of more than 700 civilians in their cars, in their homes, at a festival,вҖқ he continued. вҖңAnd then the kidnappings, and then the hostage taking, and then the evidence of rape. And I think thereвҖҷs enough moral clarity in the world to say that this is wrong, itвҖҷs terrorism, and if itвҖҷs being done in your name, speak up, speak up.вҖқ

Several well-placed CBS News sources said that following his emotional on-air remarks about his children, they heard from others that the ADL and other conservative pro-Israel organizations began connecting with Dokoupil.

The former CBS senior staffer said that вҖңI would hear a lot aboutвҖқ pro-Israel organizations meeting with Dokoupil and potentially other CBS journalists, something other sources told me had been talked about within the newsroom. Zeteo has not been able to independently verify any such meetings.

вҖңI was aware that this was just kind of happening,вҖқ the ex-senior staffer stated. вҖңI just think that there was a lot of pressure, not just from the outside groups but also from very like-minded people within the network. So there were instances where people had no business speaking with correspondents in the field, like during the war in Israel or Lebanon or wherever, reaching out to them directly.вҖқ

The Coates interview, meanwhile, occurred roughly a year after the start of the war in Gaza. And it appeared to change the trajectory of not only DokoupilвҖҷs career, but that of CBS News.

After CBS News management reprimanded Dokoupil for violating the networkвҖҷs editorial standards during the contentious interview, Weiss and her right-wing pro-Israel site, The Free Press, made the morning host a cause cГ©lГЁbre.

As Weiss obtained leaked recordings of editorial meetings, she devoted copious column space to defending Dokoupil while blasting then-CBS News chief Wendy McMahon for rebuking the anchorвҖҷs interview of Coates. At the same time, Dokoupil received support from Shari Redstone, the then-chair of CBS parent company Paramount вҖ“ and a passionate backer of pro-Israeli causes.

While some of the people I spoke to acknowledged that DokoupilвҖҷs questioning of Coates was вҖңmore aggressiveвҖқ in tone than they would have liked, they still defended it.

вҖңTa-Nehisi is not coming on just to have a softball interview,вҖқ one former senior producer said, adding that the segment вҖңmade people thinkвҖқ and вҖңprobably brought more attention to the bookвҖқ in the end.

Meanwhile, ADL president Jonathan Greenblatt quickly spoke with McMahon and made it clear that Dokoupil had done nothing wrong in challenging CoatesвҖҷs вҖңone sided (sic)вҖқ views on the Palestine-Israel conflict. Greenblatt also groused that it was вҖңinsensitiveвҖқ of McMahon to reprimand Dokoupil on October 7, since it was the first anniversary of the Hamas attack.

According to three network sources, McMahon was required to meet with the ADL multiple times, in addition to her initial call with Greenblatt, following the Coates interview. Hours after Greenblatt raged about the вҖңbiased and one-sidedвҖқ nature of a January 2025 вҖҳ60 MinutesвҖҷ segment on the Israel-Gaza war, Zirinsky returned to CBS News to serve as interim executive editor overseeing standards.

вҖңJonathan Greenblatt had two meetings with CBS, and those were in 2024, when he was on a tour that took him to all the major networks for a series of editorial board meetings in response to the campus protests and a dramatic rise in antisemitic incidents post Oct. 7,вҖқ the ADL told Zeteo. The ADL added that вҖңTony Dokoupil was not present at either meeting at CBS,вҖқ which is not something Zeteo asked the organization to confirm.

McMahon did not respond to multiple requests for comment.

In the aftermath of the Coates interview, speculation began to swirl internally about DokoupilвҖҷs surprisingly combative approach.

вҖңWe always wondered who gave Tony those questions for the Ta-Nehisi Coates interview,вҖқ one well-placed CBS News source told me. вҖңAnd every time we asked, he was like, вҖҳNo, I came up with those.вҖҷ I know a bunch of people were like, вҖҳNo, youвҖҷre not that smart to come up with those questions.вҖҷвҖқ

Other network sources I spoke to noted that Dokoupil doing his own research ahead of the interview by seeking out additional context or information from prominent activists or subject matter experts, particularly those who could provide differing opinions and criticism of CoatesвҖҷs book, should not be seen as a вҖңcrazy idea.вҖқ

While Redstone was clearly a staunch defender of Dokoupil, who was now open with his pro-Israel stance, she was also in the process of selling her company to an even bigger supporter of Israel вҖ“ Skydance MediaвҖҷs David Ellison and his megawealthy father Larry Ellison, a Trump ally and the biggest private donor to IsraelвҖҷs military.

As both Redstone and the Ellisons allegedly sought a secret вҖҳside dealвҖҷ with Trump to make sure the merger went through, which saw the settlement of TrumpвҖҷs вҖңmeritlessвҖқ вҖҳ60 MinutesвҖҷ lawsuit and pledge to appoint a pro-Trump ombudsman, plans were already being put in place to install Weiss atop the network and integrate her anti-woke blog with CBS News.

The biggest beneficiary of WeissвҖҷs ascension at CBS News would be Dokoupil.

Two months after she was hired, Weiss made Dokoupil the face of her reimagined version of вҖҳCBS Evening News,вҖҷ which saw Dokoupil unveil вҖңfive simple principlesвҖқ that would guide the show moving forward вҖ“ such as вҖңWe Love America!вҖқ

Weiss praised Dokoupil at the time, saying in a statement that he was the вҖңperson to winвҖқ back viewersвҖҷ вҖңtrust in the mediaвҖқ because вҖңhe believes in old-school journalistic values: asking the hard questions, following the facts wherever they lead and holding power to account.вҖқ

DokoupilвҖҷs promotion, however, came after Weiss tried desperately to land a big outside hire to jump ship to CBS News, only to repeatedly fail to poach big names such as CNNвҖҷs Anderson Cooper and FoxвҖҷs Bret Baier, who both rejected her entreaties and are signed to multi-year deals with their respective networks.

With Dokoupil now a highly polarizing figure both within the newsroom and externally, the criticism of his promotion was fierce and immediate. It didnвҖҷt help matters when he kicked off his run as the nightly news anchor by claiming heвҖҷd be вҖңmore accountable and more transparentвҖқ than Cronkite.

вҖңThe reason heвҖҷs so bad at this is that heвҖҷs not actually climbed the ranks in any meaningful way,вҖқ a senior CBS News staffer told Zeteo. вҖңHeвҖҷs not somebody who climbed through the ranks of the newsroom in the way that the rest of the people have, and that is why he will say disparaging or idiotic things about someone like Walter Cronkite.вҖқ

Meanwhile, the first weeks of DokoupilвҖҷs run were nothing short of disastrous. Besides taking heat for the programвҖҷs вҖңMAGA-codedвҖқ coverage, which featured softball interviews with Pete Hegseth and Trump, a вҖңboth-sides messвҖқ regarding January 6, an ICE ride-along with now-deposed Department of Homeland Security head Kristi Noem, and a вҖңsaluteвҖқ to Marco Rubio, Dokoupil also found himself struggling to navigate technical glitches and gaffes.

Indeed, on his first official day as вҖҳCBS Evening NewsвҖҷ anchor, Dokoupil stumbled on air, confused as to whether he was supposed to discuss a story about Minnesota Governor Tim Walz or Arizona Senator Mark Kelly. вҖңFirst day, big problems here,вҖқ an embarrassed Dokoupil conceded.

It was later revealed that the reason for the snafu was that Weiss and her aides were still rewriting DokoupilвҖҷs script just minutes before the show went to air, which led to a teleprompter mishap when the rewritten material was added twice.

In fact, according to CBS News insiders and staffers I spoke with, Weiss has been extremely hands-on with the evening news broadcast since installing Dokoupil as its anchor.

вҖңBari was always on the set and just standing off camera вҖ“ feeding him lines and saying how she wanted him to act and speak,вҖқ one knowledgeable source pointed out. вҖңSo itвҖҷs just kind of like, if youвҖҷre willing to do that вҖ“ IвҖҷm not sure anchors in the past would have been okay with the president of the network leading them around by the nose.вҖқ

Weiss also introduced Dokoupil as the face of the networkвҖҷs flagship nightly telecast with a вҖңroad showвҖқ that saw him bounce around from city to city via private plane. The behind-the-scenes chaos over the ever-changing logistics stemming from WeissвҖҷs impetuousness resulted in the show spending twice as much as the planned $1.5 million budget for the two-week вҖңLive From AmericaвҖқ tour, according to three network sources.

Recently, the program has also seen the loss of over a dozen staffers who took voluntary buyouts offered by Weiss and CBS News president Tom Cibrowski amid plummeting staff morale. Additionally, the network axed the programвҖҷs number two producer, Javier Guzman, in DokoupilвҖҷs first week for supposedly вҖңunderminingвҖқ executive producer Kim Harvey.

With the show already in a constant state of flux and disarray three months into his tenure as anchor, some CBS News sources acknowledged that Dokoupil isnвҖҷt being placed in the best position to succeed.

вҖңItвҖҷs the people that donвҖҷt know what the fuck they are doing that are putting Tony in bad situations,вҖқ the ex-CBS senior staffer said. вҖңAgain, the job of the executive producer, the job of the president of the network, is first and foremost to protect the anchor and protect the brand.вҖқ

Dokoupil was given the anchorвҖҷs chair at вҖҳCBS Evening NewsвҖҷ in part because the two-man experiment the network tried last year вҖ“ which saw the program hosted by John Dickerson and Maurice DuBois вҖ“ was drawing weak television ratings.

However, with Weiss running the ship and Dokoupil front and center, viewers are continuing to abandon the show. According to Nielsen, вҖҳCBS Evening NewsвҖҷ averaged just 4.3 million total viewers in the first quarter of this year, down 7% year-over year. In the key advertising demographic of adults aged 25-54, the drop is even worse вҖ“ 18%, with a mere 541,000 viewers.

Meanwhile, the networkвҖҷs broadcast competitors actually saw their ratings shoot up. ABCвҖҷs вҖҳWorld News Tonight with David MuirвҖҷ is up 8% with 8.7 million nightly viewers, while вҖҳNBC Nightly News with Tom LlamasвҖҷ averaged 7 million viewers this past quarter, a year-over-year increase of 6%.

In recent weeks, the ratings have only continued to sink. Between April 6 and April 17, вҖҳCBS Evening NewsвҖҷ wasnвҖҷt able to crack 4 million total viewers during any of the 10 weeknight broadcasts вҖ“ and on three of those nights, the programвҖҷs viewership was below 3.6 million.

The rightward shift of both the network and its storied nightly news broadcast is clearly not attracting new audiences while repelling previously loyal viewers. As StatusвҖҷs Oliver Darcy noted last month, вҖңit is all but impossible for outlets like CBS News to win over any meaningful swath of the Fox News audience.вҖқ

It remains to be seen whether Weiss continues to stick with Dokoupil in the long run while keeping the show on its current Trump-friendly path, ratings be damned. Of course, with the Ellisons continuing to pursue their media mogul ambitions by buying up CNNвҖҷs parent company, Warner Bros. Discovery, the chances seem likely that the editorial stance of the show вҖ“ at the very least вҖ“ wonвҖҷt shift.

As for whether any of this constitutes good journalism or news reporting on DokoupilвҖҷs or WeissвҖҷs part, the senior CBS News staffer didnвҖҷt mince words.

вҖңI donвҖҷt think he understands really how to do the job of journalism, because, in a way, heвҖҷs never actually had to do it. HeвҖҷs just been handed these huge jobs that are way, way too big for him, and heвҖҷs not smart enough or savvy enough or cares enough about the craft of journalism enough to be good at it,вҖқ they stated. вҖңThe problem is, it dovetails with Bari Weiss, who doesnвҖҷt love journalism, doesnвҖҷt care about it, isnвҖҷt even curious about it, and only wants to hear her really weird, regressive, narrow, bigoted, whatever you want to call it, views amplified back to her.вҖқ

Justin Baragona is the author of ZeteoвҖҷs weekly вҖҳRagebaitвҖҷ newsletter, which focuses on the right-wing outrage-industrial complex and the intersection of the political and media landscapes. Send Justin any tips on Signal to justinbaragona.85.

Check out more from Zeteo:

item.title: 5 Laws and Standards That Require the US Cut Off Weapons to Israel
item.description: A memo is circulating in Congress detailing five laws and international standards that obligate the United States to stop sending or selling weapons to Israel. Sent by the Institute for Middle East Understanding Policy Project, the memo has been distributed to offices across Capitol Hill. A sourceвҖҰ
item.author: Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
A memo is circulating in Congress detailing five laws and international standards that obligate the United States to stop sending or selling weapons to Israel.

Sent by the Institute for Middle East Understanding Policy Project, the memo has been distributed to offices across Capitol Hill. A source familiar with the effort says the memo was sent to every Democratic office in Congress.

The memo comes after 40 senators voted to block some arms sales to Israel in mid-April вҖ“ a record number. Still, the entire Republican Party and a handful of Democrats prevented Congress from fulfilling its legal obligations and blocking some weapons transfers to Israel in response to its genocide in Gaza, and its ramped-up violence in the West Bank, Lebanon, and Iran.

One of the votes вҖ“ which ultimately failed to pass вҖ“ was simply to block the sale of bulldozers to Israel. The Israeli government has used these bulldozers to demolish homes in the West Bank, as it seeks to illegally expand there and steal even more land. Israeli soldiers also used a bulldozer to kill 23-year-old American peace activist Rachel Corrie in 2003, as she stood against such demolitions.

The high mark of support to block arms transfers to Israel underscores how drastically public support for Israel has cratered, particularly among rank-and-file Democrats, but also among independents and even some Republicans. With that collapse, public pressure is at an all-time high for Congress to follow the law and stop using US tax dollars to aid IsraelвҖҷs violence.

Accordingly, an upsurge of politicians and organizations is shifting on the issue of arming Israel. Everyone from Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, to likely 2028 candidate Rahm Emanuel, to pro-Israel and pro-peace organization J Street has come out in opposition to US aid to Israel. However, the new rising consensus still leaves open the opportunity for Israel to buy US weapons with its own money. While cutting aid to Israel may be a radical shift from the status quo, it still falls short of fully blocking all weapon transfers to Israel.

Here, as per the memo, are five laws and standards obligating Congress to do so:

The Foreign Assistance Act (FAA) holds that вҖңno security assistance may be provided to any country the government of which engages in a consistent pattern of gross violations of internationally recognized human rights.вҖқ The FAA defines gross violations of human rights to include вҖңtorture or cruel, inhuman, or degrading treatment or punishment, prolonged detention without charges and trial, causing the disappearance of persons by the abduction and clandestine detention of those persons, and other flagrant denial of the right to life, liberty, or the security of person.вҖқ

The IMEU memo cites the Israeli human rights organization BвҖҷTselemвҖҷs damning report, вҖңWelcome to Hell: The Israeli Prison System as a Network of Torture Camps,вҖқ which details how the Israeli government has created вҖңa network of camps dedicated to the abuse of inmates as a matter of policy,вҖқ facilities вҖңin which every inmate is deliberately subjected to harsh, relentless pain and suffering,вҖқ and which operate вҖңas de-facto torture camps.вҖқ

BвҖҷTselem has found that at least 84 Palestinians have died in these Israeli detention centers, often due to вҖңsystematic abuse, including physical and psychological violence, inhuman conditions, deliberate starvation and denial of medical treatment.вҖқ Thousands of Palestinians are remanded to such facilities, often with no semblance of due process through a system called вҖңadministrative detention,вҖқ in which Israeli authorities detain Palestinians for months on end without charge or trial.

IsraelвҖҷs regime of torturous prisons moreover flouts the International Court of Justice. In January 2024, the court issued provisional measures mandating Israel to take all measures possible to prevent the following acts, in regards to Palestinians: вҖңkilling members of the group; (b) causing serious bodily or mental harm to members of the group; (c) deliberately inflicting on the group conditions of life calculated to bring about its physical destruction in whole or in part; and (d) imposing measures intended to prevent births within the group.вҖқ

Alongside its genocide of Palestinians more broadly, IsraelвҖҷs documented torture of Palestinian prisoners (again, often held with no charge or trial, making such detainees more appropriately dubbed as hostages), would hold it in violation of these measures.

The Arms Export Control Act holds that the US can sell or lease weapons to countries for internal security, self-defense, to prevent the proliferation of weapons of mass destruction, support in participating in United Nations arrangements, or for support in public works or other development pursuits.

IsraelвҖҷs usage of US weapons, the memo states, to maintain its illegal military occupation of Palestine, and to commit potential war crimes and crimes against humanity in Gaza, the West Bank, Lebanon, and Iran вҖңclearly constitute unacceptable usage of US weapons under the AECA.вҖқ

In the aftermath of World War II and the Holocaust, the US joined the rest of the world in voting for the Convention on the Prevention and Punishment of the Crime of Genocide, and ratified it in 1988, alongside passing a domestic law that designated genocide as a federal crime.

In 2007, the ICJ ruled that nations part of the Genocide Convention must вҖңemploy all means reasonably available to them, so as to prevent genocide so far as possible.вҖқ While a nation would not be responsible just by virtue of вҖңthe desired resultsвҖқ not being achieved, it would be responsible if it вҖңmanifestly failed to take all measures to prevent genocide which were within its power, and which might have contributed to preventing the genocide.вҖқ

Given that Israel has explicitly conducted its genocide with US weapons, the nation is currently failing to take measures to prevent it, and is thus responsible for it.

Article 1 of the Geneva Conventions of 1949 states, вҖңThe high contracting parties undertake to respect and to ensure respect for the present convention in all circumstances.вҖқ

In 2016, the International Committee of the Red Cross (ICRC), which serves as the watchdog over the Geneva Conventions, stated that nations have both a вҖңnegative and positive obligationвҖқ to вҖңensure respectвҖқ for the convention.

Under the negative obligation, the ICRC said, nations вҖңmay neither encourage, nor aid or assist in violations of the conventions by Parties to a conflict.вҖқ

Under the positive obligation, nations вҖңmust do everything reasonably in their power to prevent and bring such violations to an end.вҖқ

When it comes to weapons transfers specifically, the ICRC states that, in order for nations to honor Article 1, they are prohibited from transferring weapons to a party where there is a clear risk that it would contribute to violations of international humanitarian law.

Given the parameters from the ICRC, and IsraelвҖҷs documented violations of humanitarian law, the US would be obligated to stop sending weapons to Israel.

The International Law Commission of the United Nations adopted these articles in 2001, as a backbone for how international law can customarily deal with legal accountability for wrongful acts.

Article 16 of the papers establishes that вҖңa state which aids or assists another state in the commission of an internationally wrongful act by the latter is internationally responsible for doing so,вҖқ if вҖңthat state does so with knowledge of the circumstances of the internationally wrongful act,вҖқ or if вҖңthe act would be internationally wrongful if committed by that state.вҖқ

Article 41 also obligates states to вҖңcooperate to bring to an end through lawful means any serious breachвҖқ of international law, and for states to not aid or assist other states in breaking international law.

The ICJ cited these articles in July 2024, when it issued an opinion deeming IsraelвҖҷs occupation of Palestinian territory to be illegal. The court said Israel was obligated to end its illegal presence вҖңas rapidly as possible.вҖқ

With regards to the culpability of other states, the ICJ held that they are вҖңunder an obligation not to render aid or assistance in maintaining the situation created by IsraelвҖҷs illegal presence in the Occupied Palestinian Territory.вҖқ

Given Israel uses US weapons to maintain its illegal occupation of Palestine, the US would be obligated to stop sending weapons to Israel.

These five laws and standards do not even include the Leahy Law, which bans US aid to specific military units that commit gross human rights violations. The US, including under former President Joe Biden, has blatantly refused to enforce this law in the past. It also does not include the National Security Memorandum-20 report, which is supposed to pause weapons transfers if the US finds recipient nations are not providing credible assurances they are complying with international law and allowing humanitarian aid access. ThatвҖҷs another mechanism that the US, under Biden, outright flouted with regards to IsraelвҖҷs conduct in Gaza.

There is no shortage of laws and international standards that mandate the US stop sending weapons to Israel. And there is no shortage of public outcry that the US continues to do so anyway. With popularity for the US-Israeli War on Iran in the gutter, and US positive sentiment toward Israel continuing to plummet, the question is rapidly becoming what will end first: the USвҖҷs current relationship to Israel, or the careers of politicians who refuse to follow the law.

Have any tips or information for us? You can text Prem on Signal at premthakker.35.

item.title: Watch Mehdi's Powerful Speech in Defense of Independent Journalism at the Zeteo Second Birthday Party
item.description: Dear friends, We are now officially two years old. Happy birthday to us! At our second birthday party at the Au ZaвҖҷatar restaurant in New York, surrounded by an audience of politicians, activists, celebrities and, of course, lots of journalists, I laid out my vision for independent journalism andвҖҰ
item.author: Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
Dear friends,

We are now officially two years old. Happy birthday to us!

At our second birthday party at the Au ZaвҖҷatar restaurant in New York, surrounded by an audience of politicians, activists, celebrities and, of course, lots of journalists, I laid out my vision for independent journalism and the key goals of Zeteo.

Despite having given hundreds, perhaps thousands, of speeches over my 47 years on this planet, IвҖҷll be very honest with you: Zohran Mamdani is a hard act to follow. I was a little nervous speaking right after him.

Nevertheless, do give my remarks above a watch. I explained how ZeteoвҖҷs mission is to tell the truth вҖңno matter how awkward, or uncomfortable, or controversial,вҖқ and not вҖңboth-sidesingвҖқ or вҖңeuphemizingвҖқ the truth, whether it is about fascism at home or genocide abroad.

Independent journalism requires support. Please consider becoming a paid subscriber - even a founding member - as we celebrate this second anniversary of Zeteo. And if youвҖҷre already a paid subscriber, consider donating to Zeteo to further support our work and our growth.

I have to say: Zeteo could not have reached this milestone, two years!, had it not been for the support and encouragement of all of you: our subscribers, our friends, our family. Thank you!

Onwards and upwards!

Best wishes,

Mehdi

P.S. Keep an eye on your inbox in the coming days for exclusive pictures from our second birthday party. You can see which big-name politicians, celebs, and journalists attended and celebrated with us!

Other Zeteo stories you may have missed:

Thank you for being a paid subscriber to Zeteo, your support helps keep this media movement alive. If youвҖҷd like to wear your support, donвҖҷt forget to check out

For any issues with your subscription, please email us at info@zeteonews.com.

item.title: Fascists Want Comedian Fired
item.description: On this day in 1967, Muhammad Ali refused to be inducted into the US Army and was stripped of his heavyweight boxing title. вҖңThe real enemies of my people are right here, not in Vietnam,вҖқ he said. Good morning! Andrew here, to talk about Donald Trump вҖҷs latest effort to get Jimmy Kimmel fired. TheвҖҰ
item.author: First Draft | Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
On this day in 1967, Muhammad Ali refused to be inducted into the US Army and was stripped of his heavyweight boxing title. вҖңThe real enemies of my people are right here, not in Vietnam,вҖқ he said.

Good morning! Andrew here, to talk about Donald TrumpвҖҷs latest effort to get Jimmy Kimmel fired. The comedianвҖҷs response is well worth reading. More on that below.

In todayвҖҷs вҖҳFirst Draft,вҖҷ as Stephen Miller once said, only fascists are enraged by the idea of free speech. Plus, Republicans are using super PAC trickery to try to pick the Democrats who run against them, and Ron DeSantis wants to delete four Democratic congressional seats.

In September, President Donald Trump and MAGA managed to get Jimmy Kimmel taken off the air by twisting his words. Now, the president вҖ“ who once pledged he would вҖңbring back free speech in AmericaвҖқ вҖ“ is again demanding Kimmel be fired, this time for telling a mean joke.

Thanks to public outcry, KimmelвҖҷs previous suspension was only temporary вҖ“ and ever since Disney and ABC brought him back, heвҖҷs been unleashing on Trump with abandon. Last ThursdayвҖҷs episode was no different, when Kimmel donned a tuxedo and performed a mock version of a roast that a comedian might offer at the White House CorrespondentsвҖҷ Dinner, вҖңif our president wasnвҖҷt a trembling drama queen whoвҖҷs scared of comedy.вҖқ

Kimmel, as is the case these days, did not hold back. After joking about the presidentвҖҷs age and visibly declining health, and the possibility he вҖңhas a medical emergency tonight,вҖқ Kimmel turned to the first lady. вҖңLook at Melania Trump, so beautiful,вҖқ he said, as video of the first lady sitting in a crowd played on the screen. вҖңMrs. Trump, you have a glow like an expectant widow.вҖқ

The comments attracted little controversy at first, untilвҖҰ wellвҖҰ youвҖҷve probably heard by now: An apparent gunman tried to storm the White House CorrespondentsвҖҷ Dinner on Saturday, with multiple shots fired after he raced through the security perimeter inside the Washington Hilton вҖ“ leading the Secret Service to usher Trump, Melania, and top officials out of the gala, attendees to duck down amid the chaos, and the festivities to end early.

The unfortunate turn of events gave Trump and his team an opportunity to again call for KimmelвҖҷs firing.

Melania Trump stepped up first, saying: вҖңA coward, Kimmel hides behind ABC because he knows the network will keep running cover to protect him. Enough is enough. It is time for ABC to take a stand.вҖқ

In a statement she almost certainly didnвҖҷt write herself, the first lady claimed that вҖңKimmelвҖҷs hateful and violent rhetoric is intended to divide our country,вҖқ and that вҖңhis words are corrosive and deepens the political sickness within AmericaвҖқ вҖ“ as if her husband isnвҖҷt the countryвҖҷs #1, unquestioned, biggest hatemonger. Trump regularly calls his political opponents вҖңterrorists,вҖқ вҖңgarbage,вҖқ and the вҖңenemy from within.вҖқ HeвҖҷs accused Democratic lawmakers of вҖңSEDITIOUS BEHAVIOR, punishable by DEATH.вҖқ (Just watch this video we put together!)

Trump вҖ“ the man who just recently threatened to end a вҖңwhole civilizationвҖқ in Iran, a nation of over 90 million people вҖ“ wrote a post accusing the late-night comedian of issuing a вҖңdespicable call to violence,вҖқ adding: вҖңJimmy Kimmel should be immediately fired by Disney and ABC.вҖқ

Never mind the presidentвҖҷs ode to вҖңfree speech in our ConstitutionвҖқ during his press briefing Saturday evening, after evacuating the White House CorrespondentsвҖҷ Dinner.

No one on Earth believed he meant any of it, not after all of the effort heвҖҷs expended trying to jail journalists, sue and shake down media organizations, censor late-night hosts, and deport foreign students who oppose IsraelвҖҷs genocide in Gaza.

That hasnвҖҷt stopped right-wing journalists or some liberal pundits from playing along.

вҖңTrump can be a rude provocateur. Kimmel is a sadist masquerading as a comedian,вҖқ tweeted longtime Fox reporter Geraldo Rivera.

David Axelrod, the former chief strategist for Barack Obama, posted that Kimmel is вҖңcourageous at a time when there has been too much cowardiceвҖқ вҖ“ but suggested he apologize anyway for his вҖңtasteless joke.вҖқ Fox eagerly wrote up his advice.

Kimmel didnвҖҷt have kind words to say in his mock roast about Stephen Miller, TrumpвҖҷs chief policy architect: вҖңStephen Miller is so racist вҖ“ the reason he went bald is because his hair was black,вҖқ he joked.

We donвҖҷt have many nice words to say, either. WeвҖҷll say this, though, about Miller, who tweeted in 2022, вҖңIf the idea of free speech enrages you вҖ“ the cornerstone of democratic self-government вҖ“ than I regret to inform you that you are a fascist.вҖқ

Then, and only then, he had a point.

But, of course, weвҖҷre all used to Republican hypocrisy and shamelessness at this point, right?

Kimmel addressed the calls for his firing on his show late Monday. He did not apologize. Instead, he offered a smart point of his own.

вҖңI understand that the first lady had a stressful experience over the weekend,вҖқ Kimmel said. вҖңMost of all, I agree that painful and violent rhetoric is something we should rejectвҖҰ I think a great place to start to dial that back would be to have a conversation with your husband about it.вҖқ

Death tolls:

Iran: Iranian state media on Sunday reported, citing officials, that US-Israeli attacks have killed 3,468 people.

Lebanon: Israel has killed more than 2,521 people, including at least 177 children, in Lebanon, according to the countryвҖҷs Health Ministry.

US troops: At least 13 US troops have died in incidents related to the war.

Israel: At least 35 people, including 12 Israeli soldiers, have been killed, according to officials.

And in more war newsвҖҰ

Negotiation rejections: As Israel continues to bomb southern Lebanon, despite a ceasefire, Hezbollah maintains its disapproval of LebanonвҖҷs peace negotiations with Israel and says it will continue to confront Israeli aggression.

Economic fury: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent warned that companies that provide services to IranвҖҷs airlines may be subject to US sanctions.

Ally in Putin: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi met with Russian President Vladimir Putin, who reportedly told him that he would do вҖңeverythingвҖқ to help achieve peace in the country.

With two months left in MaineвҖҷs Senate primary, a super PAC backing Republican Senator Susan Collins is spending $2 million on attack ads against Graham Platner, the veteran and oysterman whoвҖҷs leading Janet Mills in the polls.

The group, Pine Tree Results PAC, has received at least $4.3 million in dark money, and millions more from billionaire donors. Steve Schwarzman, whose private equity firm Blackstone Group is one of the worldвҖҷs largest landlords, gave the group $2 million. Schwarzman made the donation a day before Collins voted to advance TrumpвҖҷs tax bill, which was chock-full of tax breaks for the ultra-wealthy.

Pine Tree Results accepted $1 million from a dark money group affiliated with Leonard Leo, the right-wing operative who built the Supreme CourtвҖҷs far-right supermajority, which eliminated the federal right to an abortion. Pine Tree Results also received $500,000 apiece from Jude and Christopher Reyes, brothers who co-chair the beverage distributor Reyes Holdings, which benefited from a special exemption from TrumpвҖҷs ruinous tariffs.

Pine Tree Results is chaired by Scott Reed, corporate records show. Reed, a former top strategist for the US Chamber of Commerce, chaired a different pro-Collins super PAC in 2020.

In Ohio, a super PAC funded by GOP Rep. Max MillerвҖҷs father is trying to pick his Democratic opponent, ahead of next weekвҖҷs primary.

The group, Jobs & Prosperity PAC, has received $50,000 from MillerвҖҷs dad and another $50,000 from his dadвҖҷs investment firm. The super PAC recently sent out texts calling Dem candidate Ed Fitzgerald вҖңtoo liberal,вҖқ in an apparent effort to boost his campaign against Brian Poindexter, whoвҖҷs backed by Bernie Sanders.

Billionaire tax on the ballot: California will vote this year on a one-time 5% tax on billionaires to fund public services, after the proposal garnered 1.5 million signatures to qualify for the ballot. Tech moguls have already spent tens of millions to oppose the tax and have threatened to leave California. Dem Governor Gavin Newsom, a possible 2028 contender, also opposes the tax.

Longest partial shutdown: Speaker Mike Johnson is teeing up a vote Wednesday on a revised Homeland Security funding bill, seeking to remove language that excludes funding for ICE and Border Patrol. If passed, it would further delay an end to the 10-week-long DHS shutdown, as the new measure would need to be passed by the Senate.

RonвҖҷs redistricting: Florida Governor Ron DeSantis leaked his proposed gerrymandered congressional map, which is designed to remove four Democratic seats, before the state legislature convenes today. The stateвҖҷs constitution explicitly prohibits political gerrymandering.

Ballroom finds new funding: Republicans are going to bat for TrumpвҖҷs lavish ballroom in the wake of the attack on the White House CorrespondentsвҖҷ Dinner. GOP Senator Lindsey Graham told reporters the party plans to introduce a bill to grant $400 million for the construction of the ballroom, which Trump has promised would not be funded by taxpayers.

Floridians passed the вҖңFair DistrictsвҖқ amendment in 2010 to stop exactly what DeSantis is doing right now with his mid-decade redistricting. What percent of voters approved the amendment?

Keep your eyes out for the answer below!

ZeteoвҖҷs John Harwood talks with election-law ace Marc Elias TODAY at 4pm ET (1pm PT/9pm GMT) about how Trump and his party want to manipulate the 2026 outcome and whether defenders of democracy can stop them.

item.author: First Draft | Zeteo
item.content:encoded:
On this day in 1967, Muhammad Ali refused to be inducted into the US Army and was stripped of his heavyweight boxing title. вҖңThe real enemies of my people are right here, not in Vietnam,вҖқ he said.

Good morning! Andrew here, to talk about Donald TrumpвҖҷs latest effort to get Jimmy Kimmel fired. The comedianвҖҷs response is well worth reading. More on that below.

In todayвҖҷs вҖҳFirst Draft,вҖҷ as Stephen Miller once said, only fascists are enraged by the idea of free speech. Plus, Republicans are using super PAC trickery to try to pick the Democrats who run against them, and Ron DeSantis wants to delete four Democratic congressional seats.

In September, President Donald Trump and MAGA managed to get Jimmy Kimmel taken off the air by twisting his words. Now, the president вҖ“ who once pledged he would вҖңbring back free speech in AmericaвҖқ вҖ“ is again demanding Kimmel be fired, this time for telling a mean joke.

Thanks to public outcry, KimmelвҖҷs previous suspension was only temporary вҖ“ and ever since Disney and ABC brought him back, heвҖҷs been unleashing on Trump with abandon. Last ThursdayвҖҷs episode was no different, when Kimmel donned a tuxedo and performed a mock version of a roast that a comedian might offer at the White House CorrespondentsвҖҷ Dinner, вҖңif our president wasnвҖҷt a trembling drama queen whoвҖҷs scared of comedy.вҖқ

Kimmel, as is the case these days, did not hold back. After joking about the presidentвҖҷs age and visibly declining health, and the possibility he вҖңhas a medical emergency tonight,вҖқ Kimmel turned to the first lady. вҖңLook at Melania Trump, so beautiful,вҖқ he said, as video of the first lady sitting in a crowd played on the screen. вҖңMrs. Trump, you have a glow like an expectant widow.вҖқ

The comments attracted little controversy at first, untilвҖҰ wellвҖҰ youвҖҷve probably heard by now: An apparent gunman tried to storm the White House CorrespondentsвҖҷ Dinner on Saturday, with multiple shots fired after he raced through the security perimeter inside the Washington Hilton вҖ“ leading the Secret Service to usher Trump, Melania, and top officials out of the gala, attendees to duck down amid the chaos, and the festivities to end early.

The unfortunate turn of events gave Trump and his team an opportunity to again call for KimmelвҖҷs firing.

Melania Trump stepped up first, saying: вҖңA coward, Kimmel hides behind ABC because he knows the network will keep running cover to protect him. Enough is enough. It is time for ABC to take a stand.вҖқ

In a statement she almost certainly didnвҖҷt write herself, the first lady claimed that вҖңKimmelвҖҷs hateful and violent rhetoric is intended to divide our country,вҖқ and that вҖңhis words are corrosive and deepens the political sickness within AmericaвҖқ вҖ“ as if her husband isnвҖҷt the countryвҖҷs #1, unquestioned, biggest hatemonger. Trump regularly calls his political opponents вҖңterrorists,вҖқ вҖңgarbage,вҖқ and the вҖңenemy from within.вҖқ HeвҖҷs accused Democratic lawmakers of вҖңSEDITIOUS BEHAVIOR, punishable by DEATH.вҖқ (Just watch this video we put together!)

Trump вҖ“ the man who just recently threatened to end a вҖңwhole civilizationвҖқ in Iran, a nation of over 90 million people вҖ“ wrote a post accusing the late-night comedian of issuing a вҖңdespicable call to violence,вҖқ adding: вҖңJimmy Kimmel should be immediately fired by Disney and ABC.вҖқ

Never mind the presidentвҖҷs ode to вҖңfree speech in our ConstitutionвҖқ during his press briefing Saturday evening, after evacuating the White House CorrespondentsвҖҷ Dinner.

No one on Earth believed he meant any of it, not after all of the effort heвҖҷs expended trying to jail journalists, sue and shake down media organizations, censor late-night hosts, and deport foreign students who oppose IsraelвҖҷs genocide in Gaza.

That hasnвҖҷt stopped right-wing journalists or some liberal pundits from playing along.

вҖңTrump can be a rude provocateur. Kimmel is a sadist masquerading as a comedian,вҖқ tweeted longtime Fox reporter Geraldo Rivera.

David Axelrod, the former chief strategist for Barack Obama, posted that Kimmel is вҖңcourageous at a time when there has been too much cowardiceвҖқ вҖ“ but suggested he apologize anyway for his вҖңtasteless joke.вҖқ Fox eagerly wrote up his advice.

Kimmel didnвҖҷt have kind words to say in his mock roast about Stephen Miller, TrumpвҖҷs chief policy architect: вҖңStephen Miller is so racist вҖ“ the reason he went bald is because his hair was black,вҖқ he joked.

We donвҖҷt have many nice words to say, either. WeвҖҷll say this, though, about Miller, who tweeted in 2022, вҖңIf the idea of free speech enrages you вҖ“ the cornerstone of democratic self-government вҖ“ than I regret to inform you that you are a fascist.вҖқ

Then, and only then, he had a point.

But, of course, weвҖҷre all used to Republican hypocrisy and shamelessness at this point, right?

Kimmel addressed the calls for his firing on his show late Monday. He did not apologize. Instead, he offered a smart point of his own.

вҖңI understand that the first lady had a stressful experience over the weekend,вҖқ Kimmel said. вҖңMost of all, I agree that painful and violent rhetoric is something we should rejectвҖҰ I think a great place to start to dial that back would be to have a conversation with your husband about it.вҖқ

Pine Tree Results is chaired by Scott Reed, corporate records show. Reed, a former top strategist for the US Chamber of Commerce, chaired a different pro-Collins super PAC in 2020.

In Ohio, a super PAC funded by GOP Rep. Max MillerвҖҷs father is trying to pick his Democratic opponent, ahead of next weekвҖҷs primary.

The super PAC recently sent out texts calling Dem candidate Ed Fitzgerald вҖңtoo liberal,вҖқ in an apparent effort to boost his campaign against Brian Poindexter, whoвҖҷs backed by Bernie Sanders.

Billionaire tax on the ballot: California will vote this year on a one-time 5% tax on billionaires to fund public services, after the proposal garnered 1.5 million signatures to qualify for the ballot. Tech moguls have already spent tens of millions to oppose the tax and have threatened to leave California. Dem Governor Gavin Newsom, a possible 2028 contender, also opposes the tax.

If passed, it would further delay an end to the 10-week-long DHS shutdown, as the new measure would need to be passed by the Senate.

RonвҖҷs redistricting: Florida Governor Ron DeSantis leaked his proposed gerrymandered congressional map, which is designed to remove four Democratic seats, before the state legislature convenes today. The stateвҖҷs constitution explicitly prohibits political gerrymandering.

GOP Senator Lindsey Graham told reporters the party plans to introduce a bill to grant $400 million for the construction of the ballroom, which Trump has promised would not be funded by taxpayers.

Floridians passed the вҖңFair DistrictsвҖқ amendment in 2010 to stop exactly what DeSantis is doing right now with his mid-decade redistricting. What percent of voters approved the amendment?
